title: fix(cli): restore Claude hook memory injection hookEventName
description: Hotfix: packages/cli/src/commands/claude-hook-inject.ts continueResult() omits hookSpecificOutput.hookEventName, causing Claude Code UserPromptSubmit hook JSON validation to fail. Python implementation at codemem/commands/claude_hook_runtime_cmds.py:831-837 already emits both hookEventName and additionalContext; test at tests/test_claude_hook_runtime_cmds.py:92 confirms shape. Port the field to TS, reading from payload.hook_event_name with fallback 'UserPromptSubmit'. Add TS test mirroring the Python assertion.
close_reason: Shipped via #680. continueResult() now hardcodes hookEventName=UserPromptSubmit (literal type), with invariance test that asserts the field stays correct even when payload carries a divergent hook_event_name.

title: Fix TS raw-event session bridge parity
description: Restore Python-parity behavior for raw-event ingestion by reusing or creating a local session per stable OpenCode stream and upserting the opencode_sessions bridge row. Prevent repeated raw-event flushes from creating unmapped micro-sessions and detached observer summaries.
notes: Shipped critical fix in PR 607. TypeScript raw-event ingest now reuses or creates a local session per stable OpenCode stream and upserts the opencode_sessions bridge row, matching Python parity and preventing detached unmapped micro-session summaries on repeated flushes.
close_reason: Completed via merged raw-event bridge parity and automatic reconciliation stack (#621 and follow-on merged retrieval cleanup PRs).

title: Fix hasUnsyncedSharedMemoryChanges full table scan
description: hasUnsyncedSharedMemoryChanges in sync-replication.ts loads ALL memory items into JS then queries per-row for replication ops. O(N×M) on every 409 reset response. Replace with a single SQL query using LEFT JOIN or NOT EXISTS, and LIMIT 1 since we only need to know if count > 0.
close_reason: Already shipped via #582 fix(sync): replace dirty-state full table scan and close bootstrap TOCTOU window. Commit message explicitly mentions the dirty-state full table scan replacement this issue describes. Stale in_progress entry cleanup.

title: Add TOCTOU re-check to auto-bootstrap path
description: The auto-bootstrap path in sync-pass.ts has no dirty-state re-check after fetchAllSnapshotPages. Between the localSharedCount === 0 check and applyBootstrapSnapshot, shared memories can appear during the network fetch. Add the same re-check that the re-bootstrap path has.
close_reason: Already shipped via #582 fix(sync): replace dirty-state full table scan and close bootstrap TOCTOU window. Same commit as codemem-7cix; commit message explicitly mentions closing the bootstrap TOCTOU window this issue describes. Stale in_progress entry cleanup.

title: Fix raw-event format drift
description: Observer calls can return plain text instead of XML during raw-event flush, causing zero storable output and missing memories. Add a structured retry/fail-closed path with regression coverage.
close_reason: Fixed in hotfix stack merged for 0.21.2: restored structured observer output on the Codex consumer path, fail-closed raw-event flush behavior, and reduced retry spam for tiny lifecycle-only sessions.

title: Bootstrap SQLite planner stats for TS FTS performance
description: TypeScript codemem pack/search can take ~45s on long FTS queries because Node SQLite chooses a catastrophically bad query plan when planner stats are missing. We verified that running ANALYZE on a DB copy flips the plan and drops the query to ~0.1s. Implement one-time ANALYZE bootstrap when sqlite_stat1 is missing/empty, run PRAGMA optimize opportunistically, and add regression coverage around stats bootstrap in the TS DB path.
close_reason: Completed

title: Prepare 0.20.6 hotfix release
description: Cut a 0.20.6 hotfix release for the custom observer auth-header merge fix, including version bumps, regenerated artifacts, release PR, and tag after merge.
close_reason: Completed

title: Fix raw-event micro-batch drain with zero memories
description: Dogfooding 0.20 TS parity reveals a likely data-loss-class ingest bug. Recent raw-event flush batches complete successfully and advance `last_flushed_event_seq`, creating new `sessions` rows, but produce no new `memory_items`. The fresh sessions are tiny micro-batches (often 1-3 events each, sometimes one prompt or one tool event only). In raw-event flush mode, `ingest()` only fails the batch when the observer returns no raw output at all; if the observer returns raw text that parses into no meaningful observations/summary, the batch still completes. This lets the queue burn down while user-visible memory extraction effectively produces nothing. Need to verify whether observer calls are occurring on these tiny batches, then fix batching and/or completion criteria so raw-event flushes do not silently advance on fragmented low-signal sessions that yield zero memories.
close_reason: Completed by merged PR #371 (fix(raw-events): block cursor advance on zero-memory micro-batches). Flush now fails/no-ops safely instead of silently advancing on zero-storable observer output.

title: fix: recordRawEvent missing transaction wrapper
description: store.ts recordRawEvent() performs duplicate check, session ensure, seq allocation, and insert as 4 separate operations with no transaction. recordRawEventsBatch correctly wraps in transaction. Cross-process writes via WAL mode can cause seq gaps.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #287 — wrapped recordRawEvent in db.transaction()

title: fix: Math.max spread stack overflow on large event batches
description: raw-event-flush.ts:86 uses Math.max(...tsValues) which spreads into function arguments. JavaScript has a call stack limit. Large raw event sessions with thousands of timestamps will throw RangeError. Python max() handles iterables natively; JS does not.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #287 — replaced Math.max/min spread with manual loops

title: fix: stateful regex PRIVATE_OPEN_RE causes intermittent private content leak
description: ingest-sanitize.ts uses module-level RegExp with /gi flag in .exec() call. JavaScript persists lastIndex on g-flag regexes between calls, so stripPrivate() intermittently fails to detect orphaned opening tags after its first successful match. Security-adjacent: private content may leak to observer LLM.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #287 — removed g flag from PRIVATE_OPEN_RE

title: Define DB coexistence and migration-ownership contract
description: During migration, Python and TS backends coexist and may both touch the same SQLite database. Define explicit rules for: 1) Which runtime owns schema migration at each phase (only one should run DDL). 2) WAL mode and journal handling when both runtimes access the DB. 3) Extension loading conflicts (sqlite-vec loaded by both Python and TS processes). 4) Whether both runtimes may write concurrently or if one is read-only during transition. 5) How to handle schema version tracking across the two migration systems. Reference: codemem/db.py:94 for current migration and WAL setup.
close_reason: Design doc written, reviewed, and committed. Identified 3 Phase 2 prerequisites: FTS trigger race (codemem-zl8q), executescript atomicity (codemem-i623), WAL fallback (codemem-hx3m). Additional contract rules added from review: no shared cache, BEGIN IMMEDIATE for writes, vec0 sync is app-level, startup DML storm awareness. PR #236.

title: Build Python/TS parity corpus and golden-output harness
description: Before rewriting the store (codemem-egb), capture golden fixtures and expected outputs for the critical paths: search results, timeline ordering, pack assembly, MCP tool responses, raw-event flush behavior, and sync edge cases (conflict resolution, dedup, replication round-trips). These become the acceptance tests that the TS port must pass identically. Format: JSON fixtures + expected outputs, runnable against both Python and TS store implementations. This is the parity contract that prevents the rewrite from silently changing behavior.
close_reason: Parity corpus created with 13 golden fixtures covering search, timeline, recent, get, explain, remember, forget, stats, update_visibility, recent_by_kinds. Deterministic seed database with 3 sessions, 12 memories, 2 actors, mixed visibility. All 13 fixtures pass against Python store. PR #235.

title: Spike: validate Node/TS stack for backend port
description: Spike complete. Results: better-sqlite3 ✅ (compiled from source on Node 24 arm64, no prebuild yet), sqlite-vec ✅ (extension loaded, KNN queries work, quirk: vec0 rejects rowid binding via .run() — use auto-rowid or inline SQL), FTS5 ✅ (virtual tables, MATCH, highlight all working), embeddings ✅ (@huggingface/transformers + onnxruntime-node loaded bge-small-en-v1.5, correct 384-dim output, cosine sim computed — onnxruntime has cosmetic mutex crash at process exit), mDNS ✅ (bonjour-service publish works on macOS via mDNSResponder; browse requires dns-sd subprocess on macOS since mDNSResponder owns port 5353 exclusively — pure JS multicast gets nothing; dns-sd -B and -L subprocess approach validated: browse, resolve, TXT records all working; Linux uses bonjour-service directly since no system daemon conflict; no native addons needed). GO/NO-GO: VIABLE — all four components validated on Node.
close_reason: Spike complete. Results: better-sqlite3 ✅ (compiled from source on Node 24 arm64), sqlite-vec ✅ (extension loaded, KNN queries work, rowid binding quirk noted), FTS5 ✅ (virtual tables, MATCH, highlight all working), embeddings ✅ (@huggingface/transformers + onnxruntime-node loaded bge-small-en-v1.5, correct 384-dim output, cosine sim computed — onnxruntime mutex crash at process exit is cosmetic), mDNS 🟡 (bonjour-service publish works on macOS — verified in Discovery.app — but browse fails because macOS mDNSResponder owns port 5353 exclusively; pure JS multicast listeners get nothing; real port needs native dns-sd binding like node-mdns or coordinator-only discovery). GO/NO-GO: VIABLE.

title: Establish plugin reliability baseline metrics gate
description: Define and enforce baseline reliability metrics gate for plugin event processing and session integrity.
notes: Success criteria: (1) Baseline metrics are documented with exact formulas and thresholds (event ingestion success rate, dropped-event rate, session boundary accuracy). (2) CI or pre-merge check fails when thresholds are violated. (3) A reproducible command/report shows current baseline values from a clean run.

title: Fix scanSecretsRetroactive stack overflow + false-positive 'changed'
description: Two bugs found while running 'codemem db scan-secrets --dry-run' against a real 32k-row store: (1) Maximum call stack size exceeded — at the end of the sweep, mergeDetections(...detectionLists) spreads >10k per-row detection lists into a function call which trips V8's argument-spread stack limit. Fails reliably between rows ~10256 and 10257 in id order. (2) False-positive 'changed' — scanMetadataJson compares JSON.stringify(parsed) to the stored json string. Stored JSON has different whitespace / key order than V8's canonical stringify, so every row with metadata reports changed=true even when no secret was detected. With --dry-run --json --limit=100 the result reports 'updated: 100, detections: []'.
acceptance_criteria: Sweep over the full local store completes without stack overflow on 30k+ rows. Rows with metadata where no secret is detected report changed=false and are not counted as updated. Both bugs covered by regression tests in maintenance.test.ts. Existing tests still pass.

title: Make workspace bootstrap demoable with two-phase attach
description: Prepare the public-safe workspace bootstrap contract for an AirChat Remote demo without embedding AirChat-specific commands. Add explicit join-only and finish-bootstrap phases so a private orchestrator can run worker identity emission, seed-side trust, and final readiness as separate steps.
acceptance_criteria:
Workspace bootstrap script supports join-only and finish-bootstrap phases
join-only emits identity/status/summary without failing on seed trust
finish-bootstrap can run after seed-side trust and emits ready summary
Docs describe the generic payload/artifact flow for private orchestrators
Local proof and targeted tests pass
close_reason: Added explicit join-only and finish-bootstrap phases to the workspace bootstrap script, updated the local proof to exercise the two-phase attach flow, and documented the generic contract. Validated with two-phase proof, sync command tests, lint, and tsc.

title: Fix workspace bootstrap proof invite coordinator isolation
description: The local workspace bootstrap proof can read the user's ambient codemem config during create-invite, causing coordinatorCreateInviteAction to choose an unrelated remote coordinator and fail with invalid_admin_secret. Make the proof hermetic by targeting the local proof coordinator explicitly, then validate the workspace proof reaches seed/worker readiness.
acceptance_criteria:
scripts/workspace/prove-workspace-codemem-bootstrap-local.sh creates invites against the proof coordinator, not ambient user config
Local proof run emits proof-summary.json
Proof summary verifies coordinator join, bootstrap, sync once, and marker memory
close_reason: Fixed workspace proof coordinator isolation, JSON-safe coordinator commands, workspace keys handling for sync enable, and proof summary emission. Validated with sync command test, tsc, lint, and local workspace proof.

title: Normalize Claude Code raw events before buildSessionContext to fix session classification
description:
## Problem

packages/core/src/raw-event-flush.ts calls buildSessionContext(events) on unnormalized Claude Code raw events. Claude Code events arrive with type='claude.hook' and an adapter payload, but buildSessionContext expects normalized events with types 'user_prompt' and 'tool.execute.after'. The OpenCode ingest-pipeline path already normalizes via normalizeAdapterEvents() + normalizeEventsForToolExtraction() (see ingest-pipeline.ts:274-280); the raw-event-flush path does not.

## Impact

For every Claude Code session, the following SessionContext fields fall through to defaults:
- promptCount = 0
- toolCount = 0
- filesRead = []
- firstPrompt = null

(durationMs IS populated because it's computed from raw event timestamps and does not require event-type inspection.)

Because session-policy.ts classifySessionForInjection() reads these fields, Claude Code sessions almost always fall through to the 'working' class on line 70. trivial_turn and micro_low_value branches never fire, so shouldSuppressSummaryOnlyOutput() (which only suppresses those two classes) is a no-op for the entire Claude Code ingestion path. Track 3 summary suppression policy is silently disabled for half of all ingested sessions.

## Evidence

SQL query against ~/.codemem/mem.sqlite for recent Claude Code session 166599 (a 20+ hour session) shows stored session_context: promptCount=0, toolCount=0, durationMs=72886, filesModified=0, filesRead=0, firstPrompt=null. Same pattern on current session 166639.

## Fix sketch

In packages/core/src/raw-event-flush.ts around line 287, normalize events before calling buildSessionContext, matching the pattern at ingest-pipeline.ts:274-280.

## Acceptance criteria

- New Claude Code sessions show populated promptCount, toolCount, filesRead, firstPrompt in stored SessionContext (filesModified is a separate bug tracked elsewhere).
- Test: ingest synthetic Claude Code hook payload with UserPromptSubmit + PostToolUse events, assert SessionContext fields are non-default.
- Manual verification: trivial Claude Code session (single prompt, no tools) is classified as trivial_turn and its summary is suppressed.
notes: PR #715 opened for review — https://github.com/kunickiaj/codemem/pull/715. Normalized Claude Code adapter-enveloped raw events (claude.hook + _adapter) before buildSessionContext so promptCount, toolCount, firstPrompt, and filesRead populate correctly; also accepts file_path alongside filePath/path. Added regression test via flushRawEvents + capturing observer. pnpm run tsc/lint/test all clean (1236 tests passing). Note: extraction-replay.ts:462 has the same bug pattern — out of scope for this PR but should be tracked separately.

comments.author: Adam Kunicki
comments.text: Code review rework: created shared apply-patch.ts, moved normalizeEventsForSessionContext to ingest-transcript.ts, fixed test theater

title: Persist incremental sync vector catch-up across restart
description: Follow-on from docs/plans/2026-04-11-sync-vector-handling-design.md after codemem-3oh2, codemem-1x5w, and codemem-5bkh. Incremental inbound sync currently triggers best-effort post-commit vector maintenance but does not persist that work across restart. Implement a restart-safe path so incremental semantic-index catch-up survives process restarts and temporary embedding/runtime failures.
close_reason: Completed via merged PR #711 persisting incremental sync vector catch-up across restart.

title: Persist pending sync vector work across restart
description: Follow-on from docs/plans/2026-04-11-sync-vector-handling-design.md. Add a durable pending-work mechanism or resumable maintenance state so large bootstrap/indexing jobs survive restart and semantic-index lag can be resumed automatically.
close_reason: Completed via merged PR #709 persisting bootstrap vector catch-up across restart.

title: Queue receiver-side vector work from inbound sync apply
description: First slice of docs/plans/2026-04-11-sync-vector-handling-design.md. Extend the inbound replication apply path so memory_item upserts/deletes collect vector work (new memory insert, content-changing update, delete/tombstone cleanup) and run or enqueue post-commit vector maintenance. Embedding failure must remain non-fatal to sync apply. Add focused tests around applyReplicationOps behavior and vector maintenance triggers.
close_reason: Completed via merged PR #708 implementing receiver-side vector work after inbound sync apply.

title: Auto-generate vectors for synced memories on receive
description: Implement the sync vector handling design from docs/plans/2026-04-11-sync-vector-handling-design.md. The product requirement is that users must not need to run manual backfill after sync or bootstrap for semantic search to work. Canonical sync data remains memory rows; receivers must automatically build local vectors for inbound memories. Reliability is top priority: sync correctness must not depend on embedding success.
close_reason: PR #714 merged — orphan helper removed and sync vector epic complete

title: Define session continuity and conceptual merge policy
description: Define how codemem should detect conceptually continuous work spread across multiple local session rows or flush batches without immediately collapsing storage boundaries. Scope: continuity signals, when adjacent sessions should be treated as one workstream for retrieval/injection, whether to persist a continuity_group/workstream key versus derive it on read, and how this should interact with session_class, recap suppression, and rich-session under-extraction. Output should be execution-ready policy plus the smallest implementation seam.
close_reason: Reframed after investigation. Original framing (workstreams fragmented across session rows needing merge) was not supported by database evidence: sessions 166599 (Claude Code, 20+ hours) and 166600 (OpenCode, 18 hours) are each already single session rows via the adapter's stable session_id. OpenCode sessions 166635/6/7 that looked like fragmentation are actually three legitimately distinct OpenCode sessions for distinct tasks (review/implement/review). Real issues decomposed into: codemem-e96w (Claude Code raw event normalization bug, P1), codemem-h3v8 (filesModified empty for both adapters, P2), codemem-garf (per-session session_summary deduplication, P2). Cross-session topic-linking (the case that originally looked like Claude Code cross-conversation continuity) is semantic knowledge-graph work that belongs in codemem-am33.

title: Fix directEnqueue in claude-hook-ingest to bootstrap fresh DBs
description:
Gordon Ramsay review on the auto-bootstrap PR found that directEnqueue in packages/cli/src/commands/claude-hook-ingest.ts (around lines 120-198) still does raw connect(dbPath) + db.prepare("SELECT 1 FROM raw_events ...") without bootstrapping or a tableExists guard. On a fresh DB, the SELECT throws "no such table: raw_events".

## Current behavior on fresh VM (e.g. Cowork sandbox)

1. claude-hook-ingest tries HTTP observer path → fails (viewer daemon down)
2. Tries directEnqueue → throws on the raw_events prepare → caught by tryDirectFallback → logs + spools
3. Returns success to Claude Code
4. Claude sees ingest "worked" but nothing lands in the DB

The MemoryStore constructor path (flushBoundaryRawEvents) now auto-bootstraps via #TBD (the just-landed fix), so that half works. But the fast-path directEnqueue is still broken on fresh DBs.

## Fix options

1. Have directEnqueue construct a MemoryStore for the check instead of raw connect() — cleanest, but may be overkill for a hot path (extra sqlite-vec load, schema compat checks).
2. Gate the raw prepare with a tableExists("raw_events") check and, if missing, call bootstrapSchema before retrying.
3. Hoist directEnqueue onto a shared "open-and-bootstrap-if-needed" helper that both directEnqueue and MemoryStore use.

Option 3 is probably the right factoring: extract `connectAndBootstrap(dbPath)` in db.ts or store.ts that does connect → auto-bootstrap → return db handle, and have both the MemoryStore constructor and directEnqueue call it.

## Acceptance criteria

- Fresh sqlite file + claude-hook-ingest with HTTP observer disabled → direct write succeeds, raw event lands in the DB.
- No regression on the HTTP-first / viewer-up path.
- Existing claude-hook-ingest tests still pass.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #699 via ensureSchemaBootstrapped in directEnqueue

title: Prep 0.25.3 release after #685 and #686 land

Follows the same process as 0.25.2 (codemem-mr2j): bump all 12 version sites, pnpm install + pnpm build to regen lockfile/viewer static asset, open release branch + PR off main, tag v0.25.3 on the merge commit post-merge.
close_reason: Completed via merged 0.25.3 release prep on main (tag v0.25.3 at 8277decd / PR #687).

title: Prep 0.25.2 release after Claude hook stack lands
description:
Cut release/0.25.2 once #680 → #681 → #682 → #683 are all merged to main. The four PRs ship the Claude hook fixes (hookEventName hotfix, session-state tracking, ingest durability layer, contract fixtures) and 0.25.2 is the version that exposes them.

## Pre-flight (before creating the release branch)
- Verify HEAD on main is the merge commit for #683 (last in the stack)
- git checkout main && git pull --rebase
- git status --short --branch (must be clean)

## Version bumps (per CLAUDE.md release checklist, bump from 0.25.1 → 0.25.2)
- packages/core/package.json
- packages/cli/package.json
- packages/opencode-plugin/package.json
- packages/mcp-server/package.json
- packages/viewer-server/package.json
- packages/core/src/index.ts (VERSION constant)
- packages/core/src/index.test.ts (matching assertion)
- packages/cli/.opencode/plugins/codemem.js
- packages/opencode-plugin/.opencode/plugins/codemem.js
- .claude-plugin/marketplace.json (metadata version + codemem plugin entry version; MCP args stay unpinned)
- plugins/claude/.claude-plugin/plugin.json (metadata version only; MCP args stay unpinned)

## Regen lockfiles/artifacts
- pnpm install (commit lockfile changes if any)
- pnpm build (commit viewer UI bundle/static asset diffs that the build emits, e.g. packages/viewer-server/static/app.js runtime hash)
- Confirm .opencode/.npmrc still points at the public npm registry

## PR + tag flow
- New branch release/0.25.2 off updated main (NOT stacked on feature work)
- Open release PR with the standard release template body
- Wait for CI to pass, then squash-merge
- After merge: git checkout main && git pull --rebase, verify worktree clean, verify HEAD is the merged release commit, git show --stat --summary HEAD to confirm version bumps are present
- Tag the merge commit on main: git tag v0.25.2 && git push origin v0.25.2 (NEVER tag the release branch; the Release workflow triggers on v* tags)
- Do NOT immediately bump main to 0.25.3 — keep next-version bumps in a later release-prep cycle

## Notes
- Ships the Claude hook parity work from #680/#681/#682/#683
- Existing 0.25.1 was prepared via release/0.25.1 → #679 (commit 840f9e80) — this PR follows the same pattern
close_reason: Shipped via #684 (release/0.25.2). All 12 version sites bumped 0.25.1 → 0.25.2, viewer static asset hash refreshed, 1190 tests pass. Tag v0.25.2 to follow on the merge commit.

title: feat(cli): port Claude hook ingest durability to TS
description: Port the operational durability layer from codemem/commands/claude_hook_runtime_cmds.py onto the TS claude-hook-ingest path so captures aren't lost when the viewer is down or hooks race: (1) file lock ~/.codemem/claude-hook-ingest.lock with PID liveness, TTL, grace recovery, (2) disk spool ~/.codemem/claude-hook-spool/ as persistent queue, (3) stale tmp-spool recovery and spool drain on every invocation, (4) boundary flush on Stop/SessionEnd (CODEMEM_CLAUDE_HOOK_FLUSH/CODEMEM_CLAUDE_HOOK_FLUSH_ON_STOP envs), (5) ~/.codemem/plugin.log failure logging for both inject and ingest commands, (6) tests for lock semantics, spool roundtrip, drain ordering.
close_reason: Shipped via #682. New claude-hook-plugin-log.ts and claude-hook-ingest-spool.ts modules: file lock with PID liveness + TTL/grace, atomic spool, drain with quarantine for corrupt entries, boundary flush via flushRawEvents + ObserverClient, HTTP response strict validation (skipped>0 fail-over), backlog drain on HTTP-success path (Codex P1 fix), CODEMEM_PLUGIN_IGNORE defense in depth.

title: feat(cli): port Claude hook session-state tracking to TS
description: Port from codemem/commands/claude_hook_runtime_cmds.py the session-state machinery missing on the TS hook path: (1) ~/.codemem/claude-hook-context/<sha1>.json state store persisting first_prompt/last_prompt/files_modified, (2) _track_hook_session_state applied to claude-hook-inject and claude-hook-ingest (updates on UserPromptSubmit + PostToolUse(Failure), cleared on SessionEnd), (3) _build_inject_query producing a richer retrieval query from first_prompt + current prompt + project + recent file basenames (cap 500 chars), (4) working_set_paths filter passed into buildMemoryPackAsync using last 8 modified files, (5) unit tests covering state persistence, query building, and working-set propagation.
close_reason: Shipped via #681. New claude-hook-session-state.ts module with on-disk state store, trackHookSessionState lifecycle (UserPromptSubmit/PostToolUse(Failure)/SessionEnd), buildInjectQuery rich query composer, workingSetPathsFromState. Inject + ingest both wired up. Includes [pack truncated] marker, CODEMEM_PLUGIN_IGNORE short-circuit, multi-line prompt normalization, and apply_patch falsy fallback fix.

title: Prepare 0.24.0 release PR
description: Validate main after recent merges and, if ready, prepare the 0.24.0 release branch/PR with required version bumps, lockfile/artifact refresh, and release metadata updates using Graphite.
close_reason: Prepared 0.24.0 release PR with version bumps, release metadata updates, and a core build-script fix so pnpm build passes reliably for release validation.

title: Capture rich-session under-extraction eval case
description: Record the current rich-session raw-event under-extraction case as a Track 3 evaluation artifact. Scope: session stream ses_29b51abf5ffefPNFnFBKCHdiF2 / local session 166405, completed flush batch 1204-1356, pending tail 1357+, observed memory outputs 13596 and 13597, and the expected stronger set of durable observations and richer summary. This case should be used to evaluate whether codemem reduces rediscovery/scouting effort during automatic injection on long, high-signal sessions. Include the diagnosis that inputs were rich and the likely weakness is memory-generation prompt / extraction policy rather than garbage raw events.

title: Prepare 0.23.0 release PR
description: Create the 0.23.0 release branch and PR, update shared versioned package/plugin metadata, regenerate artifacts, and validate the release candidate on current main. This release is driven by the merged sessionization/retrieval/summary fix stack through PR #626.
close_reason: Already shipped via #627 chore: prepare 0.23.0 release (commit 8d282969). Stale release-prep entry cleanup.

title: Add injection-first eval scenarios for Track 3
description: Define and later implement eval scenarios that test whether automatic injection reduces rediscovery effort. Include prompts around locating relevant code, understanding past decisions, reusing prior outcomes, and recurring troubleshooting. Use these scenarios to validate Track 3 policy decisions rather than relying only on explicit recap prompts or manual vibes.
notes: Added executable injection-first scenario registry in packages/core/src/eval-scenarios.ts and wired named scenario packs into memory role-report / role-compare CLI options via --scenario. Added tests for the scenario registry and CLI command surface. Remaining work is to add richer scenario metadata/scoring and use captured live cases like codemem-31ha as formal comparison targets.

title: Define memory quality criteria around rediscovery reduction
description: Translate the Track 3 quality model into concrete criteria for durable, recap, ephemeral, and general memory. Evaluate each category by how much it reduces rediscovery effort: finding relevant code/workstreams, understanding past decisions, reusing outcomes, and avoiding repeated troubleshooting or context scouting. Keep this as a policy/model task, not a schema rewrite yet.
notes: Memory quality model now has companion implementation seams: session-policy.ts for write-time session class gating, recap-policy.ts for shared recap mode logic, and eval-scenarios.ts for executable scenario packs. Remaining work for gwez is to encode quality dimensions more directly into retrieval/injection scoring or metadata rather than only docs and heuristics.

title: Define recap policy for automatic injection vs explicit recap
description: Specify recap emission and weighting rules for Track 3. Separate automatic transform-hook injection from explicit recap/summary requests. Define which recap artifacts are allowed, demoted, or suppressed in each path; how unmapped recap should behave; and what regression metrics should guard against recap takeover in top injected context.
notes: Added packages/core/src/recap-policy.ts as the first shared code seam for recap intent and recap-like detection. Search and pack retrieval paths now use the shared helper instead of carrying separate recap-intent/recap-like logic. Targeted pack/search tests and core build pass.

title: Define injection-first session boundary matrix
description: Turn Track 3 sessionization policy into a concrete matrix for micro, working, and durable sessions. For each class, define whether codemem should store no summary, delayed recap, or durable session_summary; what signals count as meaningful work; and how automatic injection should treat the resulting artifacts. This should explicitly optimize for reducing rediscovery, scouting, and repeated work rather than maximizing summary coverage.
notes: Implemented another Track 3 seam: ingest now persists session_class and summary_disposition into session post metadata, and session_summary memories also carry session_class. Targeted ingest/session-policy tests and core build pass. This makes session policy inspectable instead of write-only behavior.

title: Raise Node minimum to 24 and enforce tsc in CI
description: Update package metadata and docs to reflect Node 24+ support, add a canonical tsc script alias, and modify GitHub CI to run the workspace TypeScript project-reference check as a required step/job.
close_reason: Added a canonical root tsc script with typecheck alias, updated CI to run pnpm run tsc on Node 24, raised published package/root Node engines metadata to >=24, and updated user/contributor docs to match. Local pnpm run tsc now surfaces the existing TypeScript failures that CI previously missed.

title: Investigate CI not catching failing typecheck
description: Inspect GitHub workflows and merge gate behavior to determine why local pnpm run typecheck fails while CI passes, focusing on whether typecheck is missing, scoped, path-filtered, or non-blocking.
close_reason: Root cause identified: CI workflow does not run the repository typecheck script. CI runs build/test/lint on Node 24 and a limited packaged plugin smoke matrix on Node 22/24, so project-reference typecheck failures can slip through if they are not covered by package build scripts.

title: Evaluate pack structure for model-friendly context
description: Assess whether codemem memory packs are structured efficiently for downstream AI model consumption rather than merely formatted for human readability. Investigate section ordering, verbosity, recap vs durable balance, redundancy, token efficiency, and whether packs should include more explicit synthesized structure instead of mostly raw memory lines. Use live DB probes and evals to compare alternatives.
close_reason: Completed via merged memory role snapshot comparison and live-database probe/eval harness (#622).

title: Design recap emission and weighting policy
description: Define how recap/session-summary memory should behave after relinking and compaction repairs. Cover when summaries should be emitted, how recap should be weighted relative to durable memory, how micro-session recap should be suppressed or demoted, and what internal validation metrics should guard against recap dominance regressing again.
notes: Starting first recap-policy implementation slice: tighten summary emission so low-signal summary-only micro-session output is suppressed earlier in ingest. Goal is to reduce recap sludge at write time before pack weighting has to compensate.

title: Investigate unmapped session relinking and compaction strategy
description: Assess whether historical unmapped raw-event/plugin sessions can be recovered instead of discarded. Determine how many unmapped sessions still retain stable streamId/opencodeSessionId metadata, whether multiple local sessions share the same stable stream, and whether a safe relink/compaction strategy can merge or re-anchor these sessions before any destructive cleanup.
notes: Attempted to package relink-impact simulation update onto PR 609, but local signing/auth blocked commit and push (1Password signing failure and SSH agent refused operation). Changes remain staged locally and validated; continuing next remediation-planner slice locally while this packaging issue remains operationally blocked.
close_reason: Completed via merged relink investigation, automatic reconciliation, and public-safe remediation stack (#621-#626).

title: Investigate micro-session regression around TS migration
description: Investigate whether the spike in under-one-minute sessions and unmapped summary production was introduced during the Python-to-TypeScript migration or a later raw-event/sessionization change. Correlate timeline evidence, relevant commits/docs, and session mapping behavior.
close_reason: Root cause identified and fixed via merged TypeScript raw-event session parity/reconciliation work. The micro-session and unmapped-summary spike was traced primarily to missing stable-session reuse / opencode bridge parity in the TS raw-event ingest path, later repaired by the merged remediation stack (#621 plus the raw-event stable-session reuse fix lineage). Remaining work is now Track 3 policy/model follow-up, not root-cause investigation.

title: Design sessionization and summary emission policy
description: Define how codemem should distinguish turn-sized activity from a real work session. Investigate micro-session overproduction, when summaries should be suppressed or delayed, whether multiple short sessions should be merged conceptually, and whether turn recap vs durable session summary should become separate artifacts. This design should feed both future retrieval quality work and retroactive DB remediation planning.
notes: Track 3 policy now has concrete artifacts plus an initial implementation slice. Docs: track-3-injection-first-memory-policy.md and session-boundary-matrix.md. Code: session-policy.ts now classifies sessions and drives summary-only suppression in ingest-pipeline.ts. Remaining 1y5q work should focus on deeper session boundary continuity/merging rules and how session class should be persisted or reused by retrieval/injection.

title: Design memory quality model and taxonomy evolution
description: Define an evidence-backed memory quality model for codemem before retroactive DB remediation. Cover recap vs durable memory, summary handling, low-value/repetitive memory policy, taxonomy evolution, project-local vs cross-project/general knowledge, and how candidate rules can be evaluated against a snapshot of the existing database.
notes: Shipped follow-up PR 608 adding mapped vs unmapped lineage reporting to the memory role snapshot harness. This now separates stable OpenCode-session lineage from detached local-session lineage in both aggregate counts and probe outputs.

title: Unify summary representation and retrieval contract
description: Fix the split summary model where some summaries are stored as kind session_summary while others are stored as kind change with metadata.is_summary = 1. Align ingest, retrieval, docs, and tests on one summary representation and define migration/compatibility behavior.
close_reason: Completed via merged summary memory handling unification and compatibility contract fixes (#626).

title: Audit memory signal quality, duplication, and taxonomy gaps
description: Investigate whether low-value, repetitive, or weakly categorized memories are degrading retrieval quality. Evaluate current memory kinds, summary behavior, project scoping, and whether short-term vs long-term or project-local vs cross-project distinctions are needed.

title: Diagnose retrieval ranking and pack ordering issues
description: Investigate why usefulness evals surface generic summaries ahead of stronger recall/task/workingset matches. Identify current ranking/section-order behavior, candidate-set composition, and evidence-backed fixes for PR 3.
close_reason: Completed via merged retrieval ranking, query expansion, and recap fallback fixes (#623-#625).

title: Implement context injection and pack-quality validation
description: Execute the approved design from docs/plans/2026-04-05-context-injection-test-design.md. Deliver deterministic adapter contract coverage for OpenCode and Claude hook paths, strengthen core pack invariants, add CLI/manual contract coverage, and add curated usefulness evals for pack quality.

title: Fix shell completion list and sync README/docs with shipped commands
description:
Audit violations: #50, #52, #53, #54, #55, #56

Shell completion list in index.ts is missing db, embed, config, setup, version. README command table is missing config, memory subcommands, most db subcommands. User-guide is missing sync doctor, sync bootstrap, coordinator bootstrap grant commands.

## Work

1. Sync the shell completion list in packages/cli/src/index.ts with all registered commands.
2. Update README.md command table to include all shipped commands.
3. Update docs/user-guide.md sync section with doctor, bootstrap, and other missing commands.
4. Update docs/coordinator-discovery.md with list-bootstrap-grants and revoke-bootstrap-grant.

## Acceptance criteria

- Shell completion lists every registered command.
- README command table matches the shipped tree.
- User-guide covers all sync subcommands.
- Coordinator docs cover bootstrap grant commands.

Parent epic: codemem-vcam
close_reason: Shipped in PR #597: shell completion synced, README grouped command table, user-guide and coordinator docs updated

title: Fix uncaught throws in CLI action handlers
description:
Audit violations: #32, #33, #34, #36, #37

Several action handlers throw uncaught Errors, producing stack traces instead of user-friendly messages.

Must-fix:
- enqueue-raw-event.ts: 5 throw new Error() calls in action handler (lines 36, 39, 52, 53, 56)
- memory.ts remember handler: throw err at line 96

Should-fix:
- serve.ts startBackgroundViewer: throw new Error at line 322
- mcp.ts: unguarded dynamic import at line 13
- Various --json failure paths fall through to p.log.error instead of emitting structured JSON

## Work

1. Wrap each action handler in try/catch at the boundary.
2. Catch errors, format as user-friendly messages, set process.exitCode.
3. In --json mode, emit {error, message} to stdout instead of p.log.error to stderr.
4. For plumbing commands (enqueue-raw-event), emit structured error to stdout per conventions.

## Acceptance criteria

- No CLI action handler throws uncaught Errors.
- Bad input produces a clear message, not a stack trace.
- --json mode always gets structured failure output.
- Tests verify error handling paths.

Parent epic: codemem-vcam
close_reason: Shipped in PR #597: all action handlers catch at boundary, structured JSON errors, no more uncaught throws

title: Fix positional argument violations: sync bootstrap, config workspace
description:
Audit violations: #8, #9

sync bootstrap uses --peer as a requiredOption. The conventions doc calls this out as the canonical anti-pattern example. config workspace uses --workspace-id as a requiredOption when it should be a positional.

## Work

1. sync bootstrap: change .requiredOption("--peer <device-id>") to .argument("<peer-device-id>"). Keep --peer as a hidden alias during deprecation.
2. config workspace: change .requiredOption("--workspace-id <id>") to .argument("<workspace-id>"). Keep --workspace-id as a hidden alias.
3. Update tests and docs to use the positional form.

## Acceptance criteria

- codemem sync bootstrap abc123 works (positional).
- codemem sync bootstrap --peer abc123 works with deprecation warning.
- codemem config workspace my-ws --enable-sync works (positional).
- Tests pass.

Parent epic: codemem-vcam
close_reason: Shipped in PR #597: sync bootstrap <peer-device-id> positional, config workspace <workspace-id> positional, old flags kept as hidden aliases

title: Deprecate sync start/stop/restart lifecycle aliases
description:
Audit violations: #38, #39, #40, #41

sync start|stop|restart are lifecycle aliases for serve start|stop|restart. The conventions doc explicitly says these should not exist. serve --stop, --restart, --background are legacy flags that should also be deprecated.

## Work

1. sync start/stop/restart: emit deprecation warning to stderr, then delegate to serve start/stop/restart.
2. serve --stop/--restart/--background: accept silently with deprecation warning, delegate to the action-based equivalent.
3. Hide all deprecated forms from --help and shell completion.
4. Keep them functional for the current release window.

## Acceptance criteria

- sync start emits warning and runs serve start.
- serve --stop emits warning and runs serve stop.
- Deprecated commands do not appear in --help.
- Shell completion list does not include sync start/stop/restart.
- Tests updated to cover the deprecation warnings.

Parent epic: codemem-vcam
close_reason: Shipped in PR #597: sync start/stop/restart emit deprecation warnings and delegate to serve; serve legacy flags hidden

title: Create shared CLI option builders for --db-path, --config, --json with standard short flags
description:
Audit violations: #11, #12, #13, #14, #15, #16

Every CLI command currently copy-pastes .option("--db <path>") and .option("--db-path <path>") ad-hoc. Same for --config and --json. No command defines the standard short flags (-d, -c, -j) from docs/cli-design-conventions.md.

## Work

1. Create shared option-builder helpers (e.g. addDbOption, addConfigOption, addJsonOption) in a new packages/cli/src/shared-options.ts.
2. Each builder defines both short and long forms: -d/--db-path, -c/--config, -j/--json.
3. --db becomes a hidden alias for --db-path (accepted but not shown in --help).
4. --user and --system become hidden no-ops (Python-era Typer leftovers).
5. Refactor all command modules to use the shared builders instead of copy-paste.
6. Add -f/--foreground to serve start via the shared pattern.

## Acceptance criteria

- No command module defines --db, --db-path, --config, or --json via raw .option() calls.
- -d, -c, -j short flags work on all commands that accept the corresponding long flag.
- --db is accepted but hidden from --help.
- pnpm typecheck and pnpm test pass.

## Files touched

Every file under packages/cli/src/commands/*.ts plus a new shared-options.ts.

Parent epic: codemem-vcam
close_reason: Shipped in PR #596 + #597: shared option builders with -d/-c/-j short flags, resolveDbOpt helper, --db hidden alias

title: Audit and consolidate the codemem CLI command tree
description:
## Problem

The CLI surface has grown organically and is approaching the point where it's hard to reason about. Discovered while reviewing PRs 594 (config-first workspace) and 595 (bootstrap grant), which each added ~9 new flags or subcommands to the already-large codemem sync tree.

## Design philosophy

The CLI design conventions are now documented in docs/cli-design-conventions.md. All audit findings and target-shape proposals should be evaluated against that document. The conventions doc was written as part of this epic's prep work.

## Current smells

- codemem sync now has ~15 subcommands including a nested sync coordinator subtree with its own ~10 subcommands.
- codemem config just landed with a single workspace subcommand exposing only 6 of ~30 config keys.
- Almost every sync subcommand takes --db, --db-path, --config, --host, --port. --db and --db-path are duplicated aliases.
- Lifecycle flags (--background, --foreground, --stop, --restart) coexist with explicit subcommands (serve start|stop|restart).
- Coordinator admin commands are a CLI wrapper around the coordinator admin HTTP API, buried under sync.
- codemem serve vs codemem sync serve start — two ways to launch the viewer with overlapping flag semantics.

## Goals of the audit

1. Inventory the full command tree with file:line references.
2. Identify duplicated flag definitions that should use shared option builders.
3. Identify commands that are aliases, legacy, or redundant.
4. Propose a target command tree grouped by concern, grounded in docs/cli-design-conventions.md.
5. Identify which commands are load-bearing for automation vs interactive use.
6. Propose a deprecation path for anything removed.

## Non-goals

- Don't do the refactor in this epic. Produce the audit + target shape + migration plan.
- Don't change command behavior in the audit phase.

## Deliverables

- Written inventory (markdown under docs/)
- Target command tree diagram
- List of child tasks for consolidation work
close_reason: Epic complete: CLI audit done, conventions doc shipped, all 10 child tasks closed via PRs #596 + #597. Remaining cleanup items in codemem-a22l (P3).

title: Add workspace bootstrap script template
description: Add a public-safe workspace bootstrap script template that applies the pre-provisioned workspace runtime attachment contract: install/configure codemem, initialize identity, join the coordinator, bootstrap from the seed peer when required, and verify readiness. The template should be documented as a starting point, not a private-environment-specific implementation.
acceptance_criteria: A checked-in public-safe bootstrap script template exists and matches the workspace runtime attachment contract; it documents required inputs and emits clear readiness/failure output.
close_reason: Added public-safe workspace bootstrap script template. Shipped in PR #600: workspace bootstrap script template covering all 7 contract phases with structured readiness output.

title: Add fleet cleanup command for ephemeral peers
description: Implement an operator-facing cleanup command or scenario helper for the seed-peer fleet pilot that removes or reports stale ephemeral peers safely, while protecting stable peers and the seed peer. Keep the first cut aligned with the pilot's spec-backed Compose proving path.
acceptance_criteria: A fleet-aware cleanup surface exists for the Compose pilot; it only targets ephemeral worker peers declared in the fleet spec; it leaves the seed peer untouched; it emits a machine-readable result and useful artifacts.
close_reason: Implemented fleet cleanup scenario for ephemeral worker peers

title: Implement fleet-spec join and bootstrap workflow
description: Extend the seed-peer fleet pilot beyond fleet smoke by driving coordinator group materialization, worker invite/join flow, and initial bootstrap from the checked-in fleet spec for the Compose proving backend. The implementation should keep using public-safe naming and produce topology/readiness artifacts for each worker.
acceptance_criteria: A spec-backed fleet scenario can create the declared coordinator groups, join the declared workers, bootstrap them from the seed peer, and record readiness state transitions and artifacts for each worker.
close_reason: Implemented spec-driven fleet-ready scenario with group materialization, worker join, bootstrap, and readiness snapshot

title: Add fleet spec and Compose fleet-smoke scenario
description: Implement the first thin vertical slice of the seed-peer fleet pilot: add an executable fleet spec format, check in a Compose-backed sample spec with one durable seed peer and two isolated swarm groups, and add a fleet-smoke scenario that loads the spec, starts the declared services, verifies codemem CLI reachability on those nodes, and records a resolved-topology artifact.
acceptance_criteria: A checked-in fleet spec exists for the Compose proving backend; the E2E runner can run a fleet-smoke scenario from that spec; the scenario starts the declared services and records the resolved fleet topology; codemem CLI reachability is verified on the seed and worker peers.
close_reason: Implemented fleet spec, Compose sample spec, and validated fleet-smoke scenario

title: Define workspace runtime attachment contract
description: Document the pre-provisioned workspace attachment contract for the fleet pilot, including required inputs, config and storage expectations, bootstrap steps, readiness checks, and cleanup hooks.
acceptance_criteria: A public-safe workspace runtime contract exists and is aligned with the fleet spec shape from the pilot design.
close_reason: Documented public-safe pre-provisioned workspace runtime attachment contract

title: Implement seed-peer fleet orchestration pilot
description: Implement the seed-peer fleet orchestration pilot described in docs/plans/2026-04-02-seed-peer-fleet-orchestration-pilot-design.md. This epic covers the fleet spec, local Compose proving path enhancements, pre-provisioned workspace attachment contract, lifecycle cleanup surfaces, readiness checks, and the first thin vertical slices needed to prove the model cheaply while preserving later scale options.
close_reason: Seed-peer fleet orchestration pilot track completed: design, implementation plan, fleet spec, proving scenarios, readiness flow, cleanup flow, and workspace bootstrap template are all delivered.

title: Design seed-peer fleet orchestration pilot
description: Design a seed-peer-based fleet orchestration pilot for codemem that supports multiple isolated swarms, a durable shared seed peer, local Compose proving, and a pre-provisioned workspace attachment path. The design should optimize for cheap internal validation now while preserving a credible path to later scale and possible backend evolution if adoption demands it.
close_reason: Design documented in docs/plans/2026-04-02-seed-peer-fleet-orchestration-pilot-design.md

title: Add failure artifact collection and diagnostics for E2E runs
description: Implement artifact capture for failing multi-node E2E runs, including compose logs, command transcripts, status snapshots, and copied databases or equivalent state. Make failures diagnosable without rerunning blind.
acceptance_criteria: Failed E2E runs collect useful artifacts automatically; artifact output is stored in ignored local paths; the runner surfaces where artifacts were written.
close_reason: Implemented failure artifact collection and diagnostics in the E2E runner and artifact helpers

title: Add E2E smoke scenario for stack bring-up and health
description: Add the first E2E smoke scenario that validates the local topology can start cleanly and that key services or commands are reachable. This is the first vertical slice proving the harness works at all.
acceptance_criteria: A smoke scenario can be run locally through the E2E runner; it validates stack startup and basic command/health behavior; it uses the shared topology and artifact plumbing.
close_reason: Implemented and validated smoke scenario with pnpm run e2e:smoke

title: Automate coordinator invite, join, approval, and discovery scenarios
description: Implement E2E scenarios covering coordinator group creation, invite generation, invite import, approval-required flows, and coordinator-backed discovery convergence across multiple peers.
acceptance_criteria: Local E2E scenarios validate invite creation/import, join approval when required, and coordinator discovery state for enrolled peers.
close_reason: Implemented and validated coordinator invite/join/approval/discovery scenario with pnpm run e2e:coordinator

title: Implement seed-data subsystem for E2E fixtures
description: Add explicit seed modes for E2E testing: empty, fixture-small, fixture-large, and local-import. The subsystem should support deterministic synthetic fixture generation and local-only real-data import without making personal data part of shared automation.
acceptance_criteria: The harness supports the documented seed modes; synthetic fixtures are deterministic from a seed value; local-import is opt-in and clearly isolated from shared automation.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic synthetic seed modes plus local-import path in e2e/seeds/load.ts and e2e/lib/seed.ts; validated fixture-small seed loader

title: Automate direct peer sync with data-plane assertions
description: Implement E2E scenarios for accepting discovered peers, triggering direct sync, and asserting that expected shared data replicates while filtered or private data does not leak.
acceptance_criteria: A local E2E scenario validates direct peer sync and proves expected data arrives on the receiving node using data-plane assertions, not just command success.
close_reason: Implemented and validated direct sync E2E scenario with data-plane assertions; fixed sync CLI and sync protocol identity handling needed for reliable multi-peer validation

title: Automate bootstrap and dirty-local refusal scenarios
description: Implement E2E scenarios for empty-peer bootstrap using a larger fixture dataset and for refusal behavior when the target has dirty local shared state.
acceptance_criteria: Local E2E scenarios validate initial bootstrap to an empty peer and refusal behavior for dirty local state, using the larger synthetic fixture mode.
close_reason: Implemented and validated bootstrap-to-empty-peer and dirty-local refusal E2E scenarios

title: Build host-run E2E runner skeleton and scenario framework
description: Create the host-run TypeScript E2E runner that can bring the stack up/down, reset state, execute commands inside named containers, select scenarios, and support scenario context plus wait/assert helpers.
acceptance_criteria: A local TypeScript entrypoint exists for running named E2E scenarios; runner helpers support compose lifecycle, container exec, and scenario selection; the code is structured for adding more scenarios without ad hoc shell sprawl.
close_reason: Implemented host-run TypeScript E2E runner skeleton in e2e/bin/run-local.ts and e2e/lib/*

title: Create Docker Compose topology and peer runtime contract
description: Add the first reusable local topology for E2E sync testing: coordinator, peer-a, peer-b, and optional peer-c. Define container images, mounted data/config/keys/artifact paths, stable networking, and the minimal runtime contract needed for local orchestration.
acceptance_criteria: A checked-in Docker Compose topology exists for local E2E work; peer/coordinator services have stable names and mounted state paths; the topology is documented enough for the runner to target it deterministically.
close_reason: Implemented Docker Compose topology and peer runtime contract in docker-compose.e2e.yml and e2e/images/peer.Dockerfile

title: Implement Docker/Compose E2E sync harness
description: Implement the local Docker/Compose E2E sync harness described in docs/plans/2026-04-01-docker-e2e-sync-harness-design.md. This epic covers the reusable topology, host-run orchestration harness, seed-data subsystem, coordinator scenarios, direct sync scenarios, bootstrap scenarios, and future CI-hardening follow-up work.
design: Use Docker Compose for topology, a host-run TypeScript runner for orchestration, public CLI/API entrypoints for validation, and explicit seed modes (empty, fixture-small, fixture-large, local-import).
notes: Implementation should be delivered in thin vertical slices starting with topology + runner skeleton + smoke scenario.

title: Design local Docker/Compose E2E sync harness
description: Design a first-version local Docker/Compose-based end-to-end test harness for codemem sync flows. The goal is to automate coordinator enrollment, coordinator-backed discovery, direct peer sync, and snapshot/bootstrap validation across multiple nodes while also establishing a reusable topology foundation for future multi-peer or swarm-style automation. The design should favor local reproducibility first, avoid CI-only assumptions, define a phased rollout, and include a seed-data strategy that supports both local-only real-data import and long-term deterministic synthetic datasets safe for CI.
close_reason: Design documented in docs/plans/2026-04-01-docker-e2e-sync-harness-design.md

title: Add test coverage for auto-bootstrap sync path
description: The auto-bootstrap path (localSharedCount === 0 → fetchAllSnapshotPages → applyBootstrapSnapshot) has zero test coverage. Add integration tests for: happy path, non-empty local state falls through to incremental, failure during fetch.

title: Fix workspace TypeScript declaration build failures blocking release validation
description: Release prep uncovered pre-existing workspace build failures unrelated to the 0.22.4 version bump. Repro: pnpm build, pnpm --filter @codemem/mcp build, and pnpm --filter @codemem/cloudflare-coordinator-worker build. Evidence: packages/core/dist contains declaration map files like index.d.ts.map and sync-pass.d.ts.map, but corresponding .d.ts files are missing for many modules. Downstream TypeScript builds then fail with TS6305 when importing @codemem/core declarations. The worker build also has TS7006 for the untyped input parameter in packages/cloudflare-coordinator-worker/src/request-verifier.ts line 83. Fix the core declaration emission problem and then re-verify downstream package builds.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #599: removed per-package tsc --build from vite build scripts, centralizing declaration generation at the root. All 887 tests pass.

title: Prepare 0.22.4 release PR
description: Create the 0.22.4 release-prep branch and PR by bumping required package/plugin metadata versions, regenerating any required JS lockfile/artifacts, validating the affected release surfaces, and submitting a PR using the repository release workflow.
close_reason: Completed in PR #581 for release 0.22.4 prep

title: Fast bootstrap mode for new sync peers
description: New peers currently pull ~200 memories per sync interval, which means bootstrapping a node from a source with 23,700+ memories takes an unacceptably long time. Need a bulk/fast bootstrap mode that can quickly seed a new peer with the full memory set, and separately evaluate whether the ongoing 200-per-interval limit should be raised.

title: Fix worker build race against core declarations
description: Merged build for 0.22.3 exposed a race where packages/cloudflare-coordinator-worker manually rebuilt @codemem/core inside its own build/typecheck scripts while sibling packages were consuming core dist declarations, causing transient declaration parse failures in CI. Switch the worker package to reference-aware TypeScript build mode and remove the nested core rebuild from normal build/typecheck scripts.
close_reason: Already shipped via #577 fix(build): stop worker rebuild race against core. Commit message exactly matches the worker build race this issue describes. Stale in_progress entry cleanup.

title: Fix TS db init bootstrap path
description: codemem db init and codemem serve start fail on fresh/uninitialized SQLite databases because the TS runtime only validates schema readiness instead of bootstrapping the schema it now effectively owns. Fix init/bootstrap behavior so fresh DBs can be created or initialized cleanly, and cover it with CLI/core tests.
close_reason: Already shipped via #575 fix(db): bootstrap fresh databases for init and serve. Commit addresses the fresh-db bootstrap failure this issue describes. Stale in_progress entry cleanup.

title: Add canonical Cloudflare coordinator deployment runbook
description: Write product-grade docs for deploying the coordinator on Cloudflare Workers + D1 using the validated post-0.22 TypeScript path. Include exact worker package/config/schema sources, secret setup, D1 creation, deploy steps, smoke validation, invite/join/approval expectations, and troubleshooting. Update existing coordinator docs to point to this canonical runbook instead of duplicating stale or partial steps.

title: Raw event flush batches retry forever with no max attempt limit
description: When the observer returns null (raw: null) during raw-event flush, the code throws 'observer failed during raw-event flush' which records a failure on the batch but never checks attempt_count against a max. The sweeper retries the same batch indefinitely (117+ attempts observed). This wastes API calls and blocks all flush progress for the session. Fix: check attempt_count in the flush path and mark batches as permanently failed after a configurable max (e.g., 5 attempts), advancing the flush cursor so other sessions can proceed.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #528 — added max retry limit (default 5), batches marked gave_up after exceeding

title: Sync partially working — memories from 2 days ago visible but nothing recent
description: On pi4 (2c0d24c8), memories from m4x (a20d560e) are visible from 2 days ago but nothing recent (today). On m4x, no 'other actor' memories appear at all. Needs runtime investigation: check sync daemon status on both devices, check last_sync_at timestamps, verify peers are configured correctly on both sides with bidirectional trust, and inspect sync attempt logs for errors or scope filtering that might exclude recent memories.
close_reason: Investigated — sync was working but throttled at 200 ops/pass catching up a backlog. Observer config issue on pi4 was the flush blocker, not a code bug.

title: Devices list under People & Devices goes blank on peer accept / route changes
description: renderSyncPeers() in people.ts:80 does syncPeers.textContent = '' before calling renderSyncPeersList() which uses Preact render(). This destroys Preact's VDOM tracking. When accepting a discovered peer triggers loadSyncData() → re-render while the VDOM is desynced, the devices list goes blank. Hard refresh works because it resets Preact's internal state. Fix: remove the textContent = '' call and rely on Preact's render() for reconciliation, same pattern as the health cards fix.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #525 — removed textContent='' before Preact render in devices list

title: Health cards vanish after brief flicker on Health tab
description: renderHealthOverview() uses healthGrid.textContent = '' (health.ts:237) before Preact render(), which destroys Preact's internal VDOM reference for the container. Additionally, both loadHealthData() and loadSyncData() call renderHealthOverview() concurrently during refresh, creating a race where one call's textContent='' wipes the other's rendered output. Fix: remove the textContent='' call and let Preact's render() handle reconciliation. Ensure only one final renderHealthOverview() fires per refresh cycle, or guard against concurrent calls.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #524 — removed textContent='' before Preact render in health tab

title: Treat zero-output raw-event flushes as terminal no-op

This causes backlog growth with repeated retries for sessions that legitimately produce no storable memories. Fix by distinguishing retryable observer failures from terminal no-op flushes, advancing flush state without storing memories for the latter, and preserving lossless semantics for true observer/parse/auth failures.

title: Separate prune progress chunking from delete budget

Optionally remeasure size less often than every progress chunk to improve throughput.

comments.author: Adam Kunicki
comments.text: Implemented on top of the prune redesign stack: the offline prune command now separates operator delete budget from progress granularity via --progress-ops, uses chunked age-pass updates, and remeasures size less frequently during size-pass progress.

title: Chunk age-pass bulk deletes with live progress updates
description: Replace the single large age-cutoff delete in the offline prune command with large oldest-first chunks and live progress output after each chunk. Keep the direct oldest-prefix strategy, but make it observable and less like a hung process. Preserve retained-floor correctness.
close_reason: Completed — age-pass uses 10k-row oldest-first chunks with budget tracking, live progress via onPass callback, retained-floor updates per chunk

title: Plan replication-op age prune before deleting
description: Before offline pruning begins, compute the age-pass delete plan: candidate op count, approximate bytes, cutoff cursor, and estimated batches. This gives the CLI a real denominator for progress reporting and makes the prune behavior observable before any deletes occur.
close_reason: Shipped in pruning PRs #435

title: Replace per-op size trimming with chunked oldest-prefix deletes
description: After age pruning, size-based retention should trim the oldest prefix in large op-count chunks rather than deleting one row at a time. Use approximate size measurements between chunks, not after every row. The goal is to get under the configured max size efficiently, not to achieve byte-perfect trimming.
close_reason: Completed — size-pass uses 10k-row oldest-prefix chunks with approximate re-measurement between chunks, not per-row

title: Replace per-op pruning with cutoff-based bulk age pruning
description: Current replication_ops pruning is too slow on multi-GB logs because it deletes one op at a time. Replace the age-pruning path with deterministic cutoff-based bulk deletes using the ordered cursor (created_at, op_id). Compute the last cursor at/before the age cutoff, delete the oldest range in large chunks, and update retained_floor_cursor to the last deleted cursor. This should become the default strategy for age-based retention in both the background runner and offline command.
close_reason: Shipped in pruning PRs #435

title: Allow hotfix tags from release branches
description: The release workflow hard-fails unless a tag commit is reachable from origin/main. That blocks legitimate hotfix releases tagged from release/* branches. Update release-tag preflight to allow tag commits reachable from main or a single release/* branch, while still rejecting feature-branch tags and ambiguous branch ancestry.

title: Add offline prune-replication-ops command with dry-run and progress

title: Define retention/pruning strategy for replication_ops log
description: Large long-lived codemem DBs are dominated by replication_ops (e.g. ~15.7GB of a 16.9GB DB). Define a safe retention policy and pruning/re-bootstrap strategy for replication_ops so peers can still recover within a bounded offline window, while stale peers beyond that window require re-bootstrap instead of unbounded log retention. Cover what data is canonical vs replay-only, peer catch-up windows, operational tooling, and user-visible consequences of pruning.

title: serve restart race: stop doesn't wait for process exit before rebinding
description: codemem serve restart sends stop signal but doesn't wait for the old process to fully exit before attempting to bind the new listener. On slow shutdown (large DB close, sweeper drain), the new process hits EADDRINUSE. Need to poll for port release or wait for process exit before starting.
close_reason: Shipped across #414, #406, #411, #488

title: Split viewer and sync into separate listeners on same process
description: Viewer UI/API should bind 127.0.0.1:38888 (localhost only), sync protocol endpoints (/v1/status, /v1/ops) should bind 0.0.0.0:7337 (network-accessible for peer sync). Both listeners share the same Node process, MemoryStore, and lifecycle. This matches Python's separate-port model while avoiding multi-process complexity.
close_reason: Completed: merged PR #411, viewer on 127.0.0.1:38888 and sync protocol on 0.0.0.0:7337 in same process. Will ship in next release.
