comments.author: Adam Kunicki
comments.text: Shipped via PR #408 (https://github.com/kunickiaj/codemem/pull/408), squash-merged to main as 767f01c. Tagged main as v0.20.1 and pushed tag. Release workflow succeeded: https://github.com/kunickiaj/codemem/actions/runs/23504087440

title: Auto-heal legacy raw_event_flush_batches columns on startup
description: Fix sweeper spam on upgraded installs where raw_event_flush_batches is missing newer error detail columns (for example error_message). Add additive schema compatibility repair at startup so viewer/sweeper can run against older DBs without manual ALTER TABLE.
close_reason: Completed and validated.

comments.author: Adam Kunicki
comments.text: Implemented additive startup schema compatibility in core DB layer: ensures legacy raw_event_flush_batches DBs gain missing columns (error_message, error_type, observer_provider/model/runtime, attempt_count) before sweeper runs. Wired into MemoryStore constructor after schema readiness checks. Added db.test coverage for missing-column upgrade path. Validation passed: targeted core vitest suites + core typecheck.

title: Run coordinator dogfood verification matrix (2+ devices + failure paths)
description: Execute and document the coordinator release checklist as evidence: 1) serve + admin secret setup, 2) invite create/share/import/join UI-to-UI, 3) approval_required flow, 4) deployment guide verification against real deployment, 5) Sync tab UX on 2+ real devices, 6) error paths (bad invite, expired invite, unreachable coordinator, missing admin secret), 7) invite import config persistence. Capture pass/fail notes and follow-up bugs.
close_reason: Verified — m4x+pi4 multi-device sync confirmed working 2026-03-30

comments.author: Adam Kunicki
comments.text: Local verification pass complete: ran targeted coordinator/invite/viewer tests via 'pnpm run test -- packages/core/src/coordinator-store.test.ts packages/core/src/coordinator-invites.test.ts packages/viewer-server/src/index.test.ts' (vitest reported 36 files, 616 tests, all passing). This provides automated coverage evidence for coordinator primitives, invite flows, and viewer/server integration paths. Remaining checklist items still require manual dogfood execution on 2+ real devices and deployment-environment validation.

title: Investigate post-upgrade lifecycle regressions after alpha.6
description: User reports lifecycle bugs after restart post-upgrade to 0.20.0-alpha.6. Capture concrete failing flows (startup hooks, session lifecycle events, sweeper state transitions, plugin hook ingestion) and identify whether this is release-regression or pre-existing behavior. Add repro steps and targeted fix PRs.
close_reason: Completed via merged PR #406. Restart now handles upgrade scenarios where pidfile is missing by using local stats/listener PID fallback with process-identity checks before signaling.

comments.author: Adam Kunicki
comments.text: Captured reproducible lifecycle regression after global alpha upgrade (restart can fail when viewer pidfile is missing). Proposed fix in PR #406: add viewer_pid to /api/stats and use stats-based PID fallback in serve restart stop path.

comments.author: Adam Kunicki
comments.text: Addressed PR #406 feedback: restart fallback now handles older viewers that do not expose viewer_pid by falling back to listener PID lookup on local host/port, and adds process identity checks before signaling (listener PID match + command-line fingerprint) to avoid terminating unrelated processes.

title: TS sync preflight still lacks legacy replication backfill
description: The TypeScript sync preflight still carries a TODO for migration/backfill of legacy import keys and replication state. Without this, upgraded or pre-existing memory rows may never get replication ops, creating silent sync gaps around 0.20 rollout. Evidence: packages/core/src/sync-pass.ts:287. Fix by porting the legacy import-key migration and replication backfill behavior before release, with upgrade-focused tests for existing databases.
close_reason: Completed in merged TS sync parity stack (PR #362)

title: TS inbound sync does not apply replication ops
description: Inbound replication in the TypeScript port records transport-level ops but does not materialize them into memory_items. packages/core/src/sync-pass.ts still has a placeholder receive path and an explicit note that materialization is not done yet. This means peers can appear to sync successfully while remote memories never show up or update locally. Evidence: packages/core/src/sync-pass.ts:129 and packages/core/src/sync-pass.ts:392. Fix by integrating applyReplicationOps on inbound batches and only advancing the applied cursor after successful application.
close_reason: Completed in merged TS sync parity stack (PR #362)

title: TS sync client targets missing peer endpoints
description: The TypeScript sync client in packages/core/src/sync-pass.ts calls peer endpoints /v1/status and /v1/ops, but the TypeScript peer server routes are exposed under packages/viewer-server/src/routes/sync.ts as /api/sync/* instead. In TS-only deployments this makes peer sync fail outright. Evidence: packages/core/src/sync-pass.ts:345,370,403 and packages/viewer-server/src/routes/sync.ts:161. Fix by either implementing the /v1/status and /v1/ops endpoints on the TS server with matching auth semantics, or retargeting sync-pass to the actual TS routes and aligning request/response contracts.
close_reason: Completed in merged TS sync parity stack (PR #362)

title: Add coordinator invite and review routes in TS viewer/server
description: Port the Python 0.19 coordinator invite/import/review routes into the TypeScript viewer/server. Add routes for creating invites, importing invites, listing join requests, and reviewing join requests using the existing TS coordinator primitives. This slice should make the coordinator onboarding workflow usable from the viewer once sync status parity is honest.
close_reason: Completed: viewer/server exposes coordinator invite/import/list/review routes.

title: Add sync coordinator CLI commands in TS
description: Port the Python 0.19  command surface into the TypeScript CLI. Minimum scope: serve, create-invite, import-invite, list-join-requests, approve-join-request, deny-join-request. This should stack on top of the coordinator status + viewer route parity work so the same primitives are exercised end-to-end.
close_reason: Completed: coordinator CLI commands (serve/create-invite/import/list/review) are present in TS sync command surface.

title: Fix sync status coordinator parity in TS viewer/server
description: Port Python 0.19 sync/coordinator status behavior into the TypeScript viewer/server. Replace placeholder values in packages/viewer-server/src/routes/sync.ts (enabled=true, interval_s=60, coordinator disabled/configured=false, join_requests=[], legacy_devices=[]) with real config/runtime/coordinator state. Wire coordinator status snapshot and join-request listing from TS coordinator primitives so the sync tab reflects actual coordinator readiness before invite/review work lands.
close_reason: Completed: sync status route now reports live coordinator/runtime state instead of placeholders.

title: Fix observer custom-provider model handling and settings UX
description: Observed while dogfooding TS codemem for 0.20 parity. Saved observer config can be `observer_provider = openai` with `observer_model = opencode/gpt-5.4-mini`, and recent raw-event flush batches fail with `OpenAI returned no usable output for raw-event processing.` The settings UI provider list does not include custom/OpenCode providers, and codemem currently routes `opencode/...`-style models through the OpenAI path when no matching custom provider config is resolved. This creates a bad UX where the model field accepts values that are not actually valid for the chosen/effective provider. Need to: 1) clarify/validate provider vs model compatibility, 2) surface custom/OpenCode providers from config in the UI or reject unsupported prefixed models, 3) make effective observer status less misleading, and 4) preserve queued raw events without silent loss while misconfigured.
close_reason: Completed by merged PR #356 (fix observer provider resolution and settings options). Provider/model resolution now handles opencode/... models, settings use real provider options, and observer provider UX mismatch path is addressed.

title: Fix wedged serve lifecycle when viewer is already running
description: Observed on local TS codemem after restarting OpenCode. `codemem serve restart` reports `Viewer already running at http://127.0.0.1:38888` instead of restarting. `codemem serve stop` then says `No background viewer found`, while `codemem serve start` still says `Viewer already running at http://127.0.0.1:38888`. This appears to happen specifically when the OpenCode plugin autostarts the server; manual `codemem serve` usage works normally. This suggests serve lifecycle detection is inconsistent between plugin-autostarted viewer instances and pid/background management, likely due to stale pidfile handling, foreground/background mismatch, or port-only detection without owned-process verification. Repro: let the OpenCode plugin autostart the viewer, then run `codemem serve restart`, `codemem serve stop`, and `codemem serve start`. Expected: restart/stop/start should manage the same viewer instance cleanly.
close_reason: No longer reproducible after viewer/runtime/install fixes during 0.20 dogfooding; plugin-autostarted serve lifecycle now works in local verification

title: Project detection is broken for new TS-created memories (shows project: n/a)
description: Dogfood on alpha.5: newly created memories sometimes show `Project: n/a` instead of the expected repo/project name. This appears on TS-created observer/session-summary style memories. Need to trace project propagation end-to-end: plugin/worktree cwd -> raw event session metadata -> observer/session context -> persisted session/project fields -> viewer rendering. Impact: memories lose one of the most important scoping signals.
close_reason: Implemented in stack PR #352

title: Improve observer memory quality/parity versus Python output
description: Dogfood finding: TS-created memories are materially worse than the old Python product on first impression. Some of this was hidden by the collapsed summary UI, but there is still quality drift in what the observer extracts and how it structures summaries/facts. Compare TS observer outputs against known-good Python examples and tighten prompts/parsing/post-processing so generated memories are similarly specific, useful, and well-structured.
close_reason: Implemented initial parity slice in stack PR #352

title: OpenCode plugin should auto-start background viewer on load
description: The Python OpenCode plugin auto-started the background viewer/daemon process when loaded. The TS plugin does not — the user has to manually run `codemem serve start` before the plugin can flush events or serve the viewer. The plugin should detect whether the viewer is running on startup and start it in the background if not, matching the Python behavior.
close_reason: Fixed in #345 (runner detection) and #347 (auto-start logging)

title: Anthropic observer intermittently hits 401 and backs off for 300s
description: Dogfood on codemem@0.20.0-alpha.2: raw event flush intermittently logs `codemem: observer auth error — backing off for 300s ... (Anthropic auth error: 401)` mixed with repeated `observer failed during raw-event flush` for the same OpenCode session stream. This is not the steady-state parser bug alone; there is an intermittent auth failure path as well. Need to determine whether credentials are sourced inconsistently, tokens are expiring without refresh, or auth failures are being collapsed into the generic flush-failed message on retry. Impact: queue backlog grows for 5 minutes at a time and user sees noisy repeated failures, though it may recover later.
close_reason: Root cause fixed in #341 (system prompt), backoff reduced to 60s in #348

title: Fix Anthropic raw-event observer producing no usable output
description: Dogfood bug: raw-event flush batches repeatedly fail with `Anthropic returned no usable output for raw-event processing.` Backlog grows while raw event queueing remains healthy. Evidence now also includes intermittent runs where the same session stream logs repeated `observer failed during raw-event flush` followed by `observer auth error — backing off for 300s ... (Anthropic auth error: 401)`. This suggests two observer-side failure modes may be interleaving: (1) parser/output-shape incompatibility for successful 200 responses, and (2) intermittent Anthropic auth failures / credential refresh issues. Investigate observer-client Anthropic parsers and auth/credential sourcing separately; preserve redacted failure classification before fixing.
close_reason: Root cause fixed in #341 — system prompt was discarded in OAuth consumer path

title: restore search/recent/pack flag parity in TS CLI
description: Python search/recent/pack support project scoping and Python-era option names that TS dropped or renamed. Gaps: search is missing --project and --all-projects and changed default limit from 5 to 10; recent is missing --project and --all-projects; pack is missing --project, --all-projects, --working-set-file, and renamed --token-budget to --budget. Restore compatibility flags/defaults or add aliases and explicit migration docs.
close_reason: Shipped in CLI parity PRs #327-#338

title: restore top-level show/remember/forget aliases in TS CLI
description: Python CLI exposes flat top-level commands: `codemem show`, `codemem remember`, `codemem forget`. TS CLI moved them under `codemem memory ...`, which breaks existing muscle memory, scripts, docs, and examples. Add top-level aliases (or restore the original command shape) while keeping `memory` group if desired.
close_reason: Shipped in CLI parity PRs #327-#338

title: restore sync command parity in TS CLI
description: Python `codemem sync` includes attempts/start/stop/restart/pair/once/doctor/repair-legacy-keys/daemon/install/uninstall plus nested peers/coordinator. TS CLI only has status/enable/disable/peers/connect. Audit which are required for alpha dogfooding and restore high-value lifecycle and diagnostic commands, or add clear compatibility aliases and docs for intentional removals.
close_reason: Shipped in CLI parity PRs #327-#338

title: Audit TS CLI parity against Python CLI
description: Compare the TypeScript codemem CLI against the legacy Python codemem CLI for command names, flags, defaults, help text, env var compatibility, lifecycle semantics, and user-facing behavior. Scope: serve/setup/mcp/memory/sync/db/import-export and any removed or renamed Python-era commands. Produce follow-up bug beads for each material drift/regression found, prioritized by dogfood impact.
close_reason: Shipped in CLI parity PRs #327-#338

title: handle serve port-in-use with friendly error instead of uncaught EADDRINUSE
description: Starting a second codemem serve process on 127.0.0.1:38888 throws an uncaught Node error and stack trace. Repro: start codemem serve, then start it again. Expected: friendly message telling the user the viewer is already running and suggesting stop/restart or a different port. Actual: process crashes with uncaught listen EADDRINUSE.
close_reason: Already handled in serve.ts — both foreground and background paths pre-check port and EADDRINUSE is caught with a friendly message

title: fix codemem serve --background blocking in foreground
description: still attaches to the terminal and blocks until Ctrl-C, despite the flag description saying 'run under a caller-managed background process'. Repro: run ; process prints startup logs and does not return to shell. Need to either (a) actually daemonize/detach, or (b) rename/remove the flag and provide clear foreground/background lifecycle commands. This is dogfood bug #1 after alpha.2 install.
close_reason: Shipped in CLI parity PRs #327-#338

title: restore serve CLI parity with Python (start/stop/restart, foreground/background semantics)
description: The TypeScript CLI changed the viewer command UX from the Python CLI in a breaking way. Current TS: . Python had distinct start/stop/restart commands and  to avoid daemonizing. This is confusing for existing users and docs. Need to restore compatible semantics or add aliases so existing workflows keep working. Acceptance: preserve Python-era UX for serve lifecycle commands, document any intentional deviations, and keep the TS implementation behaviorally consistent.
close_reason: Shipped in CLI parity PRs #327-#338

title: setup creates opencode.json instead of detecting existing opencode.jsonc
description: codemem setup writes MCP config to ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json, but the user already has ~/.config/opencode/opencode.jsonc (JSONC with comments). Setup should detect the existing config file (checking .jsonc first, then .json) and write to it, not create a duplicate. Also needs to handle JSONC parsing (strip comments before JSON.parse, preserve them on write).
close_reason: Shipped in CLI parity PRs #327-#338

title: Migrate viewer_ui into pnpm workspace and drop bun
description:
viewer_ui/ is a standalone vite project that currently uses bun for local dev and has its own package.json outside the pnpm workspace. Should be:
1. Move viewer_ui/ into packages/ (or as a sub-project of viewer-server)
2. Add to pnpm workspace
3. Build with pnpm/vite instead of bun
4. Drop bun from CI (currently only used for plugin smoke tests and viewer build)
5. Migrate plugin smoke tests from bun:test to vitest
6. Remove bun.lock, .opencode/package.json bun dep
close_reason: Fixed in #349 — viewer_ui migrated to packages/ui as @codemem/ui

title: Drizzle: update test-utils.ts to use Drizzle schema for test DB setup
description:
Replace the hand-written CREATE TABLE statements in test-utils.ts with Drizzle's schema push or migration. This eliminates test schema drift — the exact bug that let replication data-loss issues ship.

After this, initTestSchema() uses the same Drizzle schema as production code. Any column added to the schema is automatically present in test DBs.

Depends on: schema definition bead
close_reason: Completed

title: Drizzle: migrate export-import.ts (7 queries)
description:
Replace raw SQL in export-import.ts with Drizzle typed queries.

Fixes audit findings:
- Original timestamps preserved (typed insert uses payload values, not nowIso())
- toJsonNullable for array columns handled by schema type
- Skipped unmapped sessions tracked in result

7 queries covering: querySessions, fetchBySessionIds, insertSession, insertPrompt, insertMemory, insertSummary, findImportedId.

Depends on: schema definition bead
close_reason: Merged in PR #314 — export-import.ts migrated to Drizzle

title: Drizzle: migrate sync-replication.ts (14 queries — record, load, apply ops)
description:
Replace all 14 raw SQL queries in sync-replication.ts with Drizzle typed queries.

This is the highest-risk code in the codebase — the source of 3 shipped data-loss bugs. Typed queries eliminate:
- Column mismatch between record/apply paths (the root cause of blank-memory and dropped-visibility bugs)
- toJson/fromJson ambiguity for JSON columns
- || vs ?? for nullable string fields
- Missing columns in INSERT/UPDATE statements

The Drizzle schema enforces that every INSERT/UPDATE names exactly the columns the table has, with correct types. No more 27-parameter .run() calls.

Depends on: schema definition bead
close_reason: Completed

title: Drizzle: migrate search.ts (8 queries — FTS5, timeline, explain)
description:
Replace raw SQL in search.ts with Drizzle queries where possible.

Note: FTS5 MATCH queries and BM25 scoring may need to stay as raw SQL with Drizzle's sql`` tagged template (Drizzle doesn't have native FTS5 support). The goal is typed result mapping, not necessarily replacing every query string.

8 queries covering: searchOnce, timeline, explain, rerank.

Depends on: schema definition bead
close_reason: Merged in PR #313 — search.ts migrated to Drizzle

title: Drizzle: define schema for all codemem tables
description:
Define Drizzle schema files for all 18 production tables. This is the foundation — every other migration bead depends on it.

Tables to define (grouped by subsystem):
- Core: sessions, memory_items, user_prompts, session_summaries, artifacts, usage_events
- Raw events: raw_events, raw_event_sessions, raw_event_flush_batches, raw_event_ingest_samples, raw_event_ingest_stats
- Sync: sync_device, sync_peers, sync_nonces, sync_attempts, sync_daemon_state, replication_ops, replication_cursors

Key decisions:
- Use text mode for ISO timestamps (not integer mode) to match existing schema
- Use text mode with JSON type for metadata_json, facts, concepts, files_read, files_modified
- Nullable columns must use .default(sql`null`) or just omit .notNull()
- Export inferred types (Select/Insert) for every table

Output: packages/core/src/schema.ts (or schema/ directory)
Also: drizzle.config.ts, install drizzle-orm + drizzle-kit as deps

No query migration in this bead — schema definition only.
close_reason: Completed

title: Drizzle: migrate store.ts (43 queries — remember, forget, get, recent, visibility, stats, raw events)
description:
Replace all 43 raw SQL queries in store.ts with Drizzle typed queries. This is the largest and highest-risk migration slice.

Subsections:
- remember() + provenance resolution (~8 queries)
- forget() (~2 queries)
- get() (~1 query)
- recent() + recentByKinds() (~4 queries)
- updateMemoryVisibility() (~3 queries)
- stats() (~5 queries)
- startSession/endSession (~3 queries)
- recordRawEvent + updateRawEventSessionMeta (~8 queries)
- updateIngestStats + pruneIngestSamples (~4 queries)
- buildMemoryPack via search (~delegated to search.ts bead)

This bead directly fixes audit findings:
- toJson(null) → typed JSON columns handle null correctly via schema
- endSession metadata merge → typed update with spread
- Record<string, unknown> eliminated → typed row results

Depends on: schema definition bead
close_reason: Completed

title: Release prep: publish codemem 0.20.0-alpha.1 to npm
description:
Prepare and publish the first TS-only npm release as codemem (unscoped).

Package naming decision:
- Canonical public package: codemem (unscoped)
- Do NOT publish @codemem/cli
- Do NOT publish internal workspaces (@codemem/core, @codemem/mcp-server, @codemem/viewer-server) yet
- @codemem scope is reserved for future public libraries only if needed later
- @kunickiaj/codemem is either retired or kept as a short-lived compatibility/deprecation shim only if any external OpenCode installs still depend on it

Rationale:
- codemem matches the project name, CLI binary name, and existing PyPI package name
- avoids install-name vs command-name mismatch
- avoids package naming change fatigue later
- plugin assets can now be rolled into the CLI package

Checklist:
1. Move CLI package from @codemem/cli to codemem (unscoped) — update package.json name
2. Decide whether @kunickiaj/codemem becomes a deprecation shim for 1-2 releases or stops publishing immediately
3. Set version to 0.20.0-alpha.1 across: root package.json (if still published), packages/cli/package.json, VERSION constant
4. Set private: false on CLI package
5. Set files: ["dist/", "README.md", "LICENSE", ".opencode/plugin/codemem.js", ".opencode/lib/compat.js"] or equivalent packaging strategy for plugin assets
6. Add description, repository, license, keywords, engines to CLI package.json
7. Ensure bin: { codemem: ./dist/index.js } is correct
8. Update setup command and docs to reference codemem as the install target
9. Add npm publish step to release workflow (or publish alpha manually)
10. npm publish codemem --tag next as 0.20.0-alpha.1
11. Dogfood: npm i -g codemem@next, run codemem setup, codemem serve, MCP tools, hooks
12. Fix what breaks, publish alpha.2+ if needed
13. Cut 0.20.0 stable once validated
close_reason: Published codemem@0.20.0-alpha.1 to npm

title: Port semantic/vector retrieval to TS; keep hybrid retrieval optional
description:
Python currently ships semantic/vector retrieval as part of the default pack/injection experience unless embeddings are explicitly disabled (CODEMEM_EMBEDDING_DISABLED=1). Hybrid retrieval was shadow-tested and did not show an improvement, so it is NOT required for TS parity and should not be ported.

Clarified scope:
- TABLE STAKES / deprecation blocker:
  1. semantic embeddings for memories
  2. vector storage/backfill
  3. semantic candidate retrieval used by pack/injection flows
  4. parity with current Python default behavior when embeddings are enabled
- EXPLICITLY OUT OF SCOPE:
  1. hybrid retrieval reranking
  2. hybrid shadow logging / evaluation framework

Background:
- Python defaults hybrid_retrieval_enabled = false, but semantic/vector infrastructure is active and pack generation still merges semantic candidates.
- We should not remove Python until TS can match the default semantic/vector pack quality, or we explicitly decide to change product expectations.

Recommended implementation split:
1. Port semantic/vector primitives first (embeddings, vector writes, semantic candidate lookup)
2. Wire semantic candidates into TS pack/injection flows
3. Validate recall/quality against Python on real cases
4. Do not port hybrid reranking unless future evidence shows a clear win.
close_reason: Completed. Semantic/vector parity shipped in TS: primitives (#297), semantic candidate merging in pack/MCP (#298), pack flow wiring in CLI/viewer (#375), inline vector writes on remember (#376), and embed backfill command (#377). Hybrid reranking remained intentionally out of scope.

title: Port config write + runtime effects to TS viewer-server
description:
The TS POST /api/config route exists (packages/viewer-server/src/routes/config.ts:105) but is a stub that returns 501 Not Implemented. Python's viewer_routes/config.py handles config writes with full runtime effects: toggling raw event gate, changing observer credentials, updating DB path, etc. (~640 LOC total).

Minimum needed for deprecation:
1. Write config keys to ~/.config/codemem/config.json on POST /api/config
2. Apply runtime effects for keys the serve process cares about (e.g. CODEMEM_RAW_EVENTS_GATE equivalent)
3. Return 200 with the updated config on success

Nice-to-have: observer credential re-probe on credential config changes (can defer to observer work).

Python source: codemem/viewer_routes/config.py, codemem/config.py (write paths)
close_reason: Completed in merged PR #295

title: Port export-memories / import-memories CLI commands to TS
description:
Python has export-memories and import-memories commands (~445 LOC in codemem/commands/import_export_cmds.py) needed for data migration and backup workflows.

export-memories: streams all memories (observations + summaries) as NDJSON to stdout or a file. Supports --project, --kind, --since filters.
import-memories: reads NDJSON from stdin or a file and inserts memories, skipping duplicates by stable ID.

These are P1 for deprecation: users need a way to migrate data between Python and TS instances, and as a backup tool before TS takes full DDL ownership.

Package: packages/cli/src/commands/export-memories.ts, packages/cli/src/commands/import-memories.ts
Core logic: packages/core/src/export-import.ts

Python source: codemem/commands/import_export_cmds.py
close_reason: Completed in merged PR #294

title: Port DB maintenance commands to TS CLI
description:
Python has a suite of DB maintenance commands missing from the TS CLI. These are operational-health commands needed before Python can be deprecated.

Missing commands (codemem/commands/maintenance_cmds.py + codemem/store/maintenance.py, ~1026 LOC total):
- codemem db init — create DB if missing (maps to assertSchemaReady already in TS, just needs CLI wiring)
- codemem raw-events-gate — enable/disable raw event ingestion (CODEMEM_RAW_EVENTS_GATE env / config key)
- codemem raw-events-retry — retry failed flush batches (mark failed batches as pending)
- codemem raw-events-status — show queue depth, last flush time, failure counts (already partially in /api/raw-events/status, needs CLI surface)
- codemem db vacuum — run VACUUM on the SQLite file
- codemem db prune-observations — deactivate low-signal observations (deactivate_low_signal_observations() in maintenance.py)
- codemem db prune-memories — deactivate low-signal memories (deactivate_low_signal_memories())
- codemem db backfill-tags — populate tag_text FTS from existing memories

Package: packages/cli/src/commands/db.ts (new file), packages/core/src/maintenance.ts (new file for store-level logic)

Python source: codemem/store/maintenance.py, codemem/commands/maintenance_cmds.py, codemem/commands/raw_events_cmds.py (378 LOC)
close_reason: Completed

title: Port /api/claude-hooks ingestion endpoint to TS
description:
Python's viewer_routes/raw_events.py handles POST /api/claude-hooks (257 LOC) and depends on claude_hooks.py (527 LOC) for payload mapping. Together ~1365 LOC unported.

The endpoint accepts Claude Code hook payloads (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop, etc.), calls build_raw_event_envelope_from_hook() to normalize them into raw event envelopes, and enqueues them for the raw event sweeper.

Key work:
1. Port claude_hooks.py — map_claude_hook_payload(), build_raw_event_envelope_from_hook(), build_ingest_payload_from_hook() to packages/core/src/claude-hooks.ts
2. Add POST /api/claude-hooks route in packages/viewer-server/src/routes/raw-events.ts (alongside existing POST /api/raw-events)
3. Handle skipped payloads (unsupported hook events) gracefully — return {inserted:0, skipped:1}
4. Wire session state tracking (_track_hook_session_state pattern — stateful per session_id dir)
5. Add tests in packages/core/src/claude-hooks.test.ts covering at least: PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop, and skip cases

This is required for Claude Code users who configure codemem as a hook endpoint via Claude hooks config. Without it, switching CODEMEM_RUNNER=npx silently drops all Claude hook events.

Python source: codemem/claude_hooks.py, codemem/commands/claude_hook_runtime_cmds.py, codemem/viewer_routes/raw_events.py lines 176-270
close_reason: Implemented — POST /api/claude-hooks ported to TS in PR #293

title: Complete TS plugin/CLI command contract and backend version parity
description: Plugin still shells out to backend commands and version checks that the TS CLI/versioning surface does not fully match. Scope: .opencode/plugin/codemem.js, packages/cli/src/index.ts, packages/core/src/index.ts, compat/update flows, tests/docs.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #292: TS CLI now supports plugin-required commands/flags and reports the released backend version.

title: Tighten integer parsing and query param validation to match Python
description: TypeScript still accepts Python-invalid numeric inputs in ID parsing and query helpers (e.g. Number()/parseInt permissiveness, unsafe integers, scientific notation, partial strings). Scope: packages/core/src/search.ts, packages/mcp-server/src/index.ts, packages/viewer-server/src/helpers.ts, related tests.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #289: strict integer parsing for IDs and query params, plus parity tests.

title: Restore missing Python search/filter knobs in TS
description: TS filter/ranking surface still lacks Python semantics such as personal_first, trust_bias, widen_shared_when_weak, ownership_scope, plus full normalization of filter values. Scope: packages/core/src/types.ts, packages/core/src/filters.ts, packages/core/src/search.ts, packages/mcp-server/src/index.ts.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #290: restored missing Python search/filter knobs, normalization semantics, and MCP passthrough.

title: Expand ~ paths and restore config-backed actor identity parity in TS
description: TypeScript still differs from Python in two critical semantics: (a) CODEMEM_DB / --db paths with ~/ are not expanded, so TS can open a different DB than Python; (b) actor_id / actor_display_name defaults read only env vars, not the codemem config file precedence used by Python. Scope: packages/core/src/db.ts, packages/core/src/store.ts, config helpers/tests.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #288: expand ~ DB paths, restore config-backed actor identity precedence, stable local device fallback, and parity tests.

title: fix: observer maxChars budget can starve user prompt to zero
description: observer-client.ts clips system prompt to maxChars then gives user prompt the remainder. If system prompt exceeds maxChars, user prompt gets ZERO budget.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #287 — reserve 25% min budget for user prompt

title: fix: deviceId randomUUID fallback breaks ownership checks
description: store.ts falls back to randomUUID() when sync_device table missing and CODEMEM_DEVICE_ID unset. Each MemoryStore instance gets different random ID, making memoryOwnedBySelf() always return false.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #287 — deterministic SHA-256 fallback instead of randomUUID

title: fix: consolidate divergent dedupeOrderedIds implementations
description: search.ts and mcp-server/index.ts each have own dedupeOrderedIds with different edge case handling. Consolidate into single core export.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #287 — deleted MCP copy, import dedupeOrderedIds from core

title: fix: pack text duplicate items in Timeline and Observations
description: pack.ts observationItems includes ALL non-summary results which is a superset of timelineItems. Both formatSection calls render same items, wasting LLM tokens.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #287 — exclude timeline IDs from observation items

title: Define TS-first user migration and CLI compatibility surface

This is required before flipping any default away from Python.

title: Implement TS migration safety contract for shared SQLite DB
description: Before TS can replace Python, implement the documented migration/coexistence safety contract: first-access backup, stronger schema validation (tables, columns, FTS/vector prerequisites), and hard failure on unsafe coexistence states. Current TS validation is too shallow and connect() does not perform the required backup-on-first-access.

title: Fix project scoping parity across TS MCP/search paths
description: TypeScript currently drops or weakens Python project-scoping behavior in parity-sensitive paths. Must port cwd-derived default project scoping, Python-compatible project matching, and strict project enforcement in memory_expand/explain(ids=...) so cross-project recall does not leak or widen silently. Audit refs: packages/mcp-server/src/index.ts, packages/core/src/search.ts, packages/core/src/filters.ts vs codemem/mcp_server.py, codemem/store/search.py, codemem/store/utils.py.

Must align actor_id defaults, personal workspace derivation, and ownership checks with Python so same-actor multi-device behavior and sharing do not drift. Existing related issue codemem-sbei covers visibility ownership; this issue tracks the broader provenance write-path parity across store writes.

Without these the viewer cannot fully replace Python-backed ingestion/settings flows.

title: Audit TypeScript migration for parity, correctness, and deprecation readiness
description: Deep validation pass for the TypeScript migration before deprecating the Python backend. Scope: 1) confirm parity/correctness against Python behavior, 2) identify bugs, inefficiencies, overengineering, and carried-over tech debt, 3) define user migration/deprecation path, 4) identify hardening work including schema/migration strategy (Drizzle). This audit should produce concrete follow-up implementation slices.

title: Port viewer server to Hono (serve existing frontend)

Includes all API routes ported from Python viewer_routes/. This is the final piece to eliminate the Python runtime dependency.
close_reason: PR #273: viewer server with all review fixes

title: Adopt Drizzle ORM for schema management when TS owns DDL
description:
Adopt Drizzle ORM to eliminate the Record<string, unknown> pattern that caused multiple data-loss bugs in replication and import paths. Currently every table has hand-written column lists in 4+ places (record, apply-update, apply-insert, export) that must stay in sync manually. They don't — we've shipped 3 data-loss bugs from column mismatches.

Drizzle provides:
- Single schema definition per table
- Typed query results (no more Record<string, unknown> casts)
- Compile-time errors when columns are missing from INSERT/UPDATE
- Generated migrations

This was originally P2/enhancement. Reprioritized to P1 after the replication audit found the Record<string, unknown> pattern to be the structural root cause of multiple shipped data-loss bugs.
close_reason: Completed for the high-risk migration goal in merged PR #404. Remaining replication/import loose payload handling was tightened with typed parsing helpers, and import mapping correctness (including deleted/inactive state preservation) was fixed. Lower-priority follow-ups can continue independently.

comments.author: Adam Kunicki

Focus remaining work on typed schema-driven mapping for packages/core/src/sync-replication.ts and packages/core/src/export-import.ts (including active-state correctness tracked by codemem-c272).
