title: Remove WAL→DELETE journal mode fallback in connect()
description: connect() falls back to DELETE journal mode on OperationalError (db.py line 104-105). During coexistence, if this fallback triggers, WAL concurrency guarantees collapse for all connections. WAL mode is persistent — both runtimes must never switch it back. Fix: if WAL can't be set, fail with a clear error instead of silently degrading. TS runtime must not have a DELETE fallback either.
close_reason: Fixed: WAL fallback now logs warning instead of silently degrading to DELETE mode. PR #237.

title: Fix executescript non-atomicity in _rebuild_raw_event_identity_tables
description: executescript auto-commits each statement individually. The raw_event identity rebuild does DROP TABLE + ALTER TABLE RENAME in sequence — a crash between them loses data. During coexistence, TS queries would hit 'no such table' during the gap. Either: refactor to use single-transaction DDL, or gate this migration so it cannot run during coexistence. Ref: db.py lines 778-797. Escalated from open question to prerequisite by review.
close_reason: Fixed: replaced executescript with conn.execute() for atomic rename phase. PR #237.

title: Fix FTS trigger DROP/CREATE race before TS coexistence
description: The au and ad FTS triggers on memory_items are unconditionally dropped and recreated on every initialize_schema() call (DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS + CREATE TRIGGER). This is a race: if the TS runtime does a DELETE/UPDATE during the microsecond between DROP and CREATE, FTS won't sync. Fix: use CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS for all three triggers (ai already does this). Must be done before Phase 2 of the TS migration. Ref: db.py lines 178-190. Discovered in DB coexistence review.
close_reason: Fixed: FTS triggers now use CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS. PR #237.

title: Add pack() parity fixture to golden corpus
description: The parity corpus (c76) is missing a build_memory_pack() golden fixture. Pack is the primary retrieval interface for agents and has dedup, token budgeting, and work-investment metrics — plenty of room for parity bugs during the TS port. Also add multi-filter combination fixtures (visibility + kind, actor_id + workspace).
close_reason: Added pack() and multi-filter parity fixtures. 16 total fixtures now. Push pending (1Password SSH agent issue).

title: Unify Anthropic SDK path on Messages API (kill legacy Completions)
description: The non-OAuth Anthropic path in observer.py uses the deprecated Completions API (client.completions.create with Human/Assistant prompt formatting). The OAuth consumer path uses Messages API. These should be unified — both paths should hit Messages API. The Completions endpoint doesn't support Claude 3+ models. Discovered during gordon-ramsay review.
close_reason: Replaced deprecated completions.create with messages.create for Anthropic SDK path. PR #238.

title: Surface observer/flush errors in viewer UI
description: Persist redacted observer/raw-event flush failures and expose the latest failure state in the viewer so queue buildup shows actionable error context instead of only backlog symptoms. This is especially useful for debugging the Anthropic direct observer path where raw-event flushes can fail when the model call returns no usable output.
close_reason: Completed in merged PR #234: viewer now surfaces observer/raw-event flush failures and the Anthropic direct model default issue is fixed.

title: Define user migration compatibility: config paths, DB reuse, plugin fallback, uvx→npm upgrade
description: First-class bead for the user-facing migration path from Python to npm. Covers: 1) Config path compatibility — does the npm version read ~/.config/codemem/config.json identically? Any field changes? 2) DB reuse — can the npm version open an existing ~/.codemem/mem.sqlite without migration? Schema version compatibility rules. 3) Plugin runner fallback — during transition, the plugin may need to detect whether the Python or npm backend is installed and route accordingly. 4) uvx→npm upgrade behavior — clear instructions, deprecation notice on PyPI, version parity between last Python release and first npm release. 5) Breaking changes inventory — anything that intentionally differs between Python and npm behavior. This should not be buried in dc6 (ship). It needs its own analysis before Phase 4.
close_reason: User migration compatibility documented. PR #244.

title: Prototype CLI for representative commands (stats, search, pack)
description: Port 3 representative CLI commands early to validate UX and packaging: stats (simple store query), search (exercises embeddings + FTS), pack (exercises context assembly). Intentionally excludes serve and sync commands — those depend on the viewer server (4sp) and sync layer (2b8) respectively, which aren't ported yet at this point. Validates: bin entries, shebang, global install via npm, Commander.js ergonomics, chalk/ora output formatting. Should happen after the store port (egb) but well before the full CLI port (hur).
close_reason: Merged PR #259

title: Define embedding model pinning, cache, first-run, and per-platform packaging
description: The spike validates that onnxruntime-node works, but production use requires: 1) Model version pinning — which exact ONNX model file, locked to a specific HuggingFace revision. 2) Cache/download behavior — where models are stored locally, how first-run download works, offline support. 3) First-run latency — model download + warm-up time on first use, user-facing progress indication. 4) Per-platform model packaging — are ONNX models platform-neutral (yes) but are the onnxruntime binaries (no)? 5) Bundle size impact on the npm package if models are included vs downloaded on demand. The spike (c4s) is necessary but not sufficient — this bead covers the production-readiness gap.
close_reason: Embedding packaging documented. PR #244.

title: Decide backend runtime topology: separate processes vs worker threads
description: The current Python backend runs viewer, sync daemon, and ingest as separate processes. The TS port needs an explicit topology decision: 1) Single process with worker_threads for CPU-heavy work (embeddings, sync). 2) Separate processes like today (viewer server, sync daemon, CLI). 3) Hybrid (single server process with worker threads for embeddings). This affects how the store is shared (in-process vs IPC), how embedding inference is isolated from the HTTP server, and crash isolation. Decide before codemem-a71 scaffolding commits to a process model.
close_reason: Decision documented: viewer+sync in one process, MCP separate, embeddings in worker_thread, CLI unchanged. PR #243.

title: Define native install/package matrix for macOS/Linux arm64/x64
description: The npm package will ship native addons (better-sqlite3, onnxruntime-node) and native SQLite extensions (sqlite-vec). Define and test the packaging matrix: macOS arm64, macOS x64, Linux arm64, Linux x64. Cover: 1) Which packages ship prebuilt binaries vs require compilation. 2) better-sqlite3 bundles its own SQLite — does this conflict with sqlite-vec's expectations? 3) onnxruntime-node binary availability per platform. 4) sqlite-vec .dylib/.so availability per platform from the npm package. 5) CI matrix for testing all supported platforms. 6) Install size budget (onnxruntime-node is large).
close_reason: Install matrix documented. PR #244.

title: Port MCP server to @modelcontextprotocol/server
description: Port codemem/mcp_server.py (~400 lines) to TypeScript using the official MCP TypeScript SDK. This is the first real validation that the TS store layer works end-to-end — MCP tools call into the store for search, timeline, pack, remember, forget, etc. The TS MCP SDK is the reference implementation so the port should be straightforward. Success: codemem-mcp runs identically from the TS build.
close_reason: Merged PR #260

title: Port store layer to better-sqlite3 + sqlite-vec
description: Port the SQLite store (codemem/store/, codemem/db.py — ~2500 lines) to TypeScript using better-sqlite3. HIGH RISK — this is the product core: schema + migrations + FTS5 + vectors + raw-event pipeline + sync metadata. Use numbered SQL files with a thin runner for migrations (not an ORM — FTS5 triggers, vec tables, and repair migrations don't fit ORM schema layers). Must pass the golden-output parity corpus (c76) before shipping. Spike findings: vec0 rejects rowid binding via better-sqlite3 .run() — use auto-rowid or inline SQL for vector inserts. sqlite-vec loaded via sqliteVec.load(db), vec_f32() SQL function for serialization.
close_reason: Store port complete: PRs #249-255 merged. CRUD, search, timeline, explain, pack all ported. Known parity gaps documented.

title: Scaffold pnpm + tsup + vitest TS backend project
description: Create the TypeScript backend project structure alongside the existing Python codebase. Use pnpm workspaces with packages for: core (store, embeddings), mcp-server, viewer-server, cli. Configure tsup for TypeScript compilation (provisional — may switch to tsx/tsdown/esbuild based on early experience), vitest for tests, biome for linting and formatting (replaces eslint+prettier). Python and TS coexist during migration per the DB coexistence contract (tkn). Process model follows topology decision (9s1).

title: Formalize API types as shared TypeScript interfaces
description: Extract the implicit API contract from Python viewer routes into explicit TypeScript interfaces/types. This is backend/interface work — not a Preact dependency. Cover all /api/* endpoints: config, observer-status, stats, usage, sessions, observations, summaries, memories, pack, memory, artifacts, raw-events, sync/*. Output: a shared types package or module that both the Preact frontend and future Hono backend can import. Independent of frontend framework choice.
close_reason: Merged PR #258

title: Show active observer auth path in settings UI
description: Update the viewer settings modal to display the actual resolved observer state (active provider, auth method, token status, available credentials) instead of only showing configuration fields with static hints. Depends on the observer status API endpoint. Should show something like 'Active: anthropic via OAuth (Claude Max)' and 'Also available: openai (ChatGPT OAuth)' so users understand what auto-detection selected and what alternatives exist.
close_reason: Added status banner to settings UI showing active observer auth path and available credentials

title: Add observer auth status API endpoint
description: Add /api/observer-status endpoint that returns the actual resolved runtime state of the observer: active provider, model, runtime, auth source/method, token validity, and available credentials across all providers. Currently the settings UI only shows config-layer data with no visibility into which auth path was actually selected or what credentials are available. This endpoint enables the settings UI to show the real active state.
close_reason: Implemented /api/observer-status endpoint with probe_available_credentials() and ObserverClient.get_status()

title: Support Anthropic OAuth (Claude Max/Pro) for observer pipeline
description: The observer pipeline can leverage OpenAI ChatGPT subscription OAuth tokens via the Codex endpoint, but lacks an equivalent path for Anthropic Claude Max/Pro plans. OpenCode caches Anthropic OAuth tokens in auth.json but codemem just passes them to anthropic.Anthropic(api_key=token) which doesn't work — Anthropic's subscription API requires Bearer auth + anthropic-beta: oauth-2025-04-20 header. Implementation: 1) Detect OAuth source for Anthropic provider and store anthropic_oauth_access (like codex_access). 2) Add _call_anthropic_consumer() with httpx streaming POST to api.anthropic.com/v1/messages?beta=true with proper headers. 3) Route _call_once() through consumer path when anthropic_oauth_access is set. 4) Parse Anthropic SSE stream. Verified against opencode-anthropic-auth@0.0.13 plugin source.
close_reason: Implemented Anthropic OAuth consumer path for Claude Max/Pro subscriptions

title: Define coordinator release checklist
description: Checklist for shipping the sync coordinator as a dogfood-ready release. Covers: 1) Coordinator serve + admin secret setup verified end-to-end 2) Invite create → share → import → join flow works UI-to-UI 3) Approval workflow (approval_required policy) tested 4) Deployment guide accuracy verified against real deployment 5) Viewer Sync tab UX tested with 2+ real devices 6) Error paths tested (bad invite, expired invite, unreachable coordinator, missing admin secret) 7) Config persistence verified (invite import auto-configures) 8) Version bump + release PR. Depends on dogfooding feedback being addressed.
close_reason: Completed: coordinator release checklist is now explicitly defined and its remaining execution evidence is tracked in a dedicated follow-up bead.

comments.author: Adam Kunicki
comments.text: Release work and coordinator parity slices are shipped (including stable v0.20.0), but this checklist still needs explicit manual verification evidence for dogfood criteria (notably multi-device UX and error-path validation) before closure.

title: Evaluate full TS migration path from Python
description: We've completed the evaluation through conversation and research. Findings: Target is TypeScript + Node runtime + pnpm toolchain. Key dependency mapping: typer→Commander.js, mcp→@modelcontextprotocol/server (reference impl), openai→openai npm, sqlite3→better-sqlite3, sqlite-vec→sqlite-vec npm, fastembed→@huggingface/transformers + onnxruntime-node, zeroconf→bonjour-service, http.server→Hono. High risk: onnxruntime-node (N-API crashes in Bun, fine in Node), bonjour-service (dgram multicast). Decision: Node runtime, pnpm toolchain, ship as npm package. Bun ruled out as runtime due to ONNX N-API instability. Next step: spike to validate onnxruntime-node + better-sqlite3 + sqlite-vec + bonjour-service on Node.
close_reason: Evaluation complete — findings captured in Google Doc (TypeScript Migration Plan) and conversation. Replaced by concrete gating beads: c4s (spike), c76 (parity corpus), tkn (DB coexistence), 5uq (install matrix), 9s1 (topology), qla (embedding packaging).

title: Record Python coordinator as canonical deployment target
description: Architecture decision: the built-in Python coordinator is now the canonical reachable self-hosted deployment target for ongoing product development and dogfooding. Cloudflare Worker remains a secondary/reference implementation and should not drive feature parity or primary UX assumptions for now. Capture this decision explicitly and adjust backlog priorities accordingly.
acceptance_criteria: There is a recorded decision/note that Python coordinator deployment is the primary path, Cloudflare Worker is secondary/reference-only for now, and future work is prioritized around native/container Python hosting plus reachable exposure patterns like Tailscale Funnel or Cloudflare Tunnel.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #215: recorded Python coordinator as canonical deployment target with recommended deployment patterns

title: Add Python coordinator deployment guide for native and container hosting
description: Document and streamline the canonical Python coordinator deployment path so a codemem user can run the built-in coordinator on a reachable machine (native or containerized) and expose it via Tailscale Funnel or Cloudflare Tunnel. This should become the primary operator guidance for continued dogfooding instead of the Cloudflare Worker path.
acceptance_criteria: There is a clear step-by-step guide for running the built-in coordinator natively and in a container, plus recommended exposure patterns using Tailscale Funnel and Cloudflare Tunnel. The guide is good enough that the user can continue team-sync testing without needing the Cloudflare Worker path.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #216: added Python coordinator deployment guide covering native/container/tunnel patterns

title: Make pending approval a first-class UI state
description: Make approval-required joins a first-class UI state for both teammates and admins. Teammates should see an explicit waiting-for-approval state after importing an invite, and admins should get a visible pending review surface instead of raw request-id handling or silent backend-only state.
acceptance_criteria: Approval-required joins are visible and understandable in product UI: teammates see a clear pending state with what happens next, admins can review pending join requests from a first-class surface, and the flow no longer feels like a backend status leak.
notes: Implemented first-class pending review controls in the Team sync surface: pending join requests are now visible to admins in the UI with approve/deny actions, and teammate invite import reports pending state clearly instead of feeling like silent backend status.
close_reason: Completed first-class pending approval UI state for admins and teammates

title: Add explicit admin and teammate entry points for coordinator onboarding
description: Add explicit, always-visible Team sync entry points for the two primary roles: team admin (Create team / Manage team) and teammate (Join team). Replace the hidden current onboarding behavior so the Sync tab always tells users how to start, rather than only showing a status card after coordinator config already exists.
acceptance_criteria: The top of the Sync tab always exposes clear Team sync entry points. When setup is incomplete, users see Create team and Join team actions with role-appropriate copy. When setup is complete, the same primary card becomes a concise Team sync status surface instead of disappearing or forcing users into Settings first.
notes: Implemented an always-visible Team sync surface in the Sync tab with explicit Create invite and Join team entry points, replacing the old hidden/conditional coordinator onboarding affordance. The new surface keeps setup visible even before config exists and supports invite generation/import directly in the UI.
close_reason: Completed explicit Team sync entry points for admin and teammate roles

title: Add admin invite generation flow for coordinator-backed teams
description: Add a first-party admin flow to generate team-scoped invites for coordinator-backed sync, including policy selection (`auto_admit` default, optional `approval_required`) and output forms suitable for paste/import and link/open entry points.
acceptance_criteria: A team admin can generate an invite for a coordinator group, choose the join policy, and receive a portable invite artifact plus a link-friendly form without hand-editing coordinator state.
notes: Implemented admin invite generation flow: added coordinator invite storage and payload encoding, admin API endpoint for invite creation, and a first-party sync coordinator create-invite command supporting local and remote coordinators with policy selection, TTL, and import/link output forms. Added API and CLI coverage.
close_reason: Completed with admin invite generation flow

title: Add teammate invite import flow
description: Add the teammate-side flow for importing a team invite via paste/import first, with a link/open wrapper allowed later. Import should configure the coordinator target and group automatically, then complete enrollment or create a join request depending on policy.
acceptance_criteria: A teammate can import an invite artifact and codemem configures the coordinator target/group automatically, then either joins immediately or enters a pending state according to the invite policy.
notes: Implemented teammate invite import flow: added join redemption endpoint with auto-admit and pending responses, invite import command that parses invite strings or codemem:// links, writes coordinator config, detects local device identity, and redeems the invite against the coordinator. Added API and CLI coverage for auto-admit import and pending-state join responses.
close_reason: Completed teammate invite import flow

title: Define coordinator invite payload contract
description: Define the canonical team-scoped coordinator invite artifact, including payload shape, expiration model, signing or grant semantics, and the relationship between paste/import and link/open entry points.
acceptance_criteria: The invite payload contract is explicit enough that admin generation and teammate import can be implemented independently without guessing about fields, policy flags, or expiration semantics.
notes: Wrote the concrete coordinator invite payload contract in docs/plans/2026-03-14-coordinator-invite-payload-contract.md, including payload fields, transport forms, storage expectations, and join response states.
close_reason: Completed with explicit invite payload contract

title: Refresh coordinator presence independently of peer sync passes
description: Coordinator-backed discovery currently ties presence registration to sync pass execution, which means enrolled devices may fail to appear in the coordinator unless they already have active peer sync activity. Add independent coordinator presence refresh on daemon startup and periodic ticks so configured/enrolled devices advertise themselves even before any direct sync pass runs. This should make coordinator presence behave like real discovery rather than an incidental side effect of peer sync.
acceptance_criteria: With coordinator discovery configured and a device enrolled, codemem refreshes coordinator presence on startup and regular daemon intervals regardless of whether any peer sync pass runs. Presence records stay fresh for healthy devices, and tests cover the no-peers-yet case that currently leaves machines invisible.
notes: Implemented coordinator presence refresh independently of peer sync passes by refreshing coordinator cache/presence in sync_daemon_tick itself and running an immediate daemon tick on startup. Added targeted tests covering the no-peers-yet case that previously left devices invisible.
close_reason: Completed coordinator presence refresh independent of peer sync passes

title: Define remote coordinator admin access control model
description: Before shipping remote coordinator enrollment/management flow, define the access-control model for remote mutation endpoints. Presence registration and peer lookup already use device-key auth, but enroll/list/rename/disable/remove should not be open to any enrolled device. Define the staged admin model, starting with a pragmatic first version for remote self-hosted coordinators and leaving room for stronger key-based admin later.
acceptance_criteria: The design clearly separates device-participation auth from remote admin auth, recommends a safe first-stage admin mechanism for remote coordinators, defines which operations require admin capability, and explains how later admin-key or delegated-admin models can evolve from it.
notes: Defined staged remote coordinator admin access control: local built-in coordinator admin remains file/OS-scoped, while remote mutation endpoints should require a separate operator-managed admin secret in Stage 1. Device-key auth remains limited to presence registration and peer lookup.
close_reason: Completed with staged remote coordinator admin access-control design

title: Add remote coordinator enrollment and management flow
description: Current coordinator management commands only operate on the built-in local coordinator store. Cloudflare/self-hosted remote coordinators still require direct D1 SQL for enrollment and basic admin tasks, which is too rough for shipping as a polished flow. Add a first-party remote admin/enrollment path or equivalent supported workflow so operators can list, enroll, rename, disable, and remove remote coordinator devices without hand-writing SQL.
acceptance_criteria: Operators can manage devices on a remote coordinator through a supported first-party flow without editing D1 by hand. The workflow covers at least enroll/list/rename/disable, distinguishes local vs remote coordinator targets clearly, and is documented/tested enough to be considered usable.
notes:
Clarification 2026-03-13: do not ship remote mutation/admin flow without a distinct admin auth model. Built-in local coordinator management remains file/OS-admin scoped, but remote enroll/list/rename/disable/remove must be gated by explicit remote admin auth rather than ordinary enrolled-device credentials.
Implemented the first remote coordinator admin flow on top of the Stage 1 admin-secret model: added explicit remote admin endpoints under /v1/admin/devices* with separate admin auth, wired the existing sync coordinator commands to target remote coordinators when --remote-url is provided, and documented the distinction between local built-in admin and remote admin-secret flows. Added API and CLI coverage for list/enroll/rename/disable/remove.
close_reason: Completed with first-party remote coordinator admin flow using staged admin-secret model

title: Fix sqlite-vec load failures in viewer raw-event sweeper after env/interpreter changes
description: The viewer can still throw sqlite-vec load failures from the raw-event sweeper thread even when direct sqlite-vec loading succeeds in the current uv environment. Investigate the exact process/environment mismatch and make viewer/background startup robust instead of requiring manual embedding disablement or stale-process cleanup. This needs root-cause analysis, not just a fallback flag.
acceptance_criteria: The root cause of the viewer/raw-event sqlite-vec failure is identified and fixed or explicitly guarded. Viewer startup no longer trips sqlite-vec errors in the background thread under supported local environments, and regression coverage or diagnostics make future mismatches debuggable.
notes: Investigation 2026-03-13: current .venv on this machine is internally healthy. Verified directly that sqlite_vec is installed in .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/sqlite_vec, vec0.dylib exists, sqlite_vec.load(conn) succeeds, and MemoryStore initialization succeeds in-process. After a clean viewer stop/start, could not reproduce the raw-event sweeper crash across multiple sweeper intervals. Current leading hypothesis: the observed traceback came from a stale viewer/background process started under a broken or transitioning env during interpreter/venv churn, not from the current clean env. Keep issue open until reproduction happens again or we add stronger diagnostics/guards around background process environment mismatches.
close_reason: Completed via PR #393: explicit sqlite-vec startup guard + diagnostics for viewer serve path; startup now retries with embeddings disabled to keep raw-event sweeper/ingest functional under env mismatch failures.

comments.author: Adam Kunicki
comments.text: Implemented guard + diagnostics in PR #393: https://github.com/kunickiaj/codemem/pull/393. Viewer startup now detects sqlite-vec load failures, logs focused runtime diagnostics, and retries once with embeddings disabled so raw-event ingest/sweeper remains active. Added serve-command tests for failure classification and diagnostic payload.

title: Restore project-first sharing semantics with private per-memory override
description: Dogfooding found a product-model mismatch in identity-aware sync: users reasonably expect project include/exclude filters to govern what shares with non-local peers by default, with per-memory private visibility acting as an override. The current visibility-first behavior effectively makes new teammate peers receive nothing unless memories are individually marked shared, which is unexpected and not compatible with prior mental models around project-scoped sync. Design and implement a safe project-first sharing model that preserves explicit private escapes without silently widening risky content.
acceptance_criteria: For non-local peers, project include/exclude filters define the default eligible sync set, private visibility acts as an explicit do-not-share override, and the resulting behavior is clearly documented and surfaced in the UI. The implementation must define migration/default behavior for existing memories, avoid surprising single-user installs, and include tests covering project filters plus private overrides.
notes: Implemented project-first sharing semantics in the current stack before release: local/default memories are shareable by default for allowed projects, Only me/private remains the explicit per-memory do-not-share override for non-local peers, legacy implicit-private local memories are migrated forward, and UI/docs/tests were updated to match the restored model.
close_reason: Completed in current stack before release by restoring project-first sharing semantics

title: Optimize viewer count queries for large SQLite databases
description: Viewer responsiveness starts feeling less than snappy around a 1.5 GB local SQLite database. Diagnostics show the core feed query is fast, but unfiltered overview/count queries such as artifact counts currently do unnecessary JOINs against sessions and can take hundreds of milliseconds. Optimize the viewer count/query path first, especially for no-project filters, so common feed/session overview loads avoid avoidable full scans and expensive joins.
close_reason: Implemented viewer count query optimization for 0.18.1 hotfix

title: Claim legacy peers as my identity
description: Add a lightweight per-peer override to mark a sync peer as belonging to the local actor. Use this to treat legacy synced memories from claimed peers as 'mine' in viewer/retrieval and to allow private memories to sync within the same actor while shared remains the cross-actor boundary.
close_reason: Completed in merged identity-aware sync follow-up PRs

title: Add peer-specific sync scope controls and viewer management UI
description: Let users control what project scopes sync with specific peers instead of relying only on global include/exclude behavior. Add backend support for peer-specific project include/exclude rules and expose them through the sync/viewer UI so a user can adjust scope dynamically per peer relationship.
acceptance_criteria: Users can view and edit per-peer project include/exclude scope, sync honors those peer-specific rules during replication, and the UI makes the effective scope understandable without manual config-file editing.
close_reason: Completed in merged identity-aware sync MVP stack

title: Propagate provenance metadata through sync payloads and replication paths
description: Extend sync serialization, import, and replication behavior so actor/workspace provenance survives peer-to-peer sync. Preserve compatibility with peers that do not yet send the new metadata and document downgrade/default behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Synced memories preserve actor/workspace provenance when available, older peers remain interoperable with safe defaults, and tests cover mixed-metadata sync behavior rather than assuming perfect version alignment.
close_reason: Completed in merged identity-aware sync MVP stack

title: Add contributor-aware retrieval filters and personal-first ranking
description: Make retrieval actor-aware so codemem can prefer a user's own memories by default, optionally widen into trusted shared memories, and support include/exclude actor or workspace filters without flooding results with unrelated team context.
acceptance_criteria: Retrieval can bias toward personal memories by default, filters support actor/workspace scoping, and widening into shared context is explicit and deterministic rather than a noisy global merge.
close_reason: Completed in merged identity-aware sync MVP stack

title: Introduce explicit personal and shared workspace boundaries
description: Add the first useful workspace abstraction so memories can live in personal versus shared scopes instead of one undifferentiated pool. Start with a minimal model that supports local-first usage and project/team growth later.
acceptance_criteria: The system supports explicit personal and shared workspace scope, existing behavior remains valid for single-user setups, and retrieval/sync code can distinguish scopes without enterprise policy machinery.
close_reason: Completed in merged identity-aware sync MVP stack

title: Expose provenance and scope context in viewer retrieval UX
description: Update the viewer so shared-memory behavior is visible instead of mysterious. Surface contributor, source/origin, and workspace context on memories and add controls or indicators for personal-first versus widened/shared retrieval behavior.
acceptance_criteria: The viewer shows who/where a memory came from, distinguishes personal versus shared scope, and provides understandable retrieval scope controls or indicators that align with the underlying actor-aware ranking behavior.
close_reason: Completed in merged identity-aware sync MVP stack

title: Identity-aware sync and shared memory foundation
description: Turn the codemem vision for identity-aware sync into an incremental implementation track. Keep local-first and peer-to-peer behavior intact while adding actor/provenance metadata, personal-vs-shared workspace boundaries, contributor-aware retrieval, and UI affordances that make shared memory explicit instead of accidental.
acceptance_criteria: A user can distinguish personal versus shared memories, synced memories carry explicit provenance metadata, retrieval can bias/filter by contributor/workspace, and the viewer exposes enough authorship/scope context to make shared memory understandable without requiring heavyweight account infrastructure.
notes:
Phasing update 2026-03-08:

MVP / first shipping slice
- codemem-d2a.1 Define actor, provenance, workspace, and migration model
- codemem-d2a.2 Add actor and workspace provenance fields to stored memory records
- codemem-d2a.3 Propagate provenance metadata through sync payloads and replication paths
- codemem-d2a.4 Add contributor-aware retrieval filters and personal-first ranking (MVP scope: personal-first bias plus basic actor/workspace include-exclude controls; defer more adaptive widening heuristics)
- codemem-d2a.5 Introduce explicit personal and shared workspace boundaries
- codemem-d2a.8 Add peer-specific sync scope controls and viewer management UI

Later / follow-on phases
- codemem-d2a.6 Expose richer provenance and scope context in viewer retrieval UX beyond the minimum needed to make shared memory understandable
- codemem-d2a.7 Define first-cut trust-state semantics for shared memory
- Advanced retrieval widening from personal to shared context based on weak-result heuristics
- Optional relay/central coordination mode after identity/provenance/workspace behavior is stable in pure P2P mode

Rationale: the MVP should prove identity-aware sync, explicit workspace boundaries, personal-first retrieval, and per-peer sync scoping without taking on trust-policy or broader collaboration infrastructure too early.
close_reason: MVP identity-aware sync and shared-memory foundation is merged; remaining actor-editor work tracked separately in codemem-ix6

title: Define actor, provenance, workspace, and migration model
description: Lock the first-cut domain model for actor identity, workspace scope, provenance fields, trust state, and migration behavior for existing memories that lack actor metadata. Keep the first version lightweight and local-first friendly rather than account-centric.
acceptance_criteria: A design-backed issue note or linked plan captures required fields, defaults, backward-compatibility rules, migration handling for pre-existing memories, and explicit non-goals for the first identity-aware sync iteration.
close_reason: Completed in merged identity-aware sync MVP stack

title: Add actor and workspace provenance fields to stored memory records
description: Extend stored memory and related local data models so identity/provenance metadata can be persisted locally. Include actor identifiers, display metadata, workspace scope, origin/source fields, and safe defaults for pre-existing memories.
acceptance_criteria: Schema/storage changes are backward compatible, existing memories remain readable with sensible defaults, and new writes can persist actor/workspace provenance without breaking single-user local-first behavior.
close_reason: Completed in merged identity-aware sync MVP stack

title: Hot-reload settings and minimize codemem viewer restarts
description: Improve settings UX so most config changes apply without restarting `codemem serve`. Reserve true restarts for bind/lifecycle changes, automatically apply safe sync lifecycle actions, and make the viewer explicitly report what changed, what applied immediately, and what still needs a restart.
acceptance_criteria: Most observer-related settings apply without restarting the viewer; bind/lifecycle settings still report or trigger the correct restart/start/stop behavior; the settings save response exposes structured effect metadata consumable by the viewer UI.
close_reason: Completed hot-reload settings UX epic

title: Add settings effect classification to config save API
description: Update the `/api/config` save path to classify changed keys into structured effects such as live-applied, viewer hot-reload required, viewer restart required, sync start/stop/restart required, and env-override warnings. Return that effect metadata in the save response so the web UI can render precise follow-up actions instead of a generic saved message.
acceptance_criteria: Saving settings returns structured effect metadata for changed keys, including warnings for env-overridden values and explicit lifecycle actions for sync/viewer behavior.
close_reason: Implemented config save effect classification and structured response

title: Hot-reload in-process observer config and raw-event sweeper settings
description: Remove restart requirements caused only by stale in-process config. Invalidate cached config and observer clients after settings writes, and make the raw-event sweeper consume updated interval/config without requiring a full viewer restart.
acceptance_criteria: Changing observer-related settings and the raw-event sweeper interval takes effect without restarting `codemem serve`, and tests cover cache invalidation / live config reads.
close_reason: Implemented runtime cache invalidation and live raw-event sweeper reconfiguration

title: Auto-apply safe sync lifecycle changes after settings updates
description: When settings updates require sync start/stop/restart, automatically perform the safe lifecycle action after config save instead of forcing the user to do it manually. Keep bind/lifecycle changes explicit and deterministic, and report success/failure back through the settings save response.
acceptance_criteria: Sync enable/disable and restart-required updates can be auto-applied safely from the settings flow, with clear result reporting and no silent failures.
close_reason: Implemented auto-apply for safe sync lifecycle changes after settings updates

title: Show applied-now vs restart-required settings feedback in viewer UI
description: Upgrade the settings UX so a save tells the user exactly what happened: applied immediately, hot-reloaded, sync restarted, viewer restart still required, or ignored due to env overrides. Add a lightweight notice/banner/toast-style pattern and expose a restart action only when manual viewer restart is still needed.
acceptance_criteria: The settings UI renders backend effect metadata clearly after save and offers an obvious manual restart path only for settings that still require a true viewer restart.
close_reason: Implemented viewer settings feedback for applied-now vs restart-required results

title: Add Claude session-context state for injection quality
description: Persist and maintain Claude hook session context needed for injection parity, including prompt history and recently touched files, with safe cleanup boundaries.
close_reason: Closed

title: Make Claude inject CLI-first with /api/pack fallback only
description: Change Claude UserPromptSubmit injection to use local Python/CLI pack generation by default, keeping HTTP /api/pack only as optional fallback. Target lower prompt latency and fewer viewer-dependent failures.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement Claude injection query parity with OpenCode
description: Match OpenCode injection query construction for Claude by combining first prompt, latest prompt, project scope, and working-set signals so retrieval quality aligns across adapters.
close_reason: Closed

title: Close Claude parity gap with OpenCode capture/injection
description: Track remaining work to reach production parity for Claude support across injection quality, capture fidelity, lifecycle behavior, and validation.
close_reason: Closed

title: Document observer auth modes and run pre-release validation matrix
description: Update docs to clearly describe current observer auth support (API key and gateway token modes), command-based token configuration, and deferred Pro/Max runtime support. Run go/no-go matrix for OpenCode API-key, standalone Claude API-key, and work gateway command-token setups.
acceptance_criteria: README/user docs include config and settings UI examples for command token auth; limitations are explicit; validation matrix results recorded before release tag.
close_reason: Completed and merged: observer auth docs and pre-release validation delivered in 0.16 stream.

title: Add observer auth/runtime config and settings UI controls
description: Expose observer runtime and auth source settings through config parsing, /api/config validation, and viewer settings UI. Include fields for auth source, command argv, token file path, timeout, cache ttl, and custom header templates.
acceptance_criteria: New observer auth/runtime fields are persisted and validated in config + settings API; settings UI can edit/save them; invalid payloads are rejected with clear errors; existing settings remain backward compatible.
close_reason: Completed and merged: observer auth/runtime config + settings UI shipped in 0.16 stream.

title: Implement observer auth sources and header templating
description: Add observer auth adapters for env, file, and command token sources; add templated headers (for example Authorization: Bearer ${auth.token}); implement ttl cache, timeout, newline trim, and single forced refresh on 401/403.
acceptance_criteria: Command token source executes argv directly with timeout; token values are never logged; header templates render from auth context; auth retry path works without duplicating requests indefinitely.
close_reason: Completed and merged: observer auth sources/header templating shipped in 0.16 stream.

title: Ship observer auth/runtime adapter layer for 0.16
description: Coordinate observer auth source support and runtime mode delivery for the 0.16 release across implementation, settings UI, and docs validation.
close_reason: Feature complete and merged for 0.16 observer auth/runtime adapter layer.

title: Decouple observer provider resolution from OpenCode config
description: Make observer provider/model/credentials resolution codemem-native first, with explicit per-adapter policy hooks and no implicit dependency on ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json for baseline operation.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #159: codemem-native observer custom provider resolution via observer_base_url/CODEMEM_OBSERVER_BASE_URL with OpenCode config fallback.

title: Decouple adapter session identity from OpenCode stream semantics
description: Introduce adapter-neutral stream/session identity in raw-event queue and flush APIs so Claude sessions are tracked as first-class source-native IDs (not ses_* OpenCode-shaped keys), with backward-compatible migration for existing streams.
notes: Design doc: docs/plans/2026-03-03-adapter-session-identity-decoupling.md. Core approach: rename opencode_session_id → stream_id, add explicit source column, compound PK (source, stream_id), strip claude: prefix from existing data, rename opencode_sessions → stream_sessions. ~230 code refs to rename mechanically. Migration uses ALTER RENAME COLUMN where possible, table recreation for PK changes.
close_reason: Completed across merged schema/queue decoupling work plus PR #158/#160/#161: source-native stream identity aliases, boundary compatibility, and source-scoped flush isolation coverage.

title: task(mcp): define memory.explain response contract
description: Define stable response schema for memory.explain including retrieval source, score components, recency contribution, kind bonus, and project scope metadata.
acceptance_criteria: Response contract documented in bead notes and reflected in tests; includes deterministic field names/types and nullability rules; unknown or unavailable components are represented safely without crashing clients.
notes:
Touchpoints: codemem/mcp_server.py tool shape plus any store helper payload typing.

Kickoff plan:
1) Define request args: query (optional), ids (optional), filters.project (optional), include_pack_context (bool, default false).
2) Define response envelope: {items: [...], missing_ids: [...], errors: [...], metadata: {...}}.
3) Per-item explain payload candidate fields: retrieval_source, score_total, score_components {fts, semantic, recency, kind_bonus, project_bonus?}, matched_query_terms, matched_tags, matched_project, rank_position.
4) Define deterministic null/default rules and non-fatal error semantics for unknown IDs and project-filter mismatches.
5) Lock contract in tests before implementation in chf.2/chf.3.
Contract finalized in docs/plans/2026-03-01-memory-explain-contract.md.
Key decisions locked:
- Request args: query|ids (+project, include_pack_context, limit) with deterministic validation rules.
- Response envelope: items, missing_ids, errors, metadata.
- Per-item rationale fields: retrieval source/rank, score total + component breakdown, match metadata, optional pack_context.
- Null/default guarantees and non-fatal structured error semantics.
- Explicit test matrix for codemem-chf.4.
Opened PR: https://github.com/kunickiaj/codemem/pull/118 (branch 03-01-feature-gap-memory-explain-contract-v2).
close_reason: Contract doc and test matrix completed in docs/plans/2026-03-01-memory-explain-contract.md

description: Implement store-side logic to assemble explainability details for selected memory IDs and retrieval contexts.
acceptance_criteria: Store returns per-item rationale payload with deterministic score components; supports missing IDs and project mismatch handling; no behavioral changes to existing search ordering when explain mode is unused.
notes:
Likely files: codemem/store/search.py and/or codemem/store/packs.py.
Kickoff for store-side explainability assembly:
1) Add a store helper that resolves candidate items from query and/or ids with project scope applied.
2) Build deterministic per-item explain payload (retrieval source/rank, score total/components, match metadata).
3) Return non-fatal missing_ids/errors envelope matching contract doc.
4) Keep existing search/ranking behavior unchanged unless explain helper is explicitly called.
Implemented in commit 2d50d86.

title: Add retroactive secret-scan CLI for existing memories
description: One-shot CLI command to sweep already-stored memories and redact in place at rollout. Without this, day-1 users have an unscanned backlog forever and the protection is forward-only.
acceptance_criteria: CLI command runs over a populated store, applies scanner to existing memory contents, rewrites stored form to redacted version. Idempotent (second run is a no-op). Reports detection counts grouped by kind. Documented in CLI help and README.
notes: Retroactive sweep landed. scanSecretsRetroactive walks memory_items in id order, scans every content-bearing column (title/subtitle/body_text/narrative/tags_text/facts[]/concepts[]/metadata_json/actor_display_name/origin_source), and writes redacted versions back via single-row in-place UPDATE — never DELETE. CLI: codemem db scan-secrets [--limit N] [--dry-run] [--json]. Per-row transaction with WHERE rev = ? guard so concurrent legitimate writes are not silently clobbered (staleWrites counter). 1 MiB per-row CPU cap (skippedOversized counter). Junction-table refresh: memory_concept_refs is rebuilt for the redacted memory when concepts change, so the concept-index does not retain unredacted strings as a side-channel; memory_file_refs left alone since files_read/files_modified are not scanned (paths are out of current scope). Idempotent: scanning [REDACTED:kind] markers is a no-op because the marker char class does not match any default rule. Tests cover legacy redact, idempotency on dual-rule (secret-context + pattern), dry-run, oversize, actor_display_name+origin_source, concept-junction refresh, and a TOCTOU regression with a racy scanner that bumps rev between SELECT and UPDATE. DEFERRED: files_read/files_modified scanning (path arrays — paths can leak query-string secrets but consistency with sync-receive scope keeps them out for now); chunked transactions (current per-row tx is correct, perf if needed later); SQL-level size cap instead of post-fetch (current cap is CPU-only, memory cap would need a SQL filter).
close_reason: Retroactive sweep with in-place UPDATE only, junction-table refresh, rev-guard for TOCTOU, oversize cap, idempotency. CLI db scan-secrets exposed. 1545/1545 tests green.

title: Apply secret scanner to sync-receive payloads
description: Route sync-received memories through the same scanner hook before persistence so peers running older versions or without scanning enabled cannot bypass write-path protection. Without receive-side scanning, sync is a clean bypass.
acceptance_criteria: Incoming peer memories pass through the scanner before persistence. Same redaction policy as write-path (silent redact, no override). Tests cover a peer payload containing a known secret pattern being redacted on receive.
notes: Wired secret scanner into both sync-receive paths via a shared redactMemoryFields helper (packages/core/src/secret-scanner.ts) called after parseMemoryPayload (sync-replication.ts) and parseSnapshotPayload (sync-bootstrap.ts). applyReplicationOps and applyBootstrapSnapshot now take an optional SecretScanner; SyncPassOptions has a scanner field threaded through runSyncPass + syncOnce + syncDaemonTick; CLI bootstrap caller and viewer-server route pass store.scanner. One-time stderr warning fires when sync runs without an explicit scanner so workspace-rule gaps are not silent. redactMemoryFields covers title/subtitle/body_text/narrative/tags_text (split-scan-rejoin to match local per-tag scan), facts/concepts string arrays, metadata_json (recursive), plus actor_display_name and origin_source per gordon review (peer-controlled persisted strings). Tests cover insert path, update path, custom-scanner-rules-apply through applyReplicationOps, peer-controlled identity field redaction, and bootstrap-snapshot redaction. Foundation review notes deferred to tzrn (AI/maintenance writers, structured detection log) and vb2s (input length cap) remain in flight.
close_reason: Sync-receive scanning landed across applyReplicationOps and applyBootstrapSnapshot; scanner threaded through CLI, viewer-server, daemon, and sync-pass. 1536/1536 tests green, lint+tsc clean.

title: Scan summarization/compaction output for secrets
description: Re-run the scanner on summarizer/compaction output before storing the summary. Summarization can paraphrase content past pattern-based detection on the source, or invent strings that look like secrets. Source-only scanning leaves a gap.
acceptance_criteria: Summarized output passes through the scanner before being stored as a memory. Test covers synthetic case where source text is clean but summary output contains a secret pattern.
notes: Wired scanner into the three direct memory_items writers that bypass MemoryStore.remember: aiBackfillStructuredContent, backfillNarrativeFromBody, backfillTagsText. Each accepts an optional SecretScanner; CLI callers in packages/cli/src/commands/db.ts:531/762/816 now pass store.scanner. The 'compaction/summarization output' goal of this issue is satisfied transitively because session_summary writes flow through store.remember() in ingest-pipeline.ts (already scanned by gl82). Confirmed summary-memory.ts and raw-event-flush.ts contain no direct memory_items writes. Test added in maintenance.test.ts covering AI-output redaction (narrative + facts containing PAT). DEFERRED: structured detection-log table (was originally targeted at this issue) — keeping the per-call log surface is fine for now since each caller already has scanner instance access; revisit when there's a concrete observability requirement. Documented case-sensitivity caveat in backfill-tags.ts comment (deriveTags lowercases, case-sensitive rules don't fire on legacy-derived tags; vb2s closes that gap for legacy data).
close_reason: AI/maintenance writers now scanner-aware; CLI callers thread store.scanner; AI output redaction tested. 1537/1537 tests green. Structured detection log deferred.

title: Add secret-scanner hook to core store write path
description: Add a single scanner chokepoint in packages/core/src/store.ts write path so every memory write is scanned for secrets and redacted before persistence. Foundation for sync-receive, compaction, workspace rules, allowlist, and retroactive sweep work. Codemem has no legitimate use case for secrets in memories, so policy is strict redact with no override flag.
design: secretlint chosen over gitleaks-binary (no PATH dependency for npm-distributed CLI) and over trufflehog (AGPL-3.0 incompatible with public npm distribution). Ported gitleaks rule packs into secretlint config for rule freshness. Single funnel in store.ts so CLI, MCP, plugin, and sync-receive all share one hook. No --allow-secret escape hatch: any override becomes the bypass that makes scanning theater.
acceptance_criteria: Scanner runs on every store write. Matches replaced with [REDACTED:<kind>] before persistence. Only redacted form persisted; original never written to disk. Detection kind+count logged (never the matched value). No override flag exists. Unit tests cover AWS keys, JWTs, GitHub PATs, and generic high-entropy strings.
notes: Foundation landed. Gordon-ramsay review surfaced these items, with disposition: FIXED in this issue: scanner moved off module-global to per-MemoryStore instance state (this.scanner) so workspace rules can plug in cleanly; redactValue now guards against non-plain objects (Date/Map/Set/RegExp/Buffer/typed-arrays returned unchanged) and detects cycles via WeakSet seen-set; Anthropic vs OpenAI rule precedence pinned via negative lookahead and a regression test; OpenAI rule given minEntropy floor (3.5) to avoid flagging low-entropy sk- prefixed identifiers; SECRET_BEARING_KEY and generic_assigned_secret patterns extended with access_token/refresh_token/id_token/bearer_token/api_token/credential variants; tags now scanned before being joined into tags_text; replication_ops payload integration test added asserting no original-secret bytes ever reach replicated state. DEFERRED to dependent issues: sync-replication apply path scanning -> codemem-hflk (already in scope); sync-bootstrap snapshot apply -> add to codemem-hflk; AI/maintenance backfill writers (ai-structured.ts, backfill-narrative.ts, backfill-tags.ts) bypass scanner today and should be wired in codemem-tzrn (compaction/AI output); structured-table detection log instead of stderr-only -> add to codemem-tzrn since both need a schema decision; input length cap and DoS guards -> add to codemem-vb2s (retroactive sweep is the path most exposed to large legacy inputs); PEM gaps (PGP MESSAGE blocks, SSH2 ENCRYPTED PRIVATE KEY, PuTTY user-key files) -> backlog under codemem-ben8 (workspace/extra rules); allowlist string=exact-match vs regex=partial-match asymmetry documented in ScannerOptions JSDoc, no behavior change.
close_reason: Foundation landed: scanner module + per-MemoryStore wire-up + 124 tests covering AWS/JWT/GitHub/Slack/Google/Stripe/Anthropic/OpenAI/PEM rules, recursive object walk with cycle/non-plain-object guards, rule precedence, tags, and replication-payload no-leak invariant. Full workspace gate (tsc + biome lint + 1529 tests) green. Deferred work captured in notes on dependents.

title: Evaluate e2e encrypted memory storage
description: Assess viability of end-to-end encrypted codemem storage so durable seed/coordinator infrastructure can persist ciphertext while plaintext access remains controlled by worker/user-held keys. Motivation: internal-only deployment does not protect against sufficiently privileged namespace users using kubectl port-forward, exec, or mounted-data inspection paths. Evaluate threat model, key management, search/indexing implications, sync protocol impact, recovery/rotation, and staged MVP options.

title: Update GitHub Actions for Node 24 runtime
description: GitHub Actions warned that Node.js 20-based JavaScript actions are deprecated and will default to Node 24 starting June 2, 2026. Audit and update the workflow actions used in this repo, especially actions/checkout@v4, actions/setup-node@v4, and pnpm/action-setup@v4. Confirm the current recommended versions, update workflows to Node-24-safe action versions, and verify CI still passes.

- Update checkout/setup-node/pnpm setup actions to Node 24-safe versions

title: Migrate settings UI to shared Radix primitives

Migrate `packages/ui/src/tabs/settings.tsx` off its remaining ad hoc interaction patterns and onto the shared Radix-backed primitive layer.

Primary targets:
- replace native select controls with `RadixSelect` where the primitive boundary says selects should be shared
- replace the current ad hoc settings section tabs with a shared tabs primitive
- review dialog dismiss affordances so the current text-only `close` treatment is normalized to something more intentional and consistent

Keep straightforward text-entry fields native unless a concrete accessibility or reuse reason appears during implementation.

- settings tab navigation uses the shared tabs primitive instead of ad hoc tab markup/behavior
- select controls in settings use the shared select primitive where appropriate
- text-entry controls intentionally remain native unless explicitly justified otherwise
- dismiss/close control styling and affordance are improved to match the component-layer direction
- targeted UI typecheck/build pass for touched files

- Replaced native settings selects with `RadixSelect`.
- Normalized the settings modal dismiss affordance with the shared close button treatment.

Primary targets:

title: Clean up remaining sync-area mixed interaction primitives
description:
Audit and clean up the remaining sync-area surfaces after the main primitive and dialog migrations land. Focus on `sync-disclosure.tsx`, `sync-actors.tsx`, `sync-peers.tsx`, `sync-legacy-claims.tsx`, `diagnostics.ts`, and nearby sync UI as needed.

This bead is the straggler pass: remove leftover hybrid usage, normalize on shared primitives where the boundary calls for them, and leave intentionally-native controls alone. Use it to finish the migration rather than to open a second architecture debate.
acceptance_criteria:
- remaining sync-area surfaces do not mix native/ad hoc and shared implementations for the same primitive class without a stated reason
- sync-area interaction patterns are consistent with the primitive boundary set by the epic

title: Add missing shared Radix primitives for viewer UI
description:
Add the shared Radix-backed primitives needed to finish the interaction-layer migration in `packages/ui`. Start from the current wrapper set (`RadixDialog`, `RadixSelect`, `RadixSwitch`) and add the missing reusable primitives required by the main surfaces, most likely tabs and radio groups.

Scope should stay focused on shared primitive/component work rather than surface migration. If existing wrappers need small API cleanup for reuse, keep that cleanup tightly scoped to enabling downstream migrations.

The primitives should be designed as reusable building blocks, not one-off wrappers around a single screen's markup.
acceptance_criteria:
- shared primitives required by downstream migration beads exist under the UI primitive/component layer
- expected missing primitives (at minimum tabs and radio group if still needed after audit) are available behind shared APIs
- any wrapper API cleanup is limited to enabling reuse
- no major surface migration is bundled into this bead
- targeted UI typecheck/build pass for touched files
notes:

- Added `@radix-ui/react-tabs` and `@radix-ui/react-radio-group` to the UI package dependencies.

title: Finish viewer Radix interaction-primitives migration
description:
## Goal

Remove the current half-migrated state in `packages/ui` by standardizing shared interaction primitives on the Radix-backed component layer, while intentionally keeping simple text-entry controls native. This epic formalizes the UI package's shift from ad hoc controls to a real component-library boundary that fits the viewer's current complexity.

## Scope

Standardize these primitive classes behind shared components:
- dialog
- dropdown menu
- select
- switch
- collapsible
- tabs
- radio group

Intentionally keep these native for now unless a concrete reuse/accessibility need appears:
- text input
- number input
- textarea
- basic checkbox

## Why now

The UI already uses some Radix primitives (`DropdownMenu`, `RadixDialog`, `RadixSelect`, `RadixSwitch`, collapsibles), but major surfaces still mix native controls, ad hoc interaction chrome, and shared primitives for the same interaction categories. That hybrid state increases inconsistency, makes future UI work harder, and leaves accessibility/keyboard behavior uneven.

## Design direction

Following the current design-maturity path for codemem's viewer UI, this work should formalize the component layer rather than abstract every form control. Complex/repeated interaction patterns should use shared Radix-backed primitives; straightforward text-entry fields should stay native.

The migration should also review dismiss affordances in dialogs and similar overlays. Low-emphasis text-only `close` actions currently look awkward in some places and should be normalized as part of the surface migrations rather than left as stray visual debt.

## Exit criteria

- `packages/ui` has a clear primitive boundary between shared Radix-backed interaction components and intentionally-native controls
- major interactive viewer surfaces no longer mix native/ad hoc vs shared implementations for the same primitive class without a stated reason
- shared primitives needed to complete the boundary exist and are reused
- validation for touched UI work runs via targeted typecheck/build commands
notes:
- Epic implemented in one pass: shared tabs/radio-group primitives added; settings and sync dialog surfaces migrated; remaining sync select cleanup completed; dismiss controls normalized.
- Validation run: `pnpm --filter @codemem/ui typecheck`, `pnpm run lint`, `pnpm --filter @codemem/ui build`.

title: Audit remaining viewer UI controls for Radix migration
description:
Review `packages/ui` for remaining interactive controls that still use mixed native or ad hoc patterns instead of the emerging Radix primitive layer. Inventory current usage of dialogs, selects, switches, menus, collapsibles, tabs, radio groups, and dismiss affordances; identify which areas are already on shared primitives, which are still hybrid, and which should intentionally stay native.

Produce the migration boundary and recommended implementation order for the epic:
- shared Radix-backed: dialog, menu, select, switch, collapsible, tabs, radio group
- native for now: text input, number input, textarea, basic checkbox

Call out specific high-value surfaces (`settings.tsx`, `sync-dialogs.tsx`, remaining sync subcomponents) and note any awkward dismiss/close controls that should be normalized during migration work.
acceptance_criteria:
- inventory of current primitive usage across `packages/ui`
- explicit keep-native vs migrate-to-shared boundary
- recommended bead/PR sequence for the epic
- list of specific target files/surfaces for follow-up implementation
- dismiss-control findings captured, including where text-only `close` affordances should be improved
notes:
- Initial audit snapshot: Radix already backs dropdown menu (`feed.ts`), dialog/select/switch primitives, and some sync collapsibles/selects/switches.
- Highest-value hybrid surfaces remain `packages/ui/src/tabs/settings.tsx` and `packages/ui/src/tabs/sync/sync-dialogs.tsx`.
- `settings.tsx` already uses `RadixDialog` but still relies on native `<select>` and ad hoc tabs.
- `sync-dialogs.tsx` still uses native `<select>` and radio handling in the duplicate-person flow.
- Dismiss affordance note from current UI review: text-only `close` controls in modal headers feel visually weak/awkward and should be normalized during migration work rather than left as incidental polish debt.
- Completed audit boundary in code: shared Radix-backed primitives now cover tabs, radio groups, dialogs, selects, switches, dropdown menus, and collapsibles; plain text inputs/number inputs/textareas/basic checkboxes remain native.
- Confirmed the main hybrid surfaces were `settings.tsx`, `sync-dialogs.tsx`, and actor merge select flows in `sync-actors.tsx`.
- Dismiss affordance issue was addressed via a shared close button component and normalized modal-close styling.

title: Add UI memory delete flow with confirmation
description: Expose manual deletion of individual memories in the viewer UI. The backend and CLI already support soft-deleting memories via the existing forget/delete path, but the UI does not offer this action today. Add a visible delete/remove action for a single memory, require explicit user confirmation before performing it, handle success and failure states clearly, and refresh the affected view after deletion. Scope should stay narrow: reuse the existing memory deactivation behavior rather than introducing hard-delete semantics.

title: Backfill SessionContext on existing sessions after e96w/h3v8/f4qy fixes
description:
## Problem

After the classification/normalization bug fixes land (codemem-e96w, codemem-h3v8, codemem-f4qy), NEW sessions will have correctly-populated SessionContext fields. But the existing database has thousands of historical sessions with wrong values:

- promptCount, toolCount, firstPrompt = 0/null for historical Claude Code sessions (pre-e96w / pre-f4qy)
- filesModified = [] for historical sessions that used apply_patch / MultiEdit / NotebookEdit (pre-h3v8)

Fix forward-only leaves the historical mass wrong, which distorts any retrospective analysis and leaves session classification stale.

## Approach: maintenance_jobs queue, not a manual CLI

Use the existing maintenance_jobs durable queue pattern (already proven by the sync-vector epic and vector-migration.ts). Enqueue one job per candidate session, drain in background, idempotent on re-run. No new CLI surface, no user action required.

## Job shape

- Job type: 'rebuild-session-context' (or similar kind constant)
- Payload: session row id
- Handler:
  1. Load raw_events matching the session's (source, stream_id)
  2. Normalize via normalizeEventsForSessionContext (exported from raw-event-flush.ts)
  3. Run buildSessionContext on normalized events
  4. Diff against current metadata_json.session_context
  5. If different, update metadata_json in place; if same, no-op
- Idempotent: re-running after the write-side fixes land produces no diff
- Refuses if raw_events are missing/pruned for the target session (hard fail, not silent skip)

## Enqueue strategy

- On first startup after the fix ships: scan sessions table, enqueue jobs for sessions whose metadata looks like it pre-dates the fix (e.g. promptCount=0 AND toolCount=0 for Claude Code sessions, or filesModified=[] for any session with apply_patch-like events)
- One-time migration, no periodic re-run needed
- Rate-limit enqueue so we don't flood the queue on first boot

## Caveats

- Depends on raw_events retention. Must land before any raw-event pruning policy ships (codemem-clv, codemem-edq) or backfill becomes partial
- Does NOT fix session_summary over-production (codemem-garf handles that via a separate maintenance job)
- Anything derived from plugin live-state rather than raw_events will not round-trip and should be detected/reported, not fabricated

## Acceptance criteria

- New maintenance_jobs handler registered
- Enqueue-on-startup scan identifies and queues pre-fix sessions
- Dry-run / observe mode via maintenance job status (idempotent diffs visible in logs before changes commit)
- Unit test: synthetic historical session row with known-bad metadata, handler runs, assert corrected values
- Integration test: enqueue, drain, observe idempotency on second run
- Refuses cleanly when raw_events missing

## Discovered from

codemem-17tp investigation. User direction: 'what we can fix we should (automatically) using maintenance tasks.'
notes: PR #718 opened — maintenance_jobs rebuild-session-context handler stacked on #717
close_reason: Shipped as PR #718

comments.author: Adam Kunicki

title: Normalize events before buildSessionContext in extraction-replay.ts (mirror of e96w)
description:
## Problem

packages/core/src/extraction-replay.ts around line 462 has the same bug pattern that codemem-e96w fixed in raw-event-flush.ts: calls buildSessionContext(events) on unnormalized raw events. This means any replay-based re-extraction path has the same silent classification degradation for Claude Code events as the flush path did before e96w landed.

## Discovered

Found by the agent fixing codemem-e96w (PR #715). Left out of that PR to keep scope tight. Replay paths are less frequently exercised than the main flush path so the immediate user-visible impact is smaller, but correctness should match across both paths.

## Fix sketch

Apply the same normalizeEventsForSessionContext() helper (or a shared export of it) that PR #715 added to raw-event-flush.ts. Extract the helper into a shared location if the duplication becomes awkward.

## Acceptance criteria

- Test coverage mirroring the raw-event-flush test, but exercising the replay path
- pnpm tsc / lint / test pass

title: Deduplicate session_summary emission within a session row
description:
## Problem

Long-running sessions accumulate multiple session_summary memories — one per flush batch that lands in an 'allow' class (working / micro_high_signal / durable). Empirical evidence from ~/.codemem/mem.sqlite recent-30 sample:

  session  | adapter   | total | session_summary
  ---------|-----------|------:|----------------:
  166599   | claude    |     5 |               2
  166600   | opencode  |    13 |               5
  166639   | claude    |     4 |               3+

43% of recent memories were session_summary. Track 3 policy says typed observations should be primary and summaries secondary support — this inverts that.

## Root cause

shouldSuppressSummaryOnlyOutput() in session-policy.ts has two compounding rules:

1. 'if observationsCount > 0 return false' — any flush with even one typed observation bypasses suppression
2. Only trivial_turn / micro_low_value are suppressed; working / micro_high_signal / durable all permit summaries

Real flushes almost always have substance and typed observations. Suppression rarely fires. Policy matches the current session-boundary-matrix.md doc — nothing is broken, the policy just wasn't written with 'one summary per session' as an invariant.

Note: the per-flush observer LLM only sees that flush's events, so each summary reflects a partial view of the session. Stitching is needed to reconstruct the full session picture.

## Approach: maintenance_jobs queue for consolidation

Aligned with codemem-3yl3 and the sync-vector migration pattern. Let the write path continue emitting per-flush summaries (no changes to the write side). A maintenance job periodically (or on idle) scans sessions with multiple session_summary memories and consolidates them into one.

## Design decision required (one of):

### Option A — Structural merge
Concatenate fields from N summaries using claude-mem-style structured sections (Request, Completed, Learned, Investigated, Next steps). No LLM calls. Deterministic. Limit: output is verbose and mechanical.

### Option B — LLM stitching
Send the N summaries (+ optional typed observations for context) to the observer LLM with a consolidation prompt. Returns one clean summary. Cost: LLM calls per session. Best output quality. Introduces LLM dependency into the maintenance path.

### Option C — Latest-wins with rollup metadata
Keep the last session_summary, delete/tombstone earlier ones, attach a rollup field recording how many were merged and their IDs (for audit). Fastest, cheapest, but loses early-session context.

## Recommended: Option B with A as fallback

LLM stitching when available (best quality, aligns with user's earlier 'stitching seems most correct' preference), fall back to structural merge if observer unavailable or rate-limited. Idempotent: if session already has 1 summary, no-op.

## Job shape

- Job type: 'consolidate-session-summaries'
- Payload: session row id
- Handler:
  1. Load session_summary memories for the session
  2. If count <= 1, no-op (idempotent)
  3. Run consolidation (option B primary, option A fallback)
  4. Write the new consolidated summary, tombstone/demote the originals with audit metadata
- Safety: never destroys underlying typed observations

## Enqueue strategy

- On idle or periodic sweep, scan for sessions with multiple session_summary memories
- Optionally: enqueue on session close (if session close detection exists) for new sessions going forward
- Skip sessions recently touched (grace window) to avoid racing with in-progress flushes

## Depends on

- codemem-e96w (must be fixed first so new summaries come from correctly-classified sessions; otherwise the consolidation pass just folds bad data)
- codemem-3yl3 (the backfill should run first so classification is corrected historically, which may in turn change which sessions have redundant summaries)

## Acceptance criteria

- Maintenance job handler registered
- Enqueue-on-idle sweep identifies candidate sessions
- Consolidation produces one session_summary per session row
- Observable reduction in session_summary share of recent memories (target: well under 25%, from current ~43%)
- Typed observations unchanged
- Tombstoned originals preserved for audit, not hard-deleted

## Discovered from

codemem-17tp investigation. User direction: 'what we can fix we should (automatically) using maintenance tasks.'
notes: On hold pending measurement of post-fix session_summary ratio. PRs #715-#718 fixed the classification bug that was the primary cause. If the ratio drops sufficiently on new sessions, garf may be downgraded to P3 backlog cleanup or closed. Design plan at ~/.claude/plans/jazzy-watching-quilt.md is ready if needed.

title: filesModified empty in SessionContext for both adapters
description:
## Problem

The SessionContext.filesModified field is stored as 0/empty for every observed session across BOTH adapters, despite sessions that clearly modified files. filesRead works correctly on the OpenCode side (session 166636 has filesRead=16), so the extraction infrastructure isn't uniformly broken — only filesModified is.

## Evidence

SQL query across 6 recent sessions in ~/.codemem/mem.sqlite:

  session | adapter   | tool_count | filesRead | filesModified
  --------|-----------|-----------:|----------:|--------------:
  166599  | claude    |          0 |         0 | 0
  166600  | opencode  |          4 |         0 | 0
  166635  | opencode  |         10 |         5 | 0
  166636  | opencode  |         68 |        16 | 0  <-- the ue7v implement session, definitely edited files
  166637  | opencode  |          8 |         4 | 0
  166639  | claude    |          0 |         0 | 0

Session 166636 is the codemem-ue7v implementation session. It had 68 tool calls and landed PR #712. It obviously modified files. filesModified=0 contradicts reality.

## Investigation needed

Root cause could be any of:
1. OpenCode plugin at packages/opencode-plugin/.opencode/plugins/codemem.js isn't capturing write/edit tool outputs into the filesModified Set (prior Explore suggested line 1013 uses a Set for both filesRead and filesModified)
2. The Set is populated but serialized as empty (Set->JSON issue)
3. Something between the plugin emit and metadata_json storage clobbers filesModified
4. For Claude Code: after codemem-17tp's normalization fix lands, filesModified extraction from PostToolUse tool_input.file_path may still be missing its own normalization

## Impact

hasModifiedFiles is always false in classifySessionForInjection(), which weakens the durable-class detection in session-policy.ts line 62-67. Sessions that should clearly be classified as 'durable' because they modified many files get classified lower (usually 'working' or 'micro_high_signal'). This isn't catastrophic but it distorts classification and correlates memories to wrong session classes.

## Acceptance criteria

- filesModified is correctly populated for OpenCode sessions that use write/edit tools
- filesModified is correctly populated for Claude Code sessions after the normalization fix lands
- Test: synthetic session with tool writes asserts the set is non-empty
notes: PR #716 opened for review — apply_patch file-path extraction added to buildSessionContext; stacked on PR #715.

title: Harden incremental sync vector queueing and remove orphaned helper

Remove or deprecate the now-orphaned maintainVectorsForReplicationApply helper so new code does not pick the wrong path, and harden/test the post-apply queueing path so a maintenance-job enqueue failure is surfaced instead of silently stranding vectors.

title: Skip vector re-embed for metadata-only sync updates
description: Incoming replication currently queues vector upserts for any memory_item update, even when title/body content is unchanged. This burns embedding compute on metadata-only changes such as visibility or tags updates. Queue receiver-side vector maintenance only when embeddable content changed, and add a regression test for the unchanged-content path.

title: Report sync semantic-index lag and failures
