title: Surface derived memory roles in retrieval diagnostics
description: Use the new derived memory-quality role inference in developer-facing diagnostics so Track 3 runtime behavior is inspectable and measurable. Scope: add inferred role/reason to pack trace and/or explain outputs, thread role-aware score components through scenario evaluation where useful, and add focused tests showing durable/ephemeral/recap roles are visible in retrieval debugging. Keep this as diagnostics/eval work, not schema persistence.

title: Create memory_vectors virtual table in bootstrapSchema
description:
Gordon Ramsay review on the auto-bootstrap PR found that schema-bootstrap.ts doesn't create the memory_vectors virtual table (sqlite-vec / vec0). Drizzle can't model virtual tables so the generated base DDL skips it, and SCHEMA_AUX_DDL only covers FTS5 + triggers + sync_retention_state.

## Consequence

On a fresh auto-bootstrapped DB:
- stats() / write paths degrade gracefully (storeVectors is in try/catch, stats() checks tableExists)
- semanticSearch → resolveSemanticSearchModel → listVectorModelCounts in packages/core/src/vectors.ts does raw db.prepare("SELECT ... FROM memory_vectors") with NO try/catch and NO tableExists guard
- First semantic search against an auto-bootstrapped DB throws "no such table: memory_vectors"

This is the same behavior on a db init-bootstrapped DB, so it's a pre-existing latent issue. But the auto-bootstrap fix invites more users down this path.

## Fix options

1. Add `CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS memory_vectors USING vec0(...)` to SCHEMA_AUX_DDL — the natural home for vec/fts virtual tables that Drizzle can't model. The vec0 column definition has to match whatever storeVectors expects (embedding vector dimension, memory_id column, etc.).
2. Add a tableExists("memory_vectors") guard to listVectorModelCounts so semantic search returns an empty result instead of throwing when the vec table is missing.

Both would be belt-and-suspenders. Option 1 is the fix; option 2 is defensive hardening.

## Acceptance criteria

- Fresh db init (or MemoryStore construction) produces a DB where `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM memory_vectors` succeeds.
- semanticSearch against a freshly-bootstrapped DB returns an empty result instead of throwing.
- Existing vec tests still pass.

title: Burn down packages/ui biome warnings (unexclude + safe-fix + noExplicitAny ratchet)
description:
Follows up on codemem-kwdx / PR #688. The packages/ui directory is currently excluded from biome.json via `!packages/ui` in files.includes, which means UI source is not linted by CI or pre-commit. Measured backlog when including ui: 224 errors + 1 warning + 6 infos, dominated by 133 noExplicitAny.

This issue tracks the first sweep: unexclude packages/ui from biome scope, apply safe autofixes (noUnusedImports, noUnusedVariables, useTemplate, etc.), and set noExplicitAny to warn specifically for packages/ui via a per-path override so the 133 existing any sites don't fail CI but new any usage in UI code is flagged. The 133 any backlog is then tracked as a separate follow-up to burn down incrementally.

## Scope

- Remove `!packages/ui` from biome.json files.includes
- Apply biome --write (safe autofixes) across packages/ui
- Manually fix the unsafe-autofixable ones that are clearly safe in context (noUnusedImports, noUnusedVariables, useOptionalChain, noExtraBooleanCast, etc.)
- Fix the easy manual categories: useTemplate (5), useIterableCallbackReturn (4), noPrototypeBuiltins (1), noTemplateCurlyInString (1), noNonNullAssertion (1)
- Add biome.json overrides section setting suspicious/noExplicitAny to warn for packages/ui only

## Non-goals

- Fixing the 133 noExplicitAny occurrences in UI code — that's a separate follow-up track

## Acceptance

- `pnpm exec biome check packages` passes for all rules EXCEPT noExplicitAny in packages/ui
- packages/ui is now in biome's scope for CI and pre-commit
- noExplicitAny count for packages/ui is unchanged but visible as warnings in biome output
- `pnpm run test` still green
- `pnpm run tsc` still green
close_reason: Completed via merged packages/ui biome cleanup and rule promotion on main (PRs #689-#698); current pnpm exec biome check packages passes.

title: Housekeeping: enforce biome lint rules we're currently letting slide
description:
The biome config has warnings/errors we've been ignoring during regular development — the most salient being `noExplicitAny` (use of `any` and loose `unknown` casts). We should tighten enforcement so these rules gate PRs instead of accumulating silently.

## Scope

- Survey current biome warnings across the workspace (`pnpm exec biome check packages` at max diagnostics) to get a baseline count by rule.
- Triage which rules to enforce now vs defer:
  - `lint/suspicious/noExplicitAny` — most impactful; requires per-file cleanups
  - Other `lint/suspicious/*` and `lint/complexity/*` rules currently flagged
  - `lint/style/*` nits that can be autofixed
- Apply `biome check --write` for autofixable rules across packages.
- For non-autofixable rules (any, unknown casts, non-null assertions in sync-auth.ts, etc.), open a focused cleanup branch per package or per rule so reviews stay tractable.
- Once a rule is clean across the workspace, promote it from warn to error in `biome.json` so regressions are caught in CI.

## Non-goals

- Rewriting existing code semantics — this is a type-safety + lint cleanup pass, not a refactor.
- Touching legacy Python code under `codemem/`.

## Acceptance

- Baseline biome warning count recorded.
- At least `noExplicitAny` and all currently-warned `lint/suspicious/*` rules promoted to error with a clean workspace.
- `pnpm exec biome check` returns zero warnings on the touched rules.
- CI gates new PRs on the promoted rules.
close_reason: Completed via merged biome enforcement cleanup on main, including promotion of lint rules back to error and green pnpm exec biome check packages (PRs #688-#698).

title: fix(cli): treat Claude hook ingest skipped>0 as benign no-op
description:
The 0.25.2 ingest path treats `{inserted:0, skipped:1}` from `/api/claude-hooks` as a transient failure and triggers the durability fallback (lock → drain → second HTTP retry → direct fallback). This is over-correct: `skipped:1` only happens when the viewer's `buildRawEventEnvelopeFromHook` returns null (Stop with no assistant text + no readable transcript, UserPromptSubmit with empty prompt, unsupported hook_event_name, etc.). The determination is deterministic — the direct fallback calls the same envelope builder and produces the same null result. The retry is pointless and produces double `plugin.log` entries per hook invocation (noise, not data loss).

## Root cause

In `packages/cli/src/commands/claude-hook-ingest.ts` `tryHttpIngest()`, the PR 682 fix added:

```ts
if (obj.skipped > 0) {
    logHookFailure("codemem claude-hook-ingest HTTP accepted but skipped payload");
    return { ok: false, inserted: obj.inserted, skipped: obj.skipped };
}
```

The viewer's `/api/claude-hooks` handler only returns `skipped:1` in one branch (envelope === null). Any real error returns 4xx/5xx. Any dedup hit returns `{inserted:0, skipped:0}`. So `skipped>0` from this endpoint is exclusively the deterministic 'no ingestible content' case.

## Fix

- Revert the `skipped>0 → failure` branch in `tryHttpIngest`. Return `{ok:true, inserted, skipped}` instead.
- Delete the `logHookFailure` call for this branch (it was noise).
- Keep the strict type validation that was added in the same PR (`typeof obj.inserted !== 'number'` etc) — that part is still correct.
- Flip the existing test `treats HTTP \`skipped > 0\` as a failure and falls through to direct ingest` to assert the no-op success shape instead.

## Evidence

Confirmed by four direct curl probes against the running 0.25.2 viewer:

| Payload | Response |
|---|---|
| Stop with no last_assistant_message | {inserted:0, skipped:1} |
| Stop with last_assistant_message | {inserted:1, skipped:0} |
| SessionStart | {inserted:1, skipped:0} |
| PostToolUse with tool_name | {inserted:1, skipped:0} |

## Impact

- Not data loss — the direct fallback path also treats null envelope as skipped and returns it to the orchestrator as ok, so the final result is correct.
- Noise: every Stop-without-text fires TWO `plugin.log` entries (one from the first HTTP attempt, one from the locked second HTTP attempt) plus does pointless work (lock + drain + direct fallback round trip) for a deterministic no-op.
- Will be batched into the next release (not cutting 0.25.3 immediately).
close_reason: Shipped via #686 fix(cli): treat Claude hook ingest skipped>0 as a benign no-op. Merged to main as commit c95f9d54.

title: Explore graph-based relationship layer for codemem retrieval
description: Investigate a lightweight graph/knowledge-link layer for codemem after the current sessionization, recap, and memory-quality work settles. Focus on derived read-only relationships rather than a graph-native source of truth. Candidate value: connect decisions, bugfixes, files, sessions, actors, follow-up work, and workstreams so retrieval can answer relationship-aware questions like what led to a decision, what work is downstream, and what threads are connected across sessions. Initial scope should evaluate edge types, extraction sources from existing memories/metadata, traversal-assisted retrieval, and whether graph context improves queries that plain text/embedding retrieval handles poorly. Keep this as a future exploration track, not a release blocker. Inspiration/reference shared by the user: silk-graph source at https://github.com/Kieleth/silk-graph and package at https://pypi.org/project/silk-graph/ . Working hypothesis: codemem may benefit from graph-derived context after current policy/model work stabilizes, especially for cross-session thread continuity, decision lineage, bugfix-to-bug linkage, file/module impact mapping, actor/workstream relationships, and downstream follow-up discovery. Recommended adoption path is incremental: derive graph edges from existing memory items and metadata first, keep graph read-only, validate on a few query classes where current retrieval is weakest, and only then consider deeper graph-native storage or workflows.
close_reason: Layer 1 (file/concept index) implemented and submitted as PRs #724-#732. Junction tables, write-time population, backfill, query functions, pack builder integration across all modes, and all Gordon review fixes shipped.

title: Consolidate config path resolution into a single resolver
description:
## Problem

codemem has five ways to locate a config file: --config flag, CODEMEM_CONFIG env, CODEMEM_RUNTIME_ROOT env, CODEMEM_WORKSPACE_ID env, and legacy global ~/.config/codemem/. Resolution is spread across 6+ functions in packages/core/src/observer-config.ts. PR 594 shipped with a silent-fallback bug (invalid CODEMEM_RUNTIME_ROOT returned null instead of falling through to workspace-id), demonstrating the pipeline is too complex to reason about.

## Work

1. Introduce resolveCodememConfigPath(options) returning { path, source, reason, fallbackChain }.
2. Refactor getCodememConfigPath, getCodememConfigWritePath, and all CLI read/write paths to use it.
3. Add codemem config where command printing the full resolution chain.
4. Document or remove the silent seed-from-legacy behavior in runWorkspaceConfigCommand.
5. Remove redundant helper exports.

## Acceptance criteria

- Single resolver function used by all read and write paths.
- codemem config where prints path + source + reason + fallback chain.
- Existing behavior preserved (same tests pass + new tests for fallback reporting).
- No silent fallbacks: every fallback is reported.
- runWorkspaceConfigCommand seed-from-legacy behavior documented or removed.

Discovered from PR 594 review feedback.

title: Fix host/port flag semantics on sync enable
description:
Audit violation: #43

sync enable uses bare --host/--port to mean the sync listener address, but convention reserves bare --host/--port for the viewer/serve service. Should be --sync-host/--sync-port.

## Work

1. Add --sync-host and --sync-port flags to sync enable.
2. Keep --host/--port as hidden aliases with deprecation warning.
3. Update docs.

## Acceptance criteria

- codemem sync enable --sync-host 0.0.0.0 --sync-port 7337 works.
- codemem sync enable --host 0.0.0.0 warns and works.
- Tests pass.

Parent epic: codemem-vcam

title: Add --json support to data-returning commands that lack it
description:
Audit violations: #20, #22, #23, #24, #26, #27, #29, #31

Multiple data-returning commands have no --json flag, breaking the automation contract.

Must-fix:
- recent (no --json at all)

Should-fix:
- import-memories (returns import counts)
- sync once (returns sync pass results)
- sync enable (returns device ID, fingerprint)
- sync doctor (diagnostic output)
- sync pair (pairing payload)
- memory show (always outputs JSON even without --json flag; should have human-readable default)
- memory remember (returns memory ID)

## Work

1. Add --json to each command via the shared option builder.
2. When --json is set: emit structured JSON on success, structured {error, message} on failure.
3. When --json is NOT set: use @clack/prompts or human-readable format.
4. memory show: add --json flag; default output becomes human-readable, --json gives raw JSON.

## Acceptance criteria

- All listed commands accept -j/--json.
- Success: single JSON object/array to stdout, exit 0.
- Failure: {error, message} to stdout, exit non-zero.
- Tests cover both JSON and non-JSON output paths.

Parent epic: codemem-vcam
close_reason: Shipped in PR #597: --json added to recent, sync once/enable/doctor/pair, memory show/remember/forget, import-memories

title: Document and harden seed peer to coordinator runtime dependency during bootstrap
description:
## Problem

PR 595 introduced a new runtime coupling: when a worker device presents a bootstrap grant to a seed peer's \`/v1/status\` or \`/v1/snapshot\` endpoint, the seed's viewer-server makes an HTTP call to the coordinator's \`/v1/admin/bootstrap-grants/:grantId\` endpoint to verify the grant (not revoked, not expired, worker enrollment matches, scope allowed).

Before this PR, the seed peer only depended on coordinator availability during enrollment and presence refresh. After this PR, **during active bootstrap load, the seed peer fans out to the coordinator on every verification attempt**.

This has three consequences worth addressing:

1. **Availability coupling** — if the coordinator is unreachable, bootstrapping new workers against the seed fails even if the seed itself is healthy. The error surfaces as \`bootstrap_grant_lookup_failed\` which is not obviously a coordinator reachability issue.
2. **Fleet burst amplification** — a batch of workers bootstrapping simultaneously against the same seed generates a burst of coordinator admin API calls from the seed. The preauth rate-limit fix (P1 from review) caps the upstream client flood, but the seed still issues a coordinator request per allowed attempt.
3. **Architectural doc drift** — \`docs/architecture.md\` does not describe this coupling. Anyone reading current docs will not expect seed → coordinator runtime traffic during bootstrap.

## Proposal

### Documentation (required)

- Add a section to \`docs/architecture.md\` describing the bootstrap grant verification flow, including the seed → coordinator runtime call and the failure modes it introduces.
- Add a troubleshooting entry to \`docs/user-guide.md\` for \`bootstrap_grant_lookup_failed\` pointing at coordinator reachability as a likely cause.

### Caching (consider)

- Add a short-TTL positive cache for verified grants at the seed, keyed by grant_id. TTL should be much shorter than grant expiry (e.g., 30s) so revocation propagates quickly.
- Cache should be in-memory only (no persistence) and scoped to the viewer-server process.
- Cache must be invalidated on any verification failure — do not cache negative results (an attacker could otherwise poison the cache against a legitimate worker).
- Adds complexity; only pursue if observed coordinator load during fleet rollout is actually problematic. File as a separate follow-up if deferring.

### Degraded-mode behavior (consider)

- Decide whether to fail-open or fail-closed when the coordinator is unreachable.
- Default should be fail-closed (current behavior): unknown grant state must not grant access.
- Document this decision explicitly.

## Acceptance criteria

- Architecture doc updated with the coupling diagram and failure modes.
- User-guide troubleshooting entry added.
- Caching decision made (do it now, or file a separate issue with measurements to justify).
- Fail-closed behavior on coordinator unreachable is documented and tested.

## Discovered from

PR 595 (ajk/bootstrap-grant-automation) — post-merge code review flagged the new runtime coupling.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #597 — Bootstrap grant verification flow section added to docs/architecture.md, troubleshooting entry added to docs/user-guide.md.

title: Limit bootstrap grant error-reason leakage at unauthenticated boundary
description:
## Problem

In \`packages/viewer-server/src/routes/sync.ts\`, the \`/v1/status\` and \`/v1/snapshot\` handlers use \`authorizeBootstrapGrantRequest\` to verify a bootstrap grant when standard signed-request auth fails. On failure, the handler returns the specific failure reason in the 401 response body when \`exposeBootstrapReason\` is true (any reason starting with \`bootstrap_grant_\`).

The possible reasons currently leaked to unauthenticated clients include:

- \`bootstrap_grant_lookup_failed\`
- \`bootstrap_grant_invalid_payload\`
- \`bootstrap_grant_revoked\`
- \`bootstrap_grant_worker_enrollment_mismatch\`
- \`bootstrap_grant_group_mismatch\`
- \`bootstrap_grant_worker_disabled\`
- \`bootstrap_grant_worker_mismatch\`
- \`bootstrap_grant_seed_mismatch\`
- \`bootstrap_grant_expired\`
- \`bootstrap_grant_scope_denied\`
- \`bootstrap_grant_signature_verification_error\`
- \`bootstrap_grant_invalid_signature\`
- \`bootstrap_grant_coordinator_not_configured\`
- \`missing_bootstrap_grant_headers\`

An attacker probing with forged grants learns exactly what is wrong each time, which accelerates credential stuffing and group/device ID enumeration (e.g., \`group_mismatch\` vs \`worker_mismatch\` vs \`seed_mismatch\` are distinguishable signals).

## Threat model

- If the coordinator and sync endpoints are only reachable on a private network / fleet VPN, the risk is low and the debuggability benefit is probably worth keeping the detailed reasons.
- If anything is reachable from the open internet (or will be), the current behavior is a small but real info-disclosure vector.

## Proposal

1. Pick a threat model explicitly and document it (probably in \`docs/architecture.md\` or a security doc under \`docs/\`).
2. If open-internet-reachable:
   - Collapse all wire responses to a single generic \`bootstrap_grant_invalid\` (or \`unauthorized\` matching the non-bootstrap path).
   - Keep the detailed reasons in the server structured logs so operators can still debug.
   - Add a test asserting that no wire response contains a reason starting with \`bootstrap_grant_\` except the sanitized placeholder.
3. If private-fleet-only:
   - Document the assumption in the user guide and/or architecture doc.
   - Consider adding a config flag \`bootstrap_grant_expose_reasons\` that defaults to \`false\` in the open-internet case.

## Acceptance criteria

- Threat model written and linked from this issue.
- Either detailed reasons are gated behind a config flag OR collapsed to generic on the wire with detailed reasons in logs.
- Test covers the selected behavior.

## Discovered from

PR 595 (ajk/bootstrap-grant-automation) — post-merge code review flagged the error taxonomy leakage.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #597 — bootstrapAttempted flag replaces exposeBootstrapReason, wire responses use generic bootstrap_grant_invalid, specific reasons logged server-side via console.warn.

title: Decide fate of bootstrap-grant scope field: commit or delete
description:
## Problem

PR 595 introduced a \`scope\` field (TEXT NOT NULL) on \`coordinator_bootstrap_grants\` and threaded it through:

- \`CoordinatorBootstrapGrant\` type
- \`CoordinatorCreateBootstrapGrantInput\`
- \`CoordinatorReviewJoinRequestBootstrapGrantInput\`
- The D1 + better-sqlite store implementations
- The coordinator admin API payload
- The viewer-server \`authorizeBootstrapGrantRequest\` verification (\`allowedScopes: ['bootstrap']\`)
- CLI flags for list/revoke
- The coordinator join-request review handler payload

In current code, \`scope\` is **always** the string literal \`\"bootstrap\"\`. There is exactly one caller, one valid value, and one allowed-scopes check. The field is doing no work.

## Options

**Option A — Delete it.** Remove \`scope\` from the schema, types, admin API, verification path, and CLI. Fewest moving parts; matches current reality.

**Option B — Commit to it.** Convert \`scope\` to a typed enum (\`BootstrapGrantScope = 'bootstrap' | ...future values\`), add tests asserting that unknown scopes are rejected at every boundary (store insert, admin API read, authorization check), and document the extension model with at least one planned second scope value. Only worth doing if there is a real near-term plan for scoped grants beyond bootstrap.

**Option C — Park it.** Leave it as a free-form string, add a TODO, accept the smell.

I'd recommend A unless there is a concrete plan for a second scope value within the next couple of releases.

## Acceptance criteria

- Decision recorded (probably as an ADR under \`docs/adr/\` or similar).
- If A: schema migration to drop the column, types updated, callers cleaned up, tests updated.
- If B: enum type committed, unknown-scope rejection tests added at every boundary, docs updated with at least one planned additional scope.
- If C: closing comment explaining why parking, plus a TODO comment in the code.

## Discovered from

PR 595 (ajk/bootstrap-grant-automation) — post-merge code review flagged the field as speculative extensibility.
close_reason: Already shipped in PR #597 — scope field was never present in the merged version of PR 595. The agents' removal was folded into the CLI refactor commit.

title: Consolidate codemem config path resolution into a single resolver
description:
## Problem

After PR 594 (config-first workspace config), codemem now has five ways to locate a config file:

1. `--config <path>` CLI flag (on ~9 sync subcommands, serve, and others)
2. `CODEMEM_CONFIG` env var
3. `CODEMEM_RUNTIME_ROOT` env var (workspace runtime dir)
4. `CODEMEM_WORKSPACE_ID` env var (workspace ID → ~/.codemem/workspaces/<id>/config/codemem.json)
5. Legacy global ~/.config/codemem/config.json{,c}

Resolution order is implemented across several functions in `packages/core/src/observer-config.ts`:

- `getCodememConfigPath()` — for reads
- `getCodememConfigWritePath()` — for writes
- `getWorkspaceScopedCodememConfigPath()` — for env-driven workspace lookup
- `getWorkspaceCodememConfigPath(workspaceId)` — for explicit workspace lookup
- `readCodememConfigFileAtPath(path)` — low-level reader
- `readWorkspaceCodememConfigFile(workspaceId)` — workspace-by-id reader
- `writeWorkspaceCodememConfigFile(workspaceId, data)` — workspace-by-id writer

This is already enough surface that PR 594 shipped with a silent-fallback bug caught in review: a malformed `CODEMEM_RUNTIME_ROOT` returned `null` from `getWorkspaceScopedCodememConfigPath()` instead of falling through to the workspace-id branch, silently landing at legacy global config. See PR 594 review thread (resolved) for details.

Additionally, `runWorkspaceConfigCommand` in `packages/cli/src/commands/config.ts` has an undocumented read-fallback: if the workspace config path doesn't exist, it reads legacy global config and uses that as the patch base, silently seeding the new workspace with the old global config on first write. This is probably intentional but is a UX footgun if a user expects a clean slate.

## Proposal

1. Introduce a single `resolveCodememConfigPath(options)` that returns a structured result:

   ```ts
   type ConfigPathResolution = {
     path: string;
     source: 'cli-flag' | 'env-codemem-config' | 'env-runtime-root' | 'env-workspace-id' | 'legacy-global';
     reason: string; // human-readable, e.g. "CODEMEM_RUNTIME_ROOT=/tmp/foo was relative, falling back to workspace-id"
     fallbackChain: ConfigPathResolution[]; // prior candidates that were rejected, for debugging
   };
   ```

2. Refactor `getCodememConfigPath`, `getCodememConfigWritePath`, and all CLI read/write paths to go through this resolver.

3. Add a `codemem config where` command (or similar) that prints the full resolution chain so users can answer "which config file is actually being used and why?"

4. Either:
   - Document the silent seed-from-legacy behavior in `runWorkspaceConfigCommand`, OR
   - Remove it and require an explicit `--seed-from-legacy` flag.

5. Remove the now-redundant helper exports that were added in PR 594 if they're not used outside observer-config.ts.

## Acceptance criteria

- Single resolver function used by all read and write paths.
- A `codemem config where` (or equivalent) command prints path + source + reason + fallback chain.
- Existing behavior is preserved for all five location mechanisms (same tests pass, plus new tests for fallback chain reporting).
- No silent fallbacks: every fallback is reported in the resolution result.
- `runWorkspaceConfigCommand` seed-from-legacy behavior is either removed or documented with tests pinning the behavior.

## Discovered from

PR 594 (ajk/config-first-workspace-automation) — review feedback + post-merge code review flagged resolution pipeline as over-complex.

title: Bootstrap CLI should report all address results not just last error
description: The bootstrap CLI tries all peer addresses sequentially but only reports the last error. If the reachable address succeeded the status check but lacked sync_reset, that result is overwritten by subsequent timeout errors on unreachable addresses. Report all address results so operators can diagnose which address worked/failed and why.

The attention item might be an offline device needing repair, but the user has to scroll and guess. The attention summary should name or describe what needs action.

title: Synced memories arrive without tags — no deriveTags during replication
description: Sync replication (sync-replication.ts:1748,1785) passes through tags_text verbatim via COALESCE. If the source device's memories have empty tags_text (e.g., pre-tags-era memories, or observer_summary/session_summary kinds that didn't get tagged at ingest), they arrive on the receiving device without tags. The backfill-tags maintenance command exists but isn't run automatically. Options: (1) call deriveTags() during replication for newly inserted memories with empty tags_text, (2) auto-run backfill-tags after sync passes that introduced tagless memories, (3) surface a recommendation in the viewer after sync (already partially done via health card).
close_reason: Fixed in PR #526 — deriveTags during replication for tagless memories

title: Clarify 'other actors' filter label — confusing for multi-device single-person setup
description: The 'other actors' scope filter (ownership_scope: 'theirs') means actor_id != local AND origin_device_id != local. So your own memories created on a different device still count as 'mine' (because actor_id matches). This is technically correct for identity-based ownership, but the label 'other actors' is confusing in a multi-device single-person setup. Consider: rename to 'Other people' to better convey it means different identities, not different devices. Or add a separate 'From other devices' filter for cross-device discovery within the same identity.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #529 — renamed 'Other actors' to 'Other people'

title: Add vacuum guidance/option after large replication-op prune

comments.author: Adam Kunicki
comments.text: Completed via #436 in the retention execution stack. Added explicit --vacuum option and post-prune vacuum guidance.

title: Revisit dirty-local reset gate to decouple from replication-op materialization proxy
description: The phase-1 dirty-local predicate in `hasUnsyncedSharedMemoryChanges()` currently infers local unsynced shared-memory state by checking for absence of a matching replication op at the current `(entity_id, rev, op_type)`. This is acceptable for phase 1 but tightly couples reset safety semantics to current replication-op materialization/backfill behavior. If replication-op generation timing or rules change later, the predicate can become overly strict or subtly wrong. Revisit this after phase 1 to define a more explicit canonical-local-dirty invariant or persist a dedicated sync-materialization marker.

title: Investigate official SDK usage for observer custom providers
description: The TypeScript observer currently uses a plain fetch path for custom providers while OpenCode uses provider SDKs. Investigate whether codemem should adopt official/provider SDKs or a shared transport abstraction to reduce header/merge divergence and improve parity.

title: Surface stale/rebootstrapping/needs_attention sync states in UI and diagnostics
description: Phase 1 visibility slice for codemem-etuk. Expose sync recovery states (running, rebootstrapping, needs_attention, failed) in /api/sync/status and the viewer Sync UI so automatic recovery is visible to users and operators.

title: Show reconnecting splash in viewer UI when server is restarting
description: When the viewer server goes down and comes back up (restart/upgrade), the web UI should show a friendly reconnecting/restarting splash screen instead of a browser error page. Implement a client-side health-check poll that auto-reconnects when the server becomes available again.

title: Startup still slow on 16-18GB DBs after 0.20.3 fixes
description: Even after backup idempotence and sync preflight skip, startup is still noticeably slow on multi-GB databases. May need startup progress indicator, or further deferral of blocking work (e.g. sqlite-vec load, FTS warmup, schema assertions). User reports ~2min on 18GB DB.

title: Restore full ownership parity in memoryOwnedBySelf for claimed peers
description: packages/core/src/store.ts:memoryOwnedBySelf is currently simplified and defers peer claim/legacy checks. Python parity includes claimed_same_actor peer IDs and legacy sync actor IDs. This can misclassify ownership for merged/claimed peer memories and affect behavior depending on owned-vs-other checks. Port full parity logic and add targeted tests.

We now enforce outbound filtering on /v1/ops, but inbound policy remains implicit. Define intended semantics for include/exclude in both directions and implement/enforce server-side receive filtering if required, with tests and docs updates.
