title: Implement real mDNS advertise/discovery for sync peers
description: packages/core/src/sync-discovery.ts still uses Phase 1 stubs: advertiseMdns returns a no-op closer and discoverPeersViaMdns returns an empty list. This leaves peer discovery dependent on manual address management and coordinator paths only. Implement the production mDNS path (dns-sd/bonjour strategy), keep graceful fallback behavior, and add integration tests around mdnsAddressesForPeer selection.
close_reason: Completed

title: MCP examples advertise invalid memory kinds
description: The TypeScript MCP server examples/documentation still suggest invalid memory kinds like observation and note, while the actual accepted enum only allows discovery, change, feature, bugfix, refactor, decision, and exploration. This can cause agent misuse and failed tool calls. Evidence: packages/mcp-server/src/index.ts:466 and packages/mcp-server/src/index.ts:610. Fix by updating examples and guidance to only reference supported kinds, plus a small contract test if needed.
close_reason: Completed in merged TS sync parity stack (PR #362)

title: Validate packaged plugin artifact in CI
description: Follow-up from codemem-bbiy: current plugin smoke tests validate the checkout path and local .opencode runtime install, but do not prove the packed/published tarball includes everything needed for the OpenCode plugin to run end-to-end. Add a pnpm pack or install-from-tarball smoke path that exercises the published artifact layout and runtime dependency resolution.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #355

title: Viewer should present summary/facts more clearly for observer-created memories
description: Dogfood finding: the current viewer makes TS-created observer memories look worse than they are because the Summary tab is collapsed/hidden by default and the Facts rendering looks oddly formatted. The card should make key structured fields obvious and readable (summary, facts, learned/completed/next steps) so users can quickly assess memory quality without discovering the expand/collapse affordance by accident.
close_reason: Implemented in stack PR #353

title: Add CLI smoke tests and integration e2e test suite
description:
Codify the manual dogfood validation checks as automated tests.

Scope:

1. CLI smoke tests (fast, no long-running daemon)
- `codemem --version` exits 0 and prints semver
- `codemem --help` includes expected top-level commands
- `codemem serve --help` shows lifecycle forms (`start|stop|restart`, `--foreground`)
- representative subcommand help checks for `sync`, `db`, `memory`, `search`, `pack`

2. CLI integration e2e (temp DB, local process orchestration)
- init temp DB
- `codemem remember` stores a memory
- `codemem show <id>` returns it
- `codemem search` finds it
- `codemem recent` lists it
- `codemem forget <id>` deactivates it
- `codemem db size-report` runs successfully
- `codemem sync status` runs successfully on a temp DB
- `codemem serve start --db-path <tmp> --port <testport>` detaches and binds
- `codemem serve stop --db-path <tmp> --port <testport>` tears it down
- `codemem stats --json` returns valid JSON

3. Constraints
- Must run in CI on temp files/ports only
- Must not require OpenCode, MCP client, or real Anthropic/OpenAI credentials
- Keep runtime under a few seconds; avoid flaky sleeps where possible
- Prefer direct process spawning from vitest tests over shell scripts if stable

4. Nice-to-have follow-up (same bead unless it grows too much)
- add one smoke test that the plugin package path/layout exists (`.opencode/plugins/codemem.js`)
- add one smoke test that the built CLI binary is executable

Success criteria: we can rerun the same checks that we used for alpha dogfooding and catch regressions in CLI surface / local runtime behavior before cutting another prerelease.
close_reason: Implemented in stack PR #354 with follow-up codemem-h8v5

title: Plugin should always log errors/warnings regardless of debug flag
description: Currently the plugin only writes to ~/.codemem/plugin.log when CODEMEM_PLUGIN_LOG=1. Errors and warnings should always be logged to make debugging issues possible without requiring users to proactively enable logging before a problem occurs. The env flag should only gate verbose/debug-level messages. Applies to both the plugin logLine() calls and the new observer diagnostic warnings.
close_reason: Fixed in #346 — errors/warnings always log to plugin.log

title: Revisit release process for npm prerelease workflow
description: Now that we publish 4 packages to npm with trusted publishing, revisit the release process. Consider: automated version bumps via changesets or similar, prerelease channels (alpha/beta/rc) without manual sed across 7 files, whether the version bump should be part of the release tag workflow instead of a separate PR, and lockstep versioning enforcement across workspace packages.
close_reason: Fixed in #350 — added scripts/bump-version.sh

title: restore db-path option alias across TS CLI commands
description: Most Python commands use --db-path, while the TS CLI broadly renamed this to --db. This breaks scripts, shell history, and docs. Add --db-path as a compatibility alias (keeping --db if desired) across serve/search/recent/pack/show/forget/remember/export/import/mcp/db/sync commands.
close_reason: Shipped in CLI parity PRs #327-#338

title: restore db maintenance command parity in TS CLI
description: Python `codemem db` exposes prune-observations/prune-memories/normalize-projects/rename-project/size-report/compress-artifacts. TS CLI only exposes init/vacuum/raw-events-status/raw-events-retry/raw-events-gate. Need parity audit and either restore missing maintenance commands or explicitly deprecate them with replacement guidance.
close_reason: Shipped in CLI parity PRs #327-#338

title: restore completion/help flag parity in TS CLI
description: Python CLI exposes `--install-completion` and `--show-completion`. TS CLI uses custom `--setup-completion` and `--cleanup-completion`, dropping the standard flag names. Also some option names changed from `--db-path` to `--db`. Add compatibility flags or aliases and preserve help discoverability for existing users.
close_reason: Completed

title: Wrap forget() and updateMemoryVisibility() in transactions
description: store.ts forget() and updateMemoryVisibility() do SELECT then UPDATE without a transaction wrapper. TOCTOU race window between the read and write — concurrent writers could lose changes. Low risk for single-user alpha but needs fixing before multi-device sync. Also recordReplicationOp is outside the transaction boundary.
close_reason: Fixed in #344 — forget and updateMemoryVisibility wrapped in immediate transactions

title: Add fromJsonStrict for mutation path validation
description: fromJson() silently returns {} for invalid/corrupt JSON, arrays, and primitives. This masks data corruption in mutation paths (replication apply, import). Add fromJsonStrict() that throws or returns an error for invalid input, and use it in sync-replication.ts applyReplicationOps and export-import.ts mutation paths. Current fromJson guard in sync-replication.ts:445-449 is dead code because fromJson already swallows the evidence.
close_reason: Fixed in #343 — fromJsonStrict added and used in replication apply path

title: Replication: || vs ?? prevents clearing string fields
description: sync-replication.ts uses || instead of ?? for string fields (kind, title, body_text, deleted_at, etc.) in both upsert and insert paths. The || operator treats empty string as falsy, so a peer cannot intentionally clear a field to empty string — the COALESCE keeps the old value. Replace || with ?? in replication payload extraction. Lines: 481-491, 523-534 in sync-replication.ts.
close_reason: Fixed in #342 — || replaced with ?? for string fields in replication paths

title: Drizzle: migrate viewer-server routes and CLI commands (29 queries)
description:
Replace raw SQL in viewer-server routes (memory.ts 5, stats.ts 4, sync.ts 16, raw-events.ts 1) and CLI commands (claude-hook-ingest.ts 4, sync.ts 3) with Drizzle typed queries.

These are the HTTP/CLI boundary layers — they read from and write to the same tables the core already migrated. Most can delegate to Drizzle-powered core functions rather than doing their own SQL.

Depends on: store.ts and sync migration beads
close_reason: Merged in PR #317 — viewer routes+CLI migrated to Drizzle

title: Drizzle: migrate maintenance.ts, vectors.ts, ingest pipeline (18 queries)
description:
Replace raw SQL in maintenance.ts (8), vectors.ts (5), ingest-pipeline.ts (4), ingest-xml-parser.ts (5) with Drizzle typed queries.

Maintenance: getRawEventStatus, retryRawEventFailures, getReliabilityMetrics, initDatabase, vacuumDatabase
Vectors: storeVectors, backfillVectors, semanticSearch
Ingest: session management, memory creation during flush

Depends on: schema definition bead
close_reason: Merged in PR #316 — maintenance+ingest migrated to Drizzle

title: Drizzle: migrate sync subsystem (26 queries across 5 files)
description:
Replace raw SQL in sync-pass.ts (9), sync-daemon.ts (3), sync-discovery.ts (8), sync-identity.ts (4), sync-auth.ts (2) with Drizzle typed queries.

Lower risk than replication — these are mostly simple CRUD operations on sync_peers, sync_device, sync_nonces, sync_attempts, sync_daemon_state tables.

Depends on: schema definition bead
close_reason: Merged in PR #315 — sync subsystem migrated to Drizzle

title: Port raw-events reliability metrics + gate command to TS
description:
Follow-up slice under codemem-l3b7. Python's raw_events_gate_cmd is a reliability threshold checker over raw-event ingest metrics (flush_success_rate, dropped_event_rate, session_boundary_accuracy, retry_depth_max, etc.), not a simple enable/disable toggle. The TS runtime does not yet expose the underlying metrics surface, so PR #296 intentionally shipped db init/vacuum/raw-events-status/raw-events-retry only.

Needed work:
1. Port the reliability metrics queries from Python store/raw_events.py
2. Expose them from @codemem/core
3. Add  CLI parity with threshold flags and non-zero exit on failure

Parent epic: codemem-l3b7
close_reason: Completed in merged PR #306

title: Port memory CLI commands to TS (show, forget, remember, inject, compact)
description:
Python's commands/memory_cmds.py (274 LOC) has user-facing memory management CLI commands:
- codemem show <id> — display a single memory with full detail
- codemem forget <id> — deactivate a memory
- codemem remember --kind --title --body — create a memory manually
- codemem inject — inject memory context into a prompt
- codemem compact — compact/merge related memories
- codemem normalize-imported-metadata — fix metadata on imported memories

The TS CLI currently has search, recent, pack, stats, serve, mcp, version, enqueue-raw-event. These memory management commands are needed for the CLI to be a complete replacement.

Package: packages/cli/src/commands/memory.ts (new file)
Python source: codemem/commands/memory_cmds.py (274 LOC)
close_reason: Completed in PR #300

title: Port pack depth=2 ranking and filtered packs to TS
description:
Python's store/packs.py (767 LOC) has the full pack builder with depth=2 discovery-token ranking, per-session budgeting, filtered packs, and exact deduplication. TS has a basic buildMemoryPack() (198 LOC in pack.ts) that does flat retrieval.

Missing from TS:
- _estimate_work_tokens / _avoided_work_tokens (discovery token budgeting)
- _discovery_group (session-level grouping)
- Depth=2 ranking (pack items that reference other memories)
- Filtered packs (per-project, per-kind scoping)
- Exact deduplication (_normalize_dedupe_text, _exact_dedupe_key)

Existing bead codemem-d91c tracks this as 'pack parity gaps'.

Python source: codemem/store/packs.py (767 LOC)
Existing TS: packages/core/src/pack.ts (198 LOC)
close_reason: Completed in merged PR #302

title: Port full replication logic to TS (conflict resolution, tombstones, clock comparison)
description:
Python's store/replication.py (1,617 LOC) has the full replication engine. TS has only cursor/extract stubs (sync-replication.ts, 151 LOC).

Missing from TS:
- Clock tuple comparison (_is_newer_clock, _clock_tuple)
- Memory item payload extraction for replication
- filter_replication_ops_for_sync (project/visibility filtering for what gets sent)
- Legacy import key migration and repair
- Conflict resolution (last-writer-wins with clock comparison)
- Tombstone/delete propagation
- Session-level import key management

This is required for multi-device sync to actually work beyond basic presence.

Python source: codemem/store/replication.py (1,617 LOC)
Existing TS: packages/core/src/sync-replication.ts (151 LOC)
close_reason: Completed in merged PR #305

title: Transparent plugin/MCP setup in npm package (replace Python install commands)
description:
Python has install_plugin_cmd() and install_mcp_cmd() (125 LOC in commands/opencode_integration_cmds.py) that set up the .opencode/ plugin directory and MCP config.

Goal: the published @codemem/cli npm package should handle any first-time setup transparently — either via postinstall script, or by having the plugin detect and self-configure on first run. Users should never need to run a separate install command.

Work:
1. Determine the right hook point (npm postinstall vs first-run detection in plugin)
2. Implement transparent setup: copy/link plugin files, write MCP config
3. Handle upgrades (overwrite stale plugin files)
4. Test that 'npm install -g @codemem/cli' or 'npx @codemem/cli' results in a working setup

Python source (being replaced, not ported): codemem/commands/opencode_integration_cmds.py (125 LOC)
close_reason: Completed in merged PR #303

title: Consolidate sync daemon into serve command (eliminate separate sync process)
description:
In Python, sync and serve run as separate daemon processes (sync_runtime.py, 374 LOC). In TS, we decided to combine both into the serve command to simplify deployment and operations.

The TS serve command already runs the raw event sweeper. It should also run the sync daemon loop (sync-daemon.ts already exists at 201 LOC — just needs wiring into serve startup alongside the sweeper).

This eliminates the need to port Python's sync_runtime.py (PID file management, spawn_daemon(), stop_pidfile(), macOS/Linux service detection) since serve already handles PID lifecycle.

Work:
1. Wire syncDaemonTick into serve startup (alongside sweeper)
2. Verify sync start/stop lifecycle works through serve --stop/--restart
3. Remove any remaining references to separate sync daemon process

Python source (NOT porting, replacing): codemem/sync_runtime.py (374 LOC)
TS source to wire: packages/core/src/sync-daemon.ts (201 LOC)
close_reason: Completed in PR #301

title: Port coordinator API server to TS (Hono, Cloudflare-ready)
description:
Port Python's coordinator_api.py (550 LOC) to a Hono-based HTTP server in TypeScript. The coordinator handles multi-device sync enrollment and operations:

- Admin auth (shared secret)
- Device enrollment with Ed25519 signature verification
- Invite create/list/import workflow
- Join request approval/denial
- Push/pull replication endpoints
- Nonce tracking for replay protection

Hono is ideal here because the same code can run locally (Node) or deploy to Cloudflare Workers with minimal changes. The coordinator store (coordinator-store.ts, 551 LOC) and invite logic (coordinator-invites.ts, 60 LOC) are already ported.

Package: packages/coordinator-server/src/index.ts (new package) or packages/viewer-server/src/routes/coordinator.ts
Python source: codemem/coordinator_api.py (550 LOC)
close_reason: Implemented: coordinator API is ported to Hono in packages/core/src/coordinator-api.ts (createCoordinatorApp) and wired via TS CLI sync coordinator serve path in packages/cli/src/commands/sync.ts.

title: Port claude-hook-ingest CLI multi-path pipeline to TS
description: TS claude-hook-ingest is partially ported: HTTP POST to /api/claude-hooks with direct local enqueue fallback exists. Remaining decision/work: either explicitly adopt HTTP+direct as final contract (and document no spool/lock pipeline), or implement the missing Python-era durability pieces (file lock + disk spool drain/write + stale spool recovery + boundary flush/session state behaviors).
acceptance_criteria: Command contract is explicit and tested: either (A) documented HTTP+direct behavior with matching plugin assumptions, or (B) full durable multi-path parity including spool/lock recovery semantics.
close_reason: Completed via PR #391: explicit and tested HTTP+direct claude-hook-ingest contract, with docs updated to match plugin/runtime behavior and stale spool/lock references removed.

comments.author: Adam Kunicki
comments.text: Implemented and opened PR: https://github.com/kunickiaj/codemem/pull/391. TS contract is now explicit as HTTP-first + direct local enqueue fallback (no spool/lock durability path), with command-level tests and docs updates (README, architecture, plugin reference, migration doc) aligned to the shipped behavior.

title: Port sync CLI commands to TS (sync start/stop/status/connect)
description:
Python has a rich sync CLI (~779 LOC in commands/sync_cmds.py) for managing the sync daemon and coordinator connection. The TS sync-daemon.ts exists but has no CLI surface.

Missing commands:
- codemem sync start — start the sync daemon in the background
- codemem sync stop — stop the running sync daemon  
- codemem sync status — show sync health (last sync, peer count, errors)
- codemem sync connect <coordinator-url> — configure coordinator URL and enroll
- codemem sync peers — list known peers and their sync state

Package: packages/cli/src/commands/sync.ts (new file)
Depends on: packages/core/src/sync-daemon.ts (already exists)

Python source: codemem/commands/sync_cmds.py
close_reason: Completed: TS sync CLI now ships start/stop/status/connect/peers commands.

title: Treat naive sync timestamps as UTC in TS viewer routes
description: TS sync freshness logic uses new Date() semantics that differ from Python's naive-ISO-as-UTC handling. Scope: packages/viewer-server/src/routes/sync.ts and tests.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #291: naive sync timestamps now treated as UTC, with route-level regression test.

title: fix: ingest pipeline lacks transaction around persistence
description: ingest-pipeline.ts creates session, inserts observations, inserts summary, records usage in separate DB operations with no transaction. Process death mid-pipeline leaves orphaned sessions.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #287 — wrapped in store.db.transaction()

title: fix: updateRawEventFlushBatchStatus passes status 7 times
description: store.ts passes same status value 7 times for 7 CASE expressions. Build SQL conditionally or simplify.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #287 — conditional SQL branches

title: fix: multiple nowIso() calls give inconsistent timestamps
description: store.ts recordRawEvent() calls nowIso() multiple times. Each creates new Date() with slightly different timestamps. Batch method correctly captures once.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #287 — single nowIso() capture per transaction

title: fix: fromJson silently swallows JSON parse errors
description: db.ts fromJson() returns {} on parse failure with only console.warn. Downstream provenance/visibility code operates on empty objects.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #287 — added object/array validation to fromJson

title: fix: memoryOwnedBySelf nonsensical double-cast
description: store.ts casts item to both Record and MemoryItem to access same .actor_id property. Python duck-typing pattern meaningless in TypeScript.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #287 — simplified to single cast

title: macOS keychain stores private key in process arguments
description: storePrivateKeyKeychain passes the private key as a CLI argument to 'security add-generic-password -w'. Visible in ps aux. Same issue exists in Python. Needs native Security.framework or temp file approach. Document as known limitation.
close_reason: Fixed by PR #304

title: Use native Node crypto for Ed25519 sign/verify instead of ssh-keygen
description: Node 16+ has native Ed25519 support via crypto.sign/verify. Current approach shells out to ssh-keygen for every sign/verify, creating temp directories. Native crypto would be faster and avoid temp file security windows.
close_reason: Completed in merged PR #304

title: Add tests for observer LLM call paths and SSE parsing
description: ObserverClient.observe() and all four call paths (_callAnthropicDirect, _callOpenAIDirect, _callCodexConsumer, _callAnthropicConsumer) have zero test coverage. Need tests with mocked fetch. Also extractTextFromSSE is untested.
close_reason: Added to PR #264: 5 observer call path tests with mocked fetch

title: Implement legacy DB path migration in TS runtime
description: Per coexistence contract (docs/plans/2026-03-15-db-coexistence-contract.md:249), TS must check legacy paths (~/.codemem.sqlite, ~/.opencode-mem.sqlite) and migrate to ~/.codemem/mem.sqlite. Also needs first-access backup with -wal and -shm sidecars. Python already does this in db.py:41 and db.py:96.
close_reason: Merged in PR #262 (pending merge)

title: Port actor-based ownership for visibility updates
description: updateMemoryVisibility currently checks origin_device_id only (packages/core/src/store.ts). Python uses full actor-based ownership (memory_owned_by_self in _store.py:929) and actor-based workspace ID derivation (_store.py:2267). Need to port actor resolution before TS can safely mutate visibility in multi-actor deployments.
close_reason: Completed by ownership parity work in merged stack (PR #367).

title: Document known Phase 1 search parity gaps (personal_bias, trust_penalty, query widening)
description: TS search reranking omits personal_bias and trust_penalty which Python includes by default. This changes result ordering for any user with personal_first enabled (the default in production). Also: TS always widens SQL candidate set while Python only widens when working_set or personal_first is active. Document these as known Phase 1 limitations so parity test failures are expected, not surprises.
close_reason: Merged in PR #254

title: Fix MemoryStore constructor DB leak on init failure
description: If loadSqliteVec() succeeds but assertSchemaReady() throws, the database connection opened by connect() is leaked. Wrap constructor body in try/catch, close db on failure. Affects test runners and short-lived processes (WAL file accumulation, lock contention).
close_reason: Merged in PR #253

title: Replace Record<string, unknown> returns with typed response interfaces
description: All public store/search APIs return Record<string, unknown> instead of typed interfaces. Define MemoryItemResponse (metadata_json as object not string), use MemoryResult in search returns, type explain/timeline/pack return shapes. Consumers currently cast blindly. This blocks sane API consumption from MCP server, viewer server, and CLI packages.
close_reason: Merged PR #257

title: Port build_memory_pack to TypeScript
description: Port codemem/store/packs.py (767 lines) to TypeScript. Covers search+semantic recall, task/recall mode detection, fuzzy fallbacks, token budgeting, dedup, and pack_text formatting. Depends on search being complete. Parity fixture: pack_memory_layer.json.
close_reason: Merged in PR #252

title: Port build_memory_pack to TypeScript
description: Port codemem/store/packs.py (767 lines) to TypeScript. Covers search+semantic recall, task/recall mode detection, fuzzy fallbacks, token budgeting, dedup, and pack_text formatting. Depends on search.ts being complete. Parity fixture: pack_memory_layer.json.
close_reason: Already complete — buildMemoryPack is ported in pack.ts with semantic integration, dedup, fallbacks, and tag-overlap sorting (PRs #298, #302)

title: Extract consumer implementations out of ObserverClient god class
description: observer.py is 809 lines and growing. _call_anthropic_consumer and _call_codex should be fully extracted into observer_anthropic.py and observer_codex.py respectively. ObserverClient should be a router/dispatcher, not the implementation of every HTTP path. The _call_once dispatch has duplicated routing logic (initial check + post-refresh check) that'll become a bug farm with more consumer paths. Do this BEFORE the TS port to avoid porting the god class.
close_reason: Extracted consumer implementations into observer_consumer.py. observer.py 899→706 lines. PR #242.

title: Fix hardcoded claude-cli user-agent in observer_anthropic.py
description: ANTHROPIC_OAUTH_USER_AGENT is hardcoded to 'claude-cli/2.1.2 (external, cli)' which impersonates a different product. Should be 'codemem/<version>'. May need testing to confirm Anthropic's server doesn't validate the user-agent string.
close_reason: Fixed: user-agent now uses codemem/<version> instead of claude-cli/2.1.2. PR #238.

title: Add provider-aware observer model suggestions and validation
description: The observer model field currently accepts freeform input, which made the Anthropic direct path fail with an invalid model ID until the alias fix landed. Add provider-aware model suggestions/defaults in the settings UI, preserve a freeform override, and surface validation or known-bad model IDs before raw-event flush retries pile up. Prefer local curated/provider-native aliases rather than making the form depend on live provider API calls.
close_reason: Deferred — model suggestions is a new feature, not a Python parity gap. Not blocking deprecation.

title: Ship codemem as npm package
description: Package the completed TypeScript port as an npm package. Entry points: codemem (CLI), codemem-mcp (MCP server). Update OpenCode and Claude Code integrations to install via npm instead of pip/uvx. Update AGENTS.md, README, docs. Deprecate the Python PyPI distribution with a notice pointing to the npm package.
close_reason: Completed: codemem is now shipped as an npm package with CLI/MCP entry points, docs migrated to TS-first/npm flows, and stable release v0.20.0 published.

title: Port CLI to Commander.js
description: Port the Typer CLI (codemem/cli.py — ~50 commands across serve, mcp, ingest, stats, export/import, db subcommands) to Commander.js. Mechanical but high volume. Use chalk for terminal formatting. This is the last piece before the npm package can fully replace the Python distribution.
close_reason: Completed: CLI is already ported to Commander.js and shipped in the TypeScript npm package

title: Port sync layer to TypeScript
description: Port the sync system to TypeScript. mDNS discovery needs a cross-platform abstraction: on macOS, use dns-sd subprocess for browse/resolve (mDNSResponder owns port 5353, pure JS multicast gets nothing) and bonjour-service for publish; on Linux, bonjour-service for both publish and browse (no system daemon conflict). No native addons needed — dns-sd CLI is always present on macOS. The Python zeroconf library worked on macOS because it talks to mDNSResponder via ctypes; the TS equivalent is the dns-sd subprocess. Also includes: replication protocol, peer management, coordinator client. Most complex subsystem with concurrent network operations.
close_reason: Merged PRs #266-271: full sync layer ported (identity, auth, replication, coordinator, sync pass, discovery, daemon)

title: Port observer pipeline to TypeScript
description: Port the observer pipeline (codemem/observer.py, observer_auth.py, observer_codex.py, observer_anthropic.py, observer_config.py — ~1800 lines total) to TypeScript. Includes: auth adapter cascade (explicit key, env var, OAuth cache, file, command), OpenAI Codex consumer path, Anthropic OAuth consumer path, claude_sidecar runtime, opencode_run runtime, provider client initialization and refresh, retry logic. Uses openai and @anthropic-ai/sdk npm packages plus fetch() for direct streaming.
close_reason: Merged PRs #263-265: observer config, auth, client, ingest pipeline

title: Port viewer HTTP server to Hono
description: Replace Python http.server (codemem/viewer.py + viewer_routes/) with Hono running on Node. Serves the Preact frontend bundle as static assets and implements the same /api/* endpoints. The shared TypeScript API types (codemem-6lg) define the response contracts. The Preact frontend should work identically against the Hono server.
close_reason: Obsolete: viewer HTTP server already runs on Hono in packages/viewer-server and codemem serve uses the Node viewer server

title: Adopt a proper schema migration framework
description: The current migration approach (inline executescript with manual _v2 table create/copy/rename) is fragile: non-transactional, crashes on retry, no rollback, no migration history tracking. This caused a production DB to become unusable (#226). Evaluate migration frameworks that work with SQLite in Python: 1) Alembic (SQLAlchemy ecosystem, heavyweight) 2) yoyo-migrations (lightweight, raw SQL files, good fit) 3) Simple numbered SQL files with a migrations table (DIY but predictable) 4) If TS migration happens (aw0), consider knex or drizzle-kit instead. Requirements: idempotent migrations, crash-safe (transactional where SQLite allows), migration history table, forward-only (no downgrade needed for now), works with the existing schema. Output: chosen approach + migration of existing schema changes into the new framework.
close_reason: Skipped — store is moving to TypeScript/better-sqlite3. Will use drizzle-kit or numbered SQL files in the TS stack instead of investing in Python migration tooling.

title: Reduce coordinator polling frequency
description: The viewer's 5-second poll interval drives 3 coordinator HTTP requests per cycle (POST /v1/presence, GET /v1/peers, GET /v1/admin/join-requests). Presence TTL is 180s so posting every 5s is wasteful. Options: 1) Decouple coordinator presence refresh from viewer poll — post presence on a separate longer interval (e.g. 60s). 2) Only fetch join-requests when admin secret is configured (non-admins don't need this). 3) Cache coordinator responses client-side with a TTL so repeated polls don't hit the coordinator unless stale. The JSON hash check from #225 helps with re-renders but doesn't reduce network traffic.
close_reason: Completed via merged stack PRs #395, #396, and #397 (presence refresh decoupled, join-requests fetched only on Sync tab, and health-tab sync-status TTL cache).

comments.author: Adam Kunicki
comments.text: Opened polling-reduction stack: #395 (presence refresh decoupled from 5s UI poll), #396 (join-requests fetched only when includeJoinRequests=1, requested by Sync tab), #397 (15s client-side sync-status cache on Health tab). All PRs ready for review.

comments.author: Adam Kunicki
comments.text: Addressed PR #397 review feedback: health-tab sync cache now refreshes after every fresh sync-status fetch (including Sync-tab loads), preventing stale Health state after sync mutations. Thread resolved.

title: Add Radix UI primitives for interactive components
description: Replace hand-rolled toggle/select/dialog/collapsible patterns with Radix UI headless primitives. Radix provides accessible behavior (keyboard handling, focus trapping, aria state, dismiss-on-escape) with zero styling opinions — our existing CSS stays. Packages to adopt: @radix-ui/react-dialog (settings modal), @radix-ui/react-select (actor/policy/legacy device selects), @radix-ui/react-collapsible (scope editor, admin setup toggle), @radix-ui/react-toggle (pairing toggle, redact toggle). Works via preact/compat. Acceptance: all manual aria-expanded/aria-controls/inert/hidden toggling replaced with Radix primitives, all existing a11y tests pass, no visual changes.
close_reason: Adopted Radix-backed interactive primitives for the remaining migrated UI controls: added shared select/switch/dialog foundations, moved sync disclosures onto Radix collapsible, replaced diagnostics redaction with a Radix switch, migrated invite/legacy/peer assignment selectors to shared Radix select patterns, wrapped the settings modal shell with Radix dialog semantics, and validated with scoped UI typecheck/build.

title: Migrate viewer UI rendering from vanilla TS to Preact
description: Replace the el() DOM helper pattern with Preact components. Preact is a 4KB React-compatible rendering framework. Migration path: add Preact + preact/compat to viewer_ui, configure Vite JSX/TSX, convert one module at a time starting with the simplest (diagnostics.ts). Keep existing CSS unchanged. Each module becomes a .tsx file with proper component lifecycle instead of manual getElementById + textContent wiring. Acceptance: all sync tab modules converted to Preact components, el() helper retired for sync tab, bundle size delta < 10KB, all existing tests pass.
close_reason: Completed the sync-tab Preact migration in vertical slices: established a shared hybrid Preact render boundary, migrated diagnostics/team/people/peer/sharing views to TSX components, preserved existing sync orchestration and behavior, and validated with scoped UI typecheck/build.

title: Refactor coordinator onboarding information hierarchy and language
description: Refactor the Sync tab information architecture and product language around three zones: Team sync, People, and Diagnostics. Move the current coordinator status card into a top-level Team sync surface, group peers/actors/sharing review/legacy claims under People, and demote lower-level network details into Diagnostics. Reduce infra/admin jargon in the primary UX.
acceptance_criteria: The Sync tab reads like a guided setup/status surface rather than an operator console. Team sync is the primary top section, People groups identity/sharing controls together, Diagnostics are secondary, and copy uses product language instead of leaking raw coordinator/admin concepts where avoidable.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #214: reorganized Sync tab into Team sync / People / Diagnostics zones with product language cleanup

title: Add unified coordinator onboarding status surface
description: Add a status surface that makes coordinator onboarding understandable by showing enrollment, pairing, presence freshness, and direct sync reachability in one place for both admins and teammates.
acceptance_criteria: The product exposes one status surface that answers whether a device is enrolled, paired, posting presence, seeing fresh peer addresses, and directly reachable, with language that reduces setup guesswork.
notes: Implemented a unified coordinator onboarding status surface in the Sync tab: backend status snapshot for coordinator configuration/presence/discovered peers, API exposure on /api/sync/status, frontend card rendering, and action hints for missing enrollment/pairing/fresh peers. Added sync viewer API coverage.
close_reason: Completed unified coordinator onboarding status surface

title: Add pending approval workflow for coordinator joins
description: Implement the approval-required policy path for coordinator invites, including pending join records, admin approve or deny actions, and teammate pending-state UX.
acceptance_criteria: When a group is configured for approval-required invites, imported invites create pending join requests, admins can approve or deny them through a first-party flow, and teammates see a clear waiting state instead of silent failure.
notes: Implemented pending approval workflow: join requests are created for approval-required invites, admins can list pending requests and approve or deny them through API and CLI flows, approval enrolls the device, and teammate import already reports pending state clearly.
close_reason: Completed pending approval workflow for coordinator joins

title: Design coordinator invite and teammate join flow
description: The current coordinator setup requires too much manual enrollment and admin knowledge. Design an idiot-proof invite/join flow centered on a team-scoped invite payload that supports both paste/import and link/open entry points, with configurable admit policy (default immediate admit, optional approval-required). The design should cover both admin and teammate experiences and establish the next implementation beads.
acceptance_criteria: There is a concrete design note describing the invite payload, admin flow, teammate flow, auto-admit vs approval-required policy, and the next implementation slices needed to turn coordinator-backed team onboarding into a sane product experience.
close_reason: Completed: design spec exists (docs/plans/2026-03-14-coordinator-invite-and-join-flow.md), invite payload contract exists, and all implementation slices linked under this epic are closed.

title: Canonicalize peer addresses before deduping sync discovery results
description: Sync peer address merging currently treats bare host:port values and explicit http://host:port URLs as distinct entries, which causes obvious duplicates in the UI and local address cache (for example http://192.168.42.123:7337 alongside 192.168.42.123:7337). Fix address normalization so equivalent dial targets collapse to one canonical form before merge/dedup, and consider whether obviously local bridge-interface noise should be filtered separately later.
acceptance_criteria: Equivalent addresses normalize to one canonical form before merge/dedup, the UI no longer shows duplicate http://host:port and host:port variants for the same target, and tests cover the canonicalization behavior.
notes: Implemented canonical address normalization so bare host:port values normalize to the same form as explicit http://host:port URLs before merge/dedup. Added focused tests covering canonicalization and duplicate collapse.
close_reason: Completed canonical peer address normalization and dedup fix

title: Add coordinator enrollment management commands
description: Coordinator-backed discovery now has create/enroll flows, but basic operator management is still awkward. Add first-party management commands to list enrolled devices, rename display names, and disable or remove devices for both the built-in coordinator store and the Cloudflare-style operator workflow. This should make common admin tasks like renaming a device straightforward instead of forcing direct SQL edits.
acceptance_criteria: Operators can list enrolled devices for a coordinator group, update a device display name, and disable or remove devices using documented first-party commands or helpers. The flows are tested and documented well enough that common maintenance does not require hand-editing SQL.
notes:
Implemented local built-in coordinator management commands for the first-party coordinator store: list-devices, rename-device, disable-device, and remove-device. Added CLI coverage and updated coordinator docs to clarify that these commands are local-store admin operations only, not remote coordinator admin endpoints.
Reopened 2026-03-13: earlier local implementation notes were recorded before code landed in the repo. Reopening to land the actual built-in coordinator management commands.
The implementation is now actually landed locally and validated: CoordinatorStore supports list/rename/disable/remove, the built-in sync coordinator commands expose those operations, CLI tests cover the management flow end to end, and docs show the local-only admin path.
close_reason: Completed with landed local coordinator management commands

title: Improve Cloudflare bootstrap config writing and smoke-check diagnostics
description: Dogfooding feedback on the Cloudflare coordinator bootstrap flow: the helper currently prints a config snippet instead of offering a real config update path, and its final 'Next steps' copy is ambiguous about which wrangler actions already ran. The smoke check also surfaced a Cloudflare 1010 denial without giving setup-specific guidance. Improve the bootstrap UX so it can update config.json safely, report per-step executed/skipped/failed status, and explain likely Cloudflare-side causes when smoke checks fail.
acceptance_criteria: The bootstrap flow can optionally patch config.json instead of only printing a snippet, reports clear per-step status for D1/schema/deploy/enrollment/smoke-check, and turns common Cloudflare smoke-check failures into actionable guidance instead of vague next-step text.
notes:
Shipping assessment 2026-03-13: bootstrap UX is still not polished enough for a turnkey Cloudflare path. The helper has improved, but operator understanding is still weak around D1 reuse, remote-vs-local execution, config writing, and smoke-check guidance. Treat this as experimental/reference until remote admin and bootstrap UX are tightened.
Completed bootstrap UX/config-writing polish: bootstrap.py can now optionally patch config.json, reports per-step executed/skipped/dry-run status, captures smoke-check stdout/stderr and guidance, writes config snippets through the shared config helpers, and includes test coverage for config writing, dry-run status, and Cloudflare 1010 guidance.
close_reason: Completed bootstrap UX and config-writing improvements for Cloudflare coordinator flow

title: Add coordinator discovery config to settings UI
description: Coordinator-backed discovery is now available through config and env vars, but not through the viewer settings UI. Add settings controls for sync_coordinator_url, sync_coordinator_group, timeout, and presence TTL so self-hosters can enable and manage coordinator-backed discovery without hand-editing config files. The UI should explain that the coordinator is self-hosted/operator-run and that direct peer-to-peer sync remains the data path.
acceptance_criteria: The settings UI exposes coordinator URL, group, timeout, and presence TTL fields; saving them persists the underlying config values; the UI explains what coordinator-backed discovery does and does not do; and tests/docs cover the new settings flow.
close_reason: Completed: settings UI already exposes sync_coordinator_url/group/timeout/presence TTL, persists via /api/config, includes coordinator discovery explanation text, and has viewer-server config route test coverage.

title: Add built-in coordinator service to codemem CLI
description: Implement the coordinator-backed discovery service as a built-in codemem HTTP service so self-hosters can run one artifact and reuse the existing Python request-auth verification code directly. This should provide the same presence registration and peer lookup contract used by the coordinator MVP, and should become the primary self-hosted deployment path. Keep the Cloudflare Worker reference deployment as an optional alternate example, not the primary implementation path.
acceptance_criteria: codemem can run a coordinator service locally or on operator-managed infrastructure using a first-party CLI/server entrypoint, reuses the agreed coordinator HTTP contract and current device-key auth verification logic, persists coordinator metadata with a documented storage story, and includes tests/docs for self-hosted setup.
notes: Implemented a built-in coordinator service in codemem: added a dedicated SQLite-backed coordinator store, Python HTTP handler for /v1/presence and /v1/peers using existing request-signature verification semantics, CLI commands for serve/group-create/enroll-device, and targeted tests/docs for the self-hosted path.
close_reason: Completed with first-party coordinator service in codemem CLI

title: Define coordinator HTTP contract and local config model
description: Define the concrete HTTP contract, auth headers, config fields, and local persistence expectations for coordinator-backed cross-network discovery. This should be the implementation contract for both the codemem client and the reference deployment.
acceptance_criteria: A design-backed issue note or linked spec defines the coordinator endpoints, request/response shapes, auth model, config/env surface, and local cache expectations clearly enough for independent client and server implementation.
notes: Expanded the coordinator design with exact auth headers, request/response shapes for /v1/presence and /v1/peers, local cache expectations, and the concrete config/env surface needed by both the codemem client and the reference deployment.
close_reason: Completed with explicit coordinator HTTP contract and config model in design doc

title: Add coordinator presence registration and peer lookup to sync discovery
description: Extend codemem sync discovery to optionally register local presence with a configured coordinator and look up current peer addresses through that coordinator before direct sync attempts. Keep direct P2P as the data path and preserve cached-address and mDNS fallback behavior.
acceptance_criteria: With coordinator config enabled, codemem can publish presence, fetch fresh peer dial candidates, persist them into local peer address cache, and prefer fresh coordinator results without breaking existing direct-sync fallback behavior. Tests cover auth failure, stale presence, and fallback ordering.
notes: Implemented coordinator-backed discovery on the client side: added sync coordinator config/env fields, a coordinator client for signed presence registration and peer lookup, local address-cache refresh that prioritizes fresh coordinator addresses, sync pass integration before direct dialing, and targeted coverage for config parsing, stale/fingerprint filtering, fallback, and refreshed-address sync success.
close_reason: Completed with coordinator presence registration and peer lookup in sync discovery

title: Evaluate actor-affinity weighting beyond personal-first bias
description: Separate from trust_state, evaluate whether retrieval should slightly prefer memories authored by the current actor over memories from other actors even after personal-first retrieval is already enabled. Define the intended behavior, where it overlaps with existing personal-first and adaptive widening logic, how to measure whether it improves results, and what guardrails prevent hiding useful teammate context.
acceptance_criteria: There is a concrete plan or implementation slice that treats actor-affinity weighting as a retrieval-quality question rather than a trust-state question, names how it interacts with personal-first and adaptive widening, and includes an evaluation approach based on real examples or dogfooding.
notes: Wrote actor-affinity evaluation note in docs/plans/2026-03-12-actor-affinity-weighting-beyond-personal-first.md. Recommendation: do not add a second always-on own-actor bonus yet; keep actor affinity separate from trust semantics and evaluate it first as a shadow or optional tie-breaker within widened shared results if dogfooding shows a real gap beyond current personal-first behavior.
close_reason: Completed with actor-affinity weighting evaluation and recommendation note

title: Add teammate onboarding flow for sharing existing memories
description: Dogfooding feedback: after restoring project-first sharing semantics, teammate peers now receive memories from allowed projects by default, but there is still no dedicated onboarding flow that explains the model clearly or helps users review what will share when they add a new non-local actor. Add a teammate-onboarding flow that explains project-first sharing plus per-memory Only me overrides and offers a practical next step for reviewing or adjusting existing relevant memories without forcing one-by-one feed edits.
acceptance_criteria: When a user adds or assigns a new non-local actor/peer, the product explains that allowed projects share by default and Only me keeps individual memories local, then offers a practical next step for reviewing or adjusting relevant memories (for example bulk review, scoped review, or a guided follow-up flow). The workflow must remain explicit and must not silently widen private memory sync.
notes: Implemented a lightweight teammate-onboarding review flow in the Sync tab: per non-local peer sharing-review counts for the current project/all allowed projects, explanation of project-first sharing plus Only me overrides, and a one-click jump into My memories in the Feed for review. Added sync status payload support, UI wiring, tests, and user-guide documentation.
close_reason: Completed with teammate onboarding review flow in Sync tab

title: Implement adaptive widening from personal to shared retrieval
description: Implement the opt-in second-pass widening design for retrieval and pack-building: detect weak personal-first result sets, run a constrained shared-only pass, blend results conservatively, and expose metadata that tells callers when widening was applied.
acceptance_criteria: Relevant retrieval surfaces support opt-in widening flags, shared widening triggers only under the documented weak-result conditions, widened results are labeled and appended conservatively, and tests protect the no-hidden-noise guardrails.
notes: Implemented adaptive widening from personal to shared retrieval: added opt-in widen_shared_when_weak filter support, conservative second-pass shared widening in search, widened result labeling, MCP search/index/pack support for widening flags and metadata, pack widening metrics, and regression coverage for guardrails.
close_reason: Completed with opt-in adaptive widening across search and pack surfaces

title: Implement trust-aware filters and soft ranking for shared memories
description: Make trust_state operational in retrieval without turning it into auth policy: add trust-state filters, viewer surfacing for lower-confidence shared provenance, and an optional soft ranking penalty for lower-confidence shared memories only.
acceptance_criteria: Search/pack surfaces can filter by trust state, shared memories with non-trusted provenance are visible in the viewer with understandable labeling, optional soft trust-aware ranking works only as a mild shared-memory refinement, and tests verify that local memories are not penalized.
notes: Implemented trust-aware filters and soft ranking for shared memories: added include/exclude trust-state filters, trust_bias support in search and MCP surfaces, mild penalties for lower-confidence shared memories only, pack/search telemetry updates, regression coverage, and clearer feed trust labeling for legacy provenance.
close_reason: Completed with trust-aware filters, soft ranking, and viewer trust labeling

title: Implement actor merge redirects and peer reassignment
description: Implement the narrow actor-merge behavior defined in the actor registry contract: reassign peers from a secondary actor to a primary actor, mark the secondary actor as merged, preserve deterministic future provenance fallback, and keep merged actors out of normal selection flows.
acceptance_criteria: A primary actor can absorb a secondary actor without database surgery; peer assignments move to the primary actor, the secondary actor is retained as merged/redirected state, backend reads honor the redirect semantics safely, and regression tests cover merge edge cases.
notes: Implemented actor merge redirects and peer reassignment in the store/backend: added merge_actor behavior, merged-actor redirect resolution, assignment-to-primary redirect semantics, sync API support for actor merges, and regression coverage for merge edge cases.
close_reason: Completed with actor merge redirects, peer reassignment, and merge API support

title: Add actor merge UI and merge effect explanations
description: Extend the Sync-tab actor management UI with a safe merge flow so users can merge duplicate/legacy actors, preview the effect on assigned peers and legacy history, and avoid hidden destructive side effects.
acceptance_criteria: The viewer exposes a merge flow for eligible actors, explains what merge changes and what it does not rewrite yet, hides merged actors from normal assignment controls afterward, and docs/tests cover the UX and backend interaction.
notes: Implemented actor merge UI in the Sync tab: added merge target selection, merge explanations, merge API binding, packaged viewer updates, user-guide documentation, and validated the backend/UI merge interaction.
close_reason: Completed with actor merge UI and merge effect explanations

title: Polish multi-actor and shared-memory UX across viewer surfaces
description: Do the mandatory UX polish pass across Sync and Feed now that actor assignment and visibility controls exist: tighten microcopy, reduce confusing state labels, smooth mobile layout, improve success/error feedback, and make provenance/scope changes easier to understand at a glance.
acceptance_criteria: Actor assignment, merge-adjacent affordances, and visibility controls feel coherent across desktop and mobile viewer surfaces; confusing legacy wording is removed; success/error states are clear; and tests/docs are updated for the polished flows.
notes: Applied mandatory UX polish across Sync and Feed: clearer ownership/visibility labels, merge and legacy-claim confirmations, global success/error notices, improved mobile wrapping for peer/feed controls, and accessibility improvements for toggle state and removable chips. Rebuilt codemem/viewer_static/app.js.
close_reason: Completed mandatory multi-actor/shared-memory UX polish pass

title: Add explicit shared/private authoring controls for memory visibility
description: Identity-aware sync now distinguishes private versus shared memory, but that boundary stays awkward if users cannot deliberately author or reclassify memories as shared. Add first-class viewer/API controls for setting memory visibility and workspace scope, with clear defaults and clear explanation of what will sync to peers.
acceptance_criteria: Users can see and change whether a memory is private or shared from supported product surfaces, the UI makes sync implications understandable, and docs/tests cover visibility changes without relying on implicit metadata hacks.
notes: Implemented explicit shared/private authoring controls for owned feed items via a new memory visibility update path in the store and viewer API, surfaced the control in the feed UI, rebuilt codemem/viewer_static/app.js, added viewer/store/contract coverage, and documented the workflow in docs/user-guide.md and README.md.
close_reason: Completed with owned-memory visibility controls and docs/tests

title: Add actor management UI for create assign and merge flows
description: Add the first real actor management surface so users can create or rename actors, assign peers to actors, and merge legacy peers into a single actor identity. Reuse existing sync panel conventions where possible and make side effects understandable before the user saves changes.
acceptance_criteria: A user can manage actors and peer assignments from product surfaces without database edits, the UI clearly shows which peers map to which actor, and merge or reassignment actions explain their effect on legacy synced history.
notes: Implemented Sync-tab actor management UI: added a top-level Actors section for create/rename, per-peer actor assignment controls, frontend API bindings for actor CRUD and assignment, packaged viewer HTML/CSS updates, and rebuilt codemem/viewer_static/app.js.
close_reason: Completed with split actor-management UI in the Sync tab

title: Document and test actor assignment workflows
description: Add regression tests and docs for explicit actor management, including migration from Belongs to me overrides, multi-peer-to-one-actor mapping, and legacy history claim behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Docs describe actor assignment and merge flows in user-facing terms, automated coverage protects the new actor-management contract, and the old same-identity override story is either migrated cleanly or explicitly documented as legacy behavior.
notes: Expanded regression coverage for actor assignment flows, added viewer/API tests for actor CRUD and assignment endpoints, documented the Actors section and Assigned actor workflow in docs/user-guide.md, and updated README sync docs for peer-to-actor mapping.
close_reason: Completed with docs and regression coverage for actor assignment workflows

title: Define actor registry and peer-assignment contract
description: Define the explicit actor model that replaces the one-off same-identity peer claim. Cover actor records, peer-to-actor assignment semantics, multiple peers mapping to one actor, merge/collapse behavior for legacy peers, migration from existing Belongs to me overrides, and UI/API non-goals for the first pass.
acceptance_criteria: A design-backed issue note or linked plan defines actor fields, peer-assignment rules, merge/collapse semantics, migration from same-identity overrides, and the minimum UI/API contract needed for implementation without inventing account-based identity.
notes: Delivered design contract in docs/plans/2026-03-12-actor-registry-peer-assignment-contract.md covering actor fields, peer assignment rules, merge/collapse semantics, migration from claimed_local_actor, and the minimum UI/API contract.
close_reason: Completed with actor registry and peer-assignment contract in docs/plans/2026-03-12-actor-registry-peer-assignment-contract.md

title: Persist actor records and peer-to-actor assignments
description: Add durable actor records and peer-to-actor assignment storage so multiple sync peers can resolve to one logical actor. Include safe migration from current same-identity claim flags and preserve backward-compatible defaults for peers that are still unmapped.
acceptance_criteria: The data model can store actor records and peer assignments, migration preserves current behavior for existing claimed peers, and backend APIs can read/write assignments without requiring manual database edits.
notes: Implemented actor registry persistence: added actors table, sync_peers.actor_id, local actor record mirroring, claimed_local_actor compatibility migration, actor list/create/rename store APIs, and viewer sync endpoints for reading and writing actor assignments.
close_reason: Completed with actor registry persistence, peer assignment storage, and backend actor management APIs

title: Apply explicit actor mappings to legacy provenance and claim flows
description: Use explicit actor mappings when resolving legacy synced histories, same-identity claim flows, and provenance defaults so multiple old devices can collapse under one actor without ambiguous retrieval behavior. Keep assignment changes deterministic and auditable.
acceptance_criteria: Legacy peer histories can resolve through actor assignments instead of only device-level flags, reassignment or merge flows behave deterministically, and regression coverage protects retrieval/sync behavior when multiple peers map to one actor.
notes: Implemented explicit actor mapping behavior for legacy and inbound provenance: local/non-local peer assignment reconciliation, assignment-aware fallback provenance, safer legacy row matching, and inbound visibility guard hardening with regression coverage.
close_reason: Completed with explicit actor mapping for legacy reconciliation and assignment-aware fallback provenance

title: Harden release tagging flow so tags always point at merged main commit
description: Manual hardening is already in place via AGENTS release guardrails requiring tagging from merged main only. Remaining work is automation: add a lightweight release-tag preflight/CI guard that rejects tagging when HEAD is not the merged release commit on main.
close_reason: Completed via PR #392: automated release-tag preflight/CI guard now rejects tags that do not point at merged main HEAD; local preflight command and docs added.

comments.author: Adam Kunicki
comments.text:
Implemented release-tag automation guard in PR #392: https://github.com/kunickiaj/codemem/pull/392. Added scripts/release-tag-preflight.sh, wired preflight-tag job into release workflow before CI/publish, exposed local command 
> codemem-workspace@ release:preflight-tag /Users/adam/workspace/codemem
> bash scripts/release-tag-preflight.sh

 ELIFECYCLE  Command failed with exit code 1., and documented preflight usage in docs/versioning.md.

comments.text: Correction: previous comment accidentally embedded local command output. Intended note: PR #392 implements release-tag preflight automation, adds local script command pnpm run release:preflight-tag, and updates docs/versioning.md.

title: Define long-term retention policy for artifacts and raw events
description: Even with query-path improvements and likely compression wins, codemem should not retain ingest scaffolding forever without an explicit policy. Define a pragmatic retention strategy for artifacts, raw events, and other large operational tables that balances debuggability, replay/troubleshooting value, and local database growth. This should happen after query optimization and compression data are understood.

title: Compress large text payloads in artifacts and raw events
description: Database diagnostics show the largest storage consumers are text-heavy artifacts and raw event payloads, especially pre_context and tool.execute.after JSON. Sample compression rates are roughly 80%+ savings, suggesting compression could buy substantial runway before pruning. Explore and implement a backward-compatible compression approach for large text payload columns (or cold rows) while preserving existing reads, migrations, and debugging workflows.

title: Add database size diagnostics and table-level storage reporting
description: SQLite database size has grown to roughly 1.5 GB, and we need better observability before changing storage architecture. Add CLI/reporting that breaks down database growth by major table/category (for example raw_events, artifacts, memory_items, indexes) and shows enough retention-related signals to identify what is driving growth over time. Include actionable output that helps decide whether pruning, retention windows, or schema/index changes are the next step.
close_reason: Completed: codemem db size-report already exists in the TS CLI with test coverage

title: Define retention and pruning strategy for raw events and large artifacts
description: codemem currently retains ingest scaffolding aggressively enough that long-lived local databases can grow into multi-GB territory. Define and implement a pragmatic retention strategy focused on the largest growth drivers first, likely raw events and selected artifact classes, while preserving enough history for troubleshooting and replay. The first step should decide what data is product-value versus temporary ingest/debug scaffolding, then add safe pruning controls and documentation.

title: Add actor editor for peer-to-actor mapping
description: Follow-on to the lightweight same-identity peer claim work. Add a real actor management UI/model so peers can be assigned to explicit actor records, multiple legacy peers can collapse into one actor identity, and future per-actor sharing/trust policies have a proper foundation. MVP of current work only adds a boolean 'belongs to my identity' override; this issue tracks the fuller actor editor and assignment flow.
acceptance_criteria: Users can manage explicit actor identities instead of one-off peer claim flags, assign multiple peers to the same actor, merge legacy peer histories under a stable actor, and understand or adjust those mappings from product surfaces without touching the database directly.
notes: Backlog refinement 2026-03-11: promote this follow-on into an epic. Break it into contract, persistence, provenance/backfill, UI, and docs/test slices so only the next safe step is ready at a time. This epic extends the shipped identity-aware sync MVP without taking on heavyweight account infrastructure.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Define first-cut trust-state semantics for shared memory
description: Decide how lightweight trust should work in the first identity-aware sync version. Clarify whether trust_state affects retrieval only, sync visibility, or both, and keep the initial model deliberately simple.
acceptance_criteria: The first trust-state model is explicitly defined with clear default behavior, interaction with contributor-aware retrieval is documented, and any deferred auth/policy concerns are named rather than implicitly punted.
close_reason: Completed in merged identity-aware sync MVP stack

title: Evaluate whether sync interval can become live-updatable without daemon restart
description: Follow-up investigation for making `sync_interval_s` update without a daemon restart. Determine whether a lightweight control path or per-cycle live config read is preferable, and document the tradeoffs before implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Decision captured on whether `sync_interval_s` remains restart-bound or becomes live-updatable, with implementation notes if we choose the latter.
notes: Decision: keep sync_interval_s restart-bound for now. The settings flow now auto-applies sync lifecycle changes, including restart when sync_interval_s changes, which removes the manual UX pain without adding a live daemon control channel. A future follow-up can make the interval live-updatable if we want finer-grained runtime control, but it is not required to complete the current UX epic.
close_reason: Decision captured: keep sync_interval_s restart-bound with automatic sync restart on settings save

title: Sweep docs for stale install-first wording
description: Search codemem and codemem-site for remaining docs that imply OpenCode users must install the codemem CLI before the plugin/backend can work. Update any stale wording so the default path stays: install uv, add plugin, restart, and let uvx resolve the backend.
close_reason: Completed

title: Clarify OpenCode install docs around uvx backend auto-resolution
description: Update repo and codemem-site install docs so OpenCode setup emphasizes the shortest path: install uv, add the plugin, restart OpenCode. Document that the plugin auto-resolves the backend via uvx on first use, and move manual codemem CLI install to an optional step for direct commands and faster local usage.
close_reason: Completed

title: Expand Claude capture with transcript-derived assistant and usage signals
description: On Stop/SessionEnd, derive assistant message and usage-like signals from transcript_path when available to close capture fidelity gaps versus OpenCode.
close_reason: Closed

title: Align Claude lifecycle flush behavior with OpenCode boundaries
description: Map Claude hook boundaries to OpenCode-style session lifecycle semantics, documenting and implementing best-effort parity where event models differ.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add Claude vs OpenCode parity end-to-end test suite
description: Add adapter parity tests that run equivalent conversational/tool flows and assert comparable memory artifacts, extraction quality, and session outcomes.
close_reason: Closed

title: Document Claude parity architecture and fallback semantics
description: Update docs to describe CLI-first injection path, fallback behavior, lifecycle differences, and known parity limits after implementation.
close_reason: Closed

title: Normalize CLI/API wording to adapter-neutral session identifiers
description: Rename user-facing opencode_session_id wording to session_stream_id/session_id aliases across commands/docs while preserving compatibility shims for existing automation.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #158 and PR #160: adapter-neutral session aliases and wording across raw-event CLI/API/docs with compatibility for opencode_session_id.

title: Ship Claude plugin marketplace/distribution install path
description: Define and ship the install path users can use outside source checkouts (marketplace/plugin registry flow), including versioned release steps, install verification, and rollback guidance.
notes: Marketplace packaging implementation in progress via PR #136 (adds .claude-plugin/marketplace.json, marketplace install docs, and manifest/version consistency tests).
close_reason: Completed and merged via PR #136 marketplace/distribution implementation.

title: Harden adapter redaction beyond private-tag markers
description: Extend sanitization to key-based secret fields and malformed marker patterns so both HTTP and CLI queue paths enforce the same defensive redaction contract.
close_reason: Implemented and submitted in PR #157: secret-shaped field redaction + malformed <private> marker hardening across adapter enqueue paths.

title: Refactor OpenCode plugin behind adapter boundary
description: Refactor the existing OpenCode integration so shell-specific event capture maps into the shared adapter contract before core ingest. Preserve current behavior and reliability semantics.
acceptance_criteria: OpenCode path emits canonical adapter events; no regression in current plugin flows; adapter boundary is isolated and test-covered.
notes:
Brainstorm decisions (2026-03-02): OpenCode integration should be refactored behind an adapter mapper boundary into schema v1 without changing existing reliability semantics (flush/retry/backoff). This task remains blocked on codemem-afm.1 schema finalization.
Started interface scaffolding (2026-03-02): added OpenCode adapter-envelope mappers in .opencode/plugin/codemem.js and attached adapter metadata to captured events via _adapter, preserving legacy event shape for existing ingest behavior.
Completed first end-to-end boundary slice (2026-03-02): backend ingest now prefers adapter envelope data when _adapter is present for prompt extraction, assistant extraction/transcript projection, and tool event projection, while preserving legacy fallback behavior.
close_reason: OpenCode adapter boundary shipped with adapter envelope projection and parity tests.

title: Build Claude Code hooks/plugin adapter MVP
description: Implement the first non-OpenCode adapter for Claude Code using first-class Claude hooks and plugin packaging. Map official hook payloads into adapter schema v1 and keep ingestion hook-first.
acceptance_criteria: MVP command/package surface implemented with Claude plugin + hooks configuration; hook events normalized into schema v1; failure/retry behavior documented; transcript fallback explicitly deferred to a future enhancement unless critical gaps are discovered.
notes:
Brainstorm decisions (2026-03-02): Claude MVP is dual-path C (structured events primary + transcript fallback). Structured data is authoritative when both exist; transcript is gap-fill only. Activation is hybrid (explicit commands + optional auto-detect), default enables Claude with auto-detect on for known safe paths and clear opt-out. Project namespace stays unified with source tagging.
Rescope (2026-03-02): Claude integration is hook-first only. Do not implement transcript fallback in this task. Use official Claude hooks and plugin packaging docs as source-of-truth.
close_reason: Claude hook-first adapter MVP shipped with install/ingest commands, template packaging, queue path integration, and reliability fixes.

title: Define adapter event schema v1
description: Specify the canonical multi-agent event envelope and lifecycle semantics used by all shell adapters. Include required/optional fields, idempotency keys, sequencing rules, and redaction boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: Schema documented with examples; validation rules defined; fixtures created for OpenCode and Claude-style sessions; backward compatibility assumptions recorded.
notes:
Brainstorm decisions (2026-03-02): schema v1 scope is conversation/tool lifecycle only; required envelope fields include schema_version/source/session_id/event_id/event_type/ts/payload; ordering is hybrid (seq preferred, fallback allowed with ordering_confidence=low); dedupe for fallback sources uses deterministic event_id hash; redaction is soft redact with [REDACTED]; ambiguous transcript chunks map to assistant with parse_confidence=low.
Added draft schema artifacts: docs/plans/adapter-event-v1.schema.json and docs/plans/adapter-event-v1.fixtures.json. These capture v1 envelope fields, event-type payload constraints, ordering fallback rules, and cross-adapter examples (OpenCode + Claude structured/fallback).
Scope update (2026-03-02): schema examples now emphasize official Claude hook-derived events only. Transcript fallback examples and parse_confidence field were removed from draft v1 artifacts; fallback moved to codemem-afm.5.
close_reason: Schema v1 artifacts landed (schema + fixtures), ingest adapter envelope support shipped, and regression tests added.

title: Multi-agent shell adapter architecture (OpenCode, Codex, Claude, Windsurf, Cursor)
description: Define a shared adapter architecture so codemem can ingest and normalize session/tool events from multiple agent shells (OpenCode, Codex, Claude Code, Windsurf, Cursor). Treat shell-specific integrations as thin adapters over a common event contract and reliability pipeline. Prioritize Claude Code CLI as the first non-OpenCode implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Architecture note defines common adapter contract, lifecycle semantics, and failure handling; support matrix and rollout stages documented; explicit non-goals include generic bash/zsh/fish shell-hook wrappers; Claude Code CLI implementation broken into follow-up subtasks.
notes: AFM stack implemented for adapter schema v1, OpenCode adapter boundary, and Claude hook-first queue-backed MVP. Follow-up tasks tracked for support matrix publication and post-MVP hardening.
close_reason: Adapter architecture rollout completed: schema/boundary, Claude MVP+parity, redaction/observability hardening, identity decoupling slices, observer config decoupling, and published support matrix/rollout docs.

notes:

Fallback behavior: when prior pack baseline metadata is missing or malformed, delta is unavailable and all delta lists/defaults are stable empty/zero values.

title: task(search): define working-set hint input contract
description: Define optional working-set hint format for search and pack paths, including normalization and bounds.
acceptance_criteria: Input contract specifies accepted fields, limits, and default behavior when unset; invalid hints are ignored safely; no ranking change when hints absent.
notes: Implemented working-set hint contract across CLI/plugin/search. Uses repeated --working-set-file flags and normalizes filter payload to working_set_paths with safe defaults when absent. Landed in PR #126 (commit 30066d5, follow-up 8b89c9b).

title: task(search): implement bounded overlap scoring
description: Implement bounded score boost based on overlap between working-set hints and memory metadata/text.
acceptance_criteria: Boost is deterministic and capped; overlap logic is unit-tested; baseline ranking remains unchanged when no hints are passed.
notes: Implemented bounded deterministic overlap reranking in codemem/store/search.py, with overfetch before rerank and cap logic that preserves requested limit. Includes metadata coercion hardening. Landed in PR #126 (commits 30066d5, 8b89c9b).

title: task(plugin): pass normalized working-set hints to retrieval
description: Plumb recently modified files and related context from plugin into retrieval calls using the new hint contract.
acceptance_criteria: Plugin sends bounded hint payload, handles missing file activity gracefully, and preserves existing behavior when hints unavailable.
notes: Plugin now passes normalized working-set hints from modified files and captures apply_patch file updates; pack args use repeated --working-set-file flags. Landed in PR #126 (commit 30066d5).

title: task(test): add rank-shift and regression coverage for working-set boost
description: Add seeded tests validating expected ranking shifts with hints and no-op behavior without hints.
acceptance_criteria: Tests assert deterministic rank movement when hints overlap and unchanged output when hints omitted; hybrid eval regressions remain green.
notes: Added regression coverage for rank shifts/no-op behavior, absolute-vs-relative matching, non-object metadata handling, and large-limit working-set queries in tests/test_store.py plus plugin helper tests in .opencode/tests/plugin-working-set.test.js. Landed in PR #126 (commits 30066d5, 8b89c9b).

title: epic(search): bounded working-set ranking signal
description: Introduce optional working-set file/path hints into ranking so memories tied to currently edited files receive a bounded boost.
acceptance_criteria: Search APIs accept optional working-set hints without changing results when unset; scoring applies a bounded overlap boost against memory file metadata and related text/tags; ranking shifts are measurable and deterministic in seeded tests; plugin passes hints from session modified files.
notes: Completed working-set ranking signal epic end-to-end: contract, bounded reranking, plugin propagation, and regression coverage. Reviewer/Codex follow-ups addressed (delta availability gating and large-limit handling). Delivered via PR #126 with follow-up commits 30066d5 and 8b89c9b.

title: epic(pack): delta metadata for successive injections

acceptance_criteria: When prior pack state is available, pack metrics include added_ids removed_ids retained_ids and pack_token_delta; behavior is deterministic for unchanged packs and bounded when prior state is missing; plugin surfaces concise new/dropped counts without runtime failures.
notes: Likely files: codemem/store/packs.py and .opencode/plugin/codemem.js. Test plan: pack delta unit tests in new pack-focused test module plus plugin toast formatting coverage in .opencode tests.

notes: Implemented memory_expand MCP helper with ID expansion and timeline context behavior, including mixed valid/invalid ID handling and project-scoped behavior. Covered by tests (tests/test_mcp_expand.py) and merged in PR #123 (commit df108da).

title: Sync: skip offline peers when triggering manual sync
description: When clicking 'Sync now' or when the daemon runs its periodic cycle, it attempts to sync with all configured peers including those known to be offline, which generates error log entries. Should detect peer online/offline state (likely from last ping status) and skip offline peers, or at minimum not log errors for expected-offline peers. Currently there is no specific online/offline state detection — we just try all peers unconditionally.
close_reason: PR #101 - exponential backoff for offline peers in daemon tick (2min→30min max)

title: Raw event flush: handle expired/invalid OpenAI auth gracefully
description: When the observer's OpenAI token expires (401 AuthenticationError), the raw-event queue worker retries repeatedly, spamming the viewer foreground log with stack traces. The flush should detect auth errors as non-retryable, log a clear warning once, and back off instead of retrying every tick. Currently the error path raises RuntimeError('observer failed during raw-event flush') which is caught but immediately retried on the next tick cycle.
close_reason: PR #100 - observer detects auth errors and sweeper backs off 5min instead of retrying every 30s tick

title: Expand viewer color theme system with additional variants
notes: Implement semantic theme tokens and add new viewer variants while preserving existing light/dark defaults. Add accessible theme switcher options and persistence. Run CodeReviewer + gordon-ramsay review before PR.

Impacted payload fields confirmed: daemon_state and status.daemon_state in /api/sync/status.
