title: Migration window: verify session_import_key adoption across active sync peers
description: Track rollout for the new session_import_key replication field. Keep this open for the next few releases while mixed-version peers are expected. Close only after confirming active devices are on a version that sends session_import_key in memory_item payloads.
notes:
v0.13.0 is now released. Remaining exit criterion requires operational confirmation from active sync peers that inbound memory_item replication payloads include session_import_key. Keep this issue open until that field is observed across active devices for at least one full release window.
Grooming update 2026-03-08: current repo version is now beyond the original v0.13.0 rollout note, so this remains an operational verification task rather than active implementation work. Exit criteria are unchanged: confirm active sync peers send session_import_key in inbound memory_item payloads across a full current release window, then close this issue and unblock codemem-cfo.
Checked local replication data on 2026-03-12 against /Users/adam/.codemem/mem.sqlite. Recent inbound memory_item replication ops showed one observed peer device (a20d560e-8d8d-48b4-9d2d-04ef95c7baf5) with 50/50 recent payloads including session_import_key. This is positive rollout evidence but not yet sufficient to confirm all active peers across a full release window.

title: Remove legacy numeric replication session_id fallback after migration window
description: After the session_import_key replication migration has been deployed for several releases, remove legacy fallback behavior that maps inbound memory payloads via numeric session_id. Keep this blocked until compatibility window is complete and all active peers are expected to send session_import_key.

title: Sync diagnostics shows stale peers as online
description: Viewer sync diagnostics marks peers as Online based on stale last_sync/last_ping. When last successful contact is old and current sync attempts fail, top-level status still shows ok and peer chips remain Online, which is misleading. Add freshness/offline heuristic, recency thresholds, and status aggregation that reflects recent errors.

title: Add CI smoke test for npm plugin install and OpenCode load
Enforce npm/PyPI version match in release workflow
Keep @opencode-ai/plugin dependency aligned between root and .opencode package.json
Improve plugin/CLI upgrade seamlessness when plugin updates first
description: Improve resilience when the OpenCode plugin version is newer than the local codemem CLI. Scope: detect plugin/CLI version mismatch at runtime, surface actionable upgrade guidance with exact commands, and evaluate an optional safe auto-upgrade path for uv-based installs (explicit opt-in only).
acceptance_criteria: 1) Plugin reliably detects and reports CLI/plugin version mismatch with clear version details. 2) User sees actionable upgrade guidance tailored for common install modes, including uv tool installs. 3) Auto-upgrade path (if implemented) is opt-in, safe, and fails gracefully without blocking core plugin behavior. 4) Behavior is documented and covered by tests for mismatch and guidance paths.
notes: Risks: false mismatch detection, unsafe or surprising auto-upgrade behavior, environment-specific install edge cases, and degraded UX if guidance is noisy or unclear.

title: Enable plugin auto-start on launch and update migration docs
Add provider-loop integration tests across OpenAI and Anthropic adapters

title: Introduce hybrid retrieval scoring behind feature flag

Added regression test tests/test_sync_api.py::test_sync_ops_get_returns_internal_error_on_unexpected_exception by monkeypatching load_replication_ops_since to raise. Validation: uv run pytest tests/test_sync_api.py tests/test_sync_api_security.py (7 passed); uv run ruff check opencode_mem/sync_api.py tests/test_sync_api.py (passed).

description: Expand tests/test_sync_viewer_api.py with invalid, negative, and oversized limit cases for /api/sync/attempts. Confirm viewer_routes/sync.py returns stable validation errors.

title: [Audit] Make install_autostart_quiet report subprocess failures truthfully
description: Fix commands/sync_service_cmds.py so install_autostart_quiet returns accurate failure status when subprocess calls fail. Ensure logging and return values match actual execution outcomes.
notes: Fixed truthful failure reporting in install_autostart_quiet and quiet service actions. Added _run_subprocess_quiet helper in opencode_mem/commands/sync_service_cmds.py; install_autostart_quiet now returns False when launchctl/systemctl subprocess steps fail instead of always returning True. Added tests/test_sync_service_cmds.py covering linux success and subprocess-failure paths. Validation: uv run pytest tests/test_sync_service_cmds.py tests/test_sync_cli.py -k "sync_enable or install_autostart_quiet" (6 passed); uv run ruff check opencode_mem/commands/sync_service_cmds.py tests/test_sync_service_cmds.py (passed).

title: [Audit] Protect pidfile stop from killing unrelated processes
description: Harden process identity checks in sync_runtime.py before sending termination signals from pidfile-based stop logic. Avoid killing unrelated processes when PID reuse occurs.

title: [Audit] Ensure plugin_ingest closes store on exceptions
description: Ensure plugin_ingest.py closes MemoryStore resources on all exception paths to avoid locked DB handles and leaked connections. Verify cleanup behavior around error boundaries.
notes: Wrapped ingest pipeline in opencode_mem/plugin_ingest.py with try/finally to guarantee MemoryStore.close() on success, early return, and exceptions. Removed ad-hoc close-only-on-happy-path behavior. Added regression test tests/test_plugin_ingest.py::test_ingest_closes_store_on_observer_exception to assert store close executes when observer raises. Validation: uv run pytest tests/test_plugin_ingest.py (11 passed); uv run ruff check opencode_mem/plugin_ingest.py tests/test_plugin_ingest.py (passed).

title: [Audit] Harden ingest arg-shape validation in event_to_tool_event

notes: Hardened opencode_mem/ingest/events.py event_to_tool_event arg handling: args now accepted only when dict; non-dict args are coerced to {} so args.get('cwd') cannot crash. Added tests/test_ingest_events.py covering non-dict args safety and dict-cwd behavior. Validation: uv run pytest tests/test_ingest_events.py tests/test_ingest_tool_events.py (9 passed); uv run ruff check opencode_mem/ingest/events.py tests/test_ingest_events.py (passed).

title: [Audit] Ensure sync HTTP client always closes connection on failure
description: Guarantee response/connection cleanup in sync/http_client.py when request setup, read, or decode paths fail. Prevent leaked sockets across retry and error paths.
notes: Updated opencode_mem/sync/http_client.py so request_json always closes HTTP(S) connections via try/finally, including request/getresponse/read failure paths. Added tests/test_sync_http_client.py to assert close() is called when request() raises and when response.read() raises. Validation: uv run pytest tests/test_sync_http_client.py tests/test_sync_daemon.py -k "request_json or sync_once" (8 passed); uv run ruff check opencode_mem/sync/http_client.py tests/test_sync_http_client.py (passed).

title: [Audit] Validate /api/sync/attempts limit parsing

notes: Updated /api/sync/attempts limit parsing in opencode_mem/viewer_routes/sync.py: invalid/non-int and non-positive limits now return 400 {error: invalid_limit}; very large limits are clamped to 500. Added tests in tests/test_sync_viewer_api.py for invalid limit rejection and high-limit clamping. Validation: uv run pytest tests/test_sync_viewer_api.py -k "sync_attempts or sync_rejects_spoofed_loopback_origin or missing_origin" (5 passed); uv run ruff check opencode_mem/viewer_routes/sync.py tests/test_sync_viewer_api.py (passed).

title: Fix sync deadlock when project include filters skip first page
description: When project include/exclude filters are active, the sync API can return ops=[] for a page where every candidate op is filtered out. In that case the client cannot advance last_applied_cursor, so repeated sync passes can deadlock on the same window. Expected: server should return a next_cursor that advances past the scanned window (even if all ops were skipped), so clients can progress until they reach allowed ops.

title: Clarify pairing accept-filter semantics in CLI and GUI
description: Make directional behavior explicit: accept filters limit what is pushed from local machine to others.
notes: Clarified pairing filter directionality in CLI and GUI text: filters on --accept are outbound-only from the accepting device. Explicitly note incoming receiver-side project filtering is not yet enforced. Updated sync pair help text, pairing hint payload/UI strings, and tests (sync CLI + sync viewer API). Focused tests passed: uv run pytest tests/test_sync_cli.py -k sync_pair; uv run pytest tests/test_sync_viewer_api.py::test_sync_pairing_payload.

title: Improve CLI pairing output for concise copy/paste across shells

notes: Ensure generated commands are concise and shell-compatible (fish and zsh at minimum).

title: Include VPN interfaces (utun*) in pairing IP discovery
description: Pairing command detects LAN and Tailscale IPs, but misses work VPN interface IP (utun4), making coworker pairing harder.
notes: Implemented VPN-aware pairing IP discovery: added interface-based IPv4 candidates with allow/block interface prefix filters (includes utun/tun/tap/wg while excluding loopback/docker/awdl). Preserved auto ordering (LAN first, Tailscale fallback). Added tests in tests/test_net.py for interface filtering and host selection. Verified with: uv run pytest tests/test_net.py and uv run pytest tests/test_sync_cli.py -k pair (all passing).

title: Add workspace-level secret rule overrides
description: Support workspace-scoped additional rules so teams can declare org-specific patterns (internal hostnames, codenames, ticket-id prefixes) without forking codemem. Default rule packs miss exactly the patterns most likely to be embarrassing leaks for a given org.
acceptance_criteria: Workspace config can declare additional regex/secretlint rules. Workspace rules merged with default pack at scanner init. Matched workspace patterns redacted same as defaults. Config schema documented.
notes: Workspace-level secret_scanner block in codemem config now drives MemoryStore.scanner construction. Schema: { secret_scanner: { rules: [{kind, pattern, flags?, minEntropy?, redactGroup?}], allowlist: [string|/regex/flags] } }. loadScannerOptionsFromConfig in secret-scanner.ts compiles regex strings safely, drops malformed entries, and returns a ScannerOptions ready for . MemoryStore constructor reads codemem config once and uses it for both actor identity and scanner rules. Tests verify workspace rules fire, allowlist passes through, defaults still active for everything else, and malformed config doesn't block store open. Note: case-sensitivity caveat from tzrn (deriveTags lowercases) still applies to backfill-tags-derived rows; vb2s closes that for legacy data.
close_reason: Workspace-level secret_scanner config block (rules + allowlist) wired into MemoryStore constructor. 1550/1550 tests green.

title: Add scanner allowlist for known-safe matches
description: Allow specific literal strings or patterns to bypass redaction so the project's own test fixtures (example tokens, mock keys) do not trip the scanner and destabilize CI.
acceptance_criteria: Scanner config supports an allowlist of literal strings and regex patterns. Initial allowlist seeded with known test fixtures so CI passes with scanner enabled. Mechanism for adding allowlist entries documented.
notes: Implemented as part of the same secret_scanner config block as ben8. Allowlist entries: plain strings match by exact equality against the matched substring; strings shaped like /pattern/flags become regex and use RegExp.test() (partial unless anchored). Documented in JSDoc on ScannerOptions and exercised by both unit tests on loadScannerOptionsFromConfig and the workspace-rule store-level integration test. Initial test fixtures are seeded inline in tests today; if the project ever wants a dedicated 'known-safe fixtures' file at repo root or in workspace defaults, add a follow-up bd to seed it.
close_reason: Workspace-level secret_scanner config block (rules + allowlist) wired into MemoryStore constructor. 1550/1550 tests green.

title: Support viewing and restoring soft-deleted memories
description: Add a later-phase recovery workflow for soft-deleted memories in the viewer. This should let users surface inactive/deleted memories in a clearly differentiated way (for example via a show-deleted filter, separate view, or visual treatment), understand that they are soft-deleted, and optionally restore them. Keep this scoped as a follow-up to the initial viewer delete UI so the first delete flow can remain small and ship sooner.

title: Audit raw connect() callers for fresh-DB safety
description:
Gordon Ramsay review found several runtime call sites that use raw connect() from packages/core/src/db.ts and could theoretically hit a fresh / uninitialized DB. Most are user-initiated maintenance / migration / sync operations that would only run after the DB already exists, but we should audit each one to confirm.

## Call sites to audit

- packages/core/src/sync-retention-runner.ts:165
- packages/core/src/vector-migration.ts:297
- packages/core/src/dedup-key-backfill.ts:200
- packages/core/src/coordinator-actions.ts:378
- packages/core/src/export-import.ts:206 and :440
- packages/core/src/extraction-replay.ts:385
- packages/core/src/extraction-eval.ts:303
- packages/core/src/maintenance.ts:282 (withDb) and :1388 (initDatabase)

initDatabase already does bootstrap-if-empty. withDb does not, but it's used by maintenance ops that expect an initialized DB.

## Action

For each call site:
1. Identify what triggers it (CLI command, background daemon, etc.)
2. Decide whether fresh-DB invocation is possible
3. If yes, add bootstrap-or-fail handling (ideally by calling the shared connectAndBootstrap helper proposed in the directEnqueue follow-up)

Probably none of these need changes, but we should confirm rather than assume.
close_reason: Completed via merged PR #712 moving schema bootstrap into connect() and covering raw fresh-DB callers by default.

title: Stabilize maintenance job ordering in viewer APIs
description: Keep active maintenance jobs in a stable order for viewer stats/sync responses so health cards do not swap positions whenever job progress updates bump updated_at.
close_reason: Completed

title: Design cross-memory synthesis for pack output (Phase B)
description: Pack-time synthesis that detects cluster patterns in selected candidates and compresses recurring failure modes, operational rules, and open threads into concise operational summaries. Design after Phases D and A ship.

title: Design doctor-style context diagnostics follow-on
description: Define the broader doctor/debug surface after PackTrace ships, including recent traces, injection proof, token-budget stats, and adapter or cache health.

title: CLI cleanup: flatten sync peers remove, rename-device flag, --json edge cases
description:
Audit violations (cleanup tier): #2, #10, #17, #18, #21, #25, #28, #30, #44, #46, #48

Remaining low-severity items from the CLI compliance audit.

- #2: sync peers remove is 3-level nesting. Flatten to sync remove-peer <peer> or equivalent.
- #10: coordinator rename-device --name could become a 3rd positional. Acceptable as-is.
- #17: Short flag registry — document -k, -t, -b, -n assignments to prevent future collisions.
- #18: Add -n to recent --limit for consistency with search/pack.
- #21, #25, #28, #30: Optional --json on export-memories, sync disable, sync connect, memory inject. Low value individually.
- #44, #46: Host/port on sync start/stop/restart and coordinator serve. Moot or defensible.
- #48: version command duplicates --version. Keep for discoverability.

Address these as time allows, not blocking.

Parent epic: codemem-vcam

title: Extract coordinator feature modules from monolithic coordinator-actions and store contract
description:
## Problem

As of PR 595, \`packages/core/src/coordinator-store-contract.ts\` defines a single \`CoordinatorStore\` interface with 20+ methods covering:

- Group CRUD
- Device enrollment / disable / remove / rename
- Invites
- Join requests + review flow (with \`_no_transition\` idempotency marker)
- Reciprocal approvals
- Bootstrap grants (4 new methods added in PR 595)
- Presence + peer listing

And \`packages/core/src/coordinator-actions.ts\` is a parallel file containing top-level action functions for every method, each with the same remote-vs-local dispatch pattern.

Both files grow linearly with every coordinator feature. Bootstrap grant alone added:

- 4 methods to the store interface (create/get/list/revoke)
- 2 action functions (\`coordinatorListBootstrapGrantsAction\`, \`coordinatorRevokeBootstrapGrantAction\`)
- 3 admin API handlers in \`coordinator-api.ts\`
- 2 new types on the store contract (\`CoordinatorBootstrapGrant\`, \`CoordinatorCreateBootstrapGrantInput\`, \`CoordinatorReviewJoinRequestBootstrapGrantInput\`, \`CoordinatorBootstrapGrantVerification\`)

The coordinator domain has clear sub-bounded-contexts: **enrollment**, **invites**, **join-requests**, **reciprocal-approvals**, **bootstrap-grants**, **presence**. They share only the concept of a group and a device.

## Proposal

Refactor into feature-scoped modules:

```
packages/core/src/coordinator/
  store-contract.ts       # base: groups, devices, enrollment
  enrollment.ts           # enroll/disable/remove/rename + actions + types
  invites.ts              # create/import/list invites
  join-requests.ts        # review flow, _no_transition, bootstrap-grant hook
  reciprocal-approvals.ts # pending pair logic
  bootstrap-grants.ts     # PR 595 additions live here
  presence.ts             # upsert, list peers
  index.ts                # re-exports
```

The monolithic \`CoordinatorStore\` interface can either be preserved as a type intersection of the per-module interfaces, or broken into multiple narrower interfaces that concrete stores implement.

Scope is large enough that this should not block other work; probably lands as a multi-PR refactor after the CLI audit epic (codemem-vcam) finishes so we're not refactoring a moving target.

## Acceptance criteria

- Coordinator code split into feature-scoped files.
- \`CoordinatorStore\` contract still compiles and both existing implementations (better-sqlite, D1) pass the shared test harness unchanged.
- No behavior change; pure mechanical refactor.
- Bootstrap-grant isolation demonstrates that adding a future coordinator feature touches ~1 file rather than ~5.

## Non-goals

- Do not change store semantics or add new features during this refactor.
- Do not rename public methods; preserve wire/API compatibility.

## Discovered from

PR 595 (ajk/bootstrap-grant-automation) — post-merge code review noted coordinator modules growing linearly.

title: Harden E2E harness for future CI adoption
description: Prepare the local E2E harness for future CI use with machine-readable output, deterministic naming/ports, cleaner artifact bundles, and reduced dependence on local-only assumptions. This is follow-on work, not a v1 blocker.
acceptance_criteria: The harness has a documented follow-on path to CI with deterministic behavior and cleaner artifact/output contracts, without blocking local-first usefulness.
close_reason: Added CI smoke job, machine-readable E2E runner output, and configurable artifact root for future CI adoption

title: Add visibility constants to replace magic strings
description: Visibility strings like shared, private, personal, shared:default are scattered across 6+ files with no enum or constants. Add typed constants.

title: Move ensureSyncResetStateTable DDL out of hot path
description: ensureSyncResetStateTable runs CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS on every getSyncResetState call. Move to schema init/migration path.

title: Validate sync dialog accessibility and review readiness
description: Validate keyboard/focus behavior, descriptive copy, async disabled states, and viewer build/typecheck results for the new sync dialog flows. Resolve follow-up polish issues before review.
close_reason: Superseded by the sync refinement accessibility pass (codemem-je25.5) and the interaction feedback stack — dialog accessibility and review readiness were addressed in those merged PRs.

title: Restore sync peer management CLI commands
description: The TypeScript CLI is missing useful peer-management commands that existed in the Python flow, such as removing stale peers and other direct peer maintenance operations. Restore a usable sync peer management surface in the TS CLI so operators do not need raw SQLite or the viewer UI for basic cleanup and repair tasks. Align help, tests, and docs with the shipped command surface.

title: Add UI control to delete sync peers
description: The viewer backend exposes DELETE /api/sync/peers/:peer_device_id, but the UI does not appear to offer any way to remove a stale or broken sync peer. Add a visible peer deletion/removal action in the relevant sync UI, including confirmation UX, API wiring, and tests/docs as needed.

title: Clarify coordinator groups vs manual peer pairing
description: Coordinator-backed discovery currently enrolls devices into groups and exposes discovered peers, but sync still depends on separate manually managed sync_peers entries. This creates a confusing split between team/group membership and actual replication relationships. Define and implement a clearer UX/model so users understand whether joining a coordinator team should imply discoverability only, suggested pairing, or active sync readiness.

title: Auto-peer devices discovered in coordinator groups
description: Add useful coordinator-driven peer management so devices that are enrolled in the same coordinator group can become usable sync peers without manual pairing friction. Explore safe defaults such as auto-creating/updating sync_peers entries from fresh coordinator discovery, optional approval gates, stale peer reconciliation, and preserving trust/auth guarantees. Include CLI/UI/docs/test updates.

title: Add missing coordinator admin CLI commands
description: The TypeScript coordinator docs and store support group and enrollment administration, but the CLI currently lacks core admin/list commands such as group-create, list-groups, list-devices, enroll-device, rename-device, disable-device, and remove-device. Add the missing commands, align help/tests/docs, and eliminate the current sqlite3-only workaround for basic coordinator administration.

title: Simplify create-invite group argument
description: The CLI help for 'codemem sync coordinator create-invite' currently shows both a positional [group] argument and a redundant --group <group> option. Review whether both are necessary, simplify the interface if possible, and update tests/help/docs to match.

title: Triage github issue 108
description: Check whether the Windows termios issue from legacy Python issue #108 is still relevant to the supported TS CLI path, then report recommended triage.
close_reason: Legacy Python-only Windows issue; current TS packages do not import pty/tty/termios. Keep GitHub #108 as unsupported-platform triage unless Windows support is explicitly targeted.

title: Refactor sync status route before more state/diagnostic concerns accrete
description: is accumulating too many responsibilities in one handler: runtime daemon state, sync attempt interpretation, peer summaries, coordinator state, join requests, and diagnostics shaping. It is acceptable today, but future changes risk turning  into a god-route that is harder to reason about and easier to break. After phase-1 sync work lands, split the route into smaller helpers/modules with clearer boundaries.

title: Implement working-set-file ranking support in TS pack builder
description: TS CLI accepts --working-set-file for pack but currently warns it is not used (packages/cli/src/commands/pack.ts). Implement working-set aware ranking/parity in TS buildMemoryPack path to match expected behavior and remove compatibility warning once functional.
close_reason: Completed

title: Populate retry_depth_max in raw event metrics
description: packages/core/src/maintenance.ts currently sets retry_depth_max to 0 with TODO because attempt_count-backed metric is missing. Implement the proper query/aggregation from raw_event_flush_batches (or add required schema support) so reliability metrics report actual retry depth.
close_reason: Completed

title: Port include_pack_context support in explain responses
description: packages/core/src/search.ts currently hardcodes pack_context: null with TODO: port include_pack_context. The MCP surface exposes include_pack_context but TS backend does not populate it, causing feature mismatch with API expectations. Implement pack_context generation path and tests for include_pack_context=true.
close_reason: Completed

title: Decide and document memory compact/inject parity for TS CLI
description: packages/cli/src/commands/memory.ts notes compact and inject are deferred. Confirm whether 0.20 should ship without these commands, and either implement parity or explicitly document intentional omission/alternatives in CLI help and docs.
close_reason: Completed

title: Align vitest on vite 8 when upstream support lands
description: vitest 3.2.4 peer-depends on vite ^5|^6|^7, so pnpm installs both vite 7 and vite 8. This causes type conflicts in vite plugins (e.g. the executable-output plugin in cli/vite.config.ts). Track upstream vitest vite-8 support and upgrade when available.
close_reason: Upgraded vitest to 4.0.17 which supports vite 8

title: Import resurrects soft-deleted memories (active hardcoded to 1)
description: export-import.ts insertMemory sets active: 1 unconditionally, ignoring the source record's active state. Combined with exportMemories(includeInactive: true), deleted memories are silently un-deleted on import. Fix: read active from source row, default to 1 only when missing.
close_reason: Completed in merged PR #404. Import now preserves source active/deleted_at state instead of forcing active=1/deleted_at=null, with regression coverage in export-import.test.ts.

comments.author: Adam Kunicki
comments.text: Implemented fix in PR #404: import now preserves memory active/deleted_at state instead of forcing active=1/deleted_at=null. Added regression assertion in export-import.test.ts.

title: Migrate remaining raw SQL queries in store.ts to Drizzle
description: ~10 raw SQL queries remain in store.ts that could benefit from Drizzle typed queries: recent/recentByKinds (dynamic filter clauses), rawEventsSinceBySeq, claimRawEventFlushBatch, batch ingestion queries. These work correctly but bypass Drizzle type safety — column renames would fail at runtime instead of compile time. Lower priority since they're read paths or use dynamic filter builders.

title: Drizzle: migrate coordinator-store.ts (25 queries, separate DB)
description:
Replace raw SQL in coordinator-store.ts (22) and coordinator-api.ts (3) with Drizzle typed queries.

The coordinator uses its OWN database (separate from main codemem DB) with its own schema. This can be migrated independently.

Tables: groups, enrollments, invites, join_requests, presence, request_nonces.

Lower priority since coordinator is not yet deployed. Can be done after core migration.

Depends on: schema definition bead (for pattern, but uses its own schema file)

title: Remove default=str replacer from event ID JSON.stringify
description: claude-hooks.ts uses a JSON.stringify replacer that coerces undefined to 'None' to match Python's json.dumps(default=str). Once Python is deprecated, this compatibility shim can be removed — undefined values should just be omitted (standard JSON behavior).

title: Remove Python-reference comments from TS codebase
description: ~60 comments across the TS codebase reference Python behavior as justification ('matches Python', 'ports Python', 'mirrors Python'). These are migration history, not code documentation. Remove the Python references while keeping any useful functional descriptions.

title: Remove v1 signature acceptance from verifySignature
description: sync-auth.ts verifySignature() accepts both v1 (SSHSIG-era) and v2 (native Ed25519) signatures for migration compatibility. Once no Python peers exist, drop v1 acceptance and only accept v2.

title: Remove sortKeys from event ID hash — use plain JSON.stringify
description: claude-hooks.ts recursively sorts object keys before hashing to match Python's json.dumps(sort_keys=True). Once Python is deprecated, JSON.stringify with default key order is sufficient — no cross-runtime ID stability needed.

title: Remove normalizeIsoTs microsecond padding — use native toISOString()
description: normalizeIsoTs() in claude-hooks.ts pads .123Z to .123000Z to produce identical event IDs as Python during the crossover period. Once Python is deprecated, use JS-native toISOString() output (.123Z) and stop the padding. Event IDs will change for new events but that's fine — no cross-runtime dedup needed.

title: Epic: Remove Python migration scaffolding after deprecation
description:
Parent epic for all post-deprecation cleanup work. Track individual cleanup tasks as subtasks discovered-from this epic.

This epic should be actioned 1-2 versions after Python is fully deprecated and no users are running the Python runtime.

Scope:
- Remove cross-runtime parity code that only exists for Python interop
- Remove Python-reference comments throughout the TS codebase
- Simplify code paths that were constrained by Python compatibility
- Clean up any remaining dead code or unused compatibility shims

title: Post-deprecation cleanup: remove Python-reference comments and migration scaffolding
description:
Once Python is fully deprecated, clean up TS codebase:

1. Remove ~60 comments referencing Python behavior ('matches Python', 'ports Python', 'mirrors Python')
2. Remove migration scaffolding:
   - normalizeIsoTs microsecond padding (use native toISOString)
   - sortKeys in event ID hash (use plain JSON.stringify)
   - v1 signature acceptance in verifySignature (only accept v2)
3. Remove any remaining cross-runtime parity hacks that no longer serve a purpose

Do NOT remove comments that describe what the code does — only remove references to the Python codebase as justification.
close_reason: Superseded by epic codemem-ui24 and its subtasks (codemem-eojy, codemem-ob6g, codemem-i3zn, codemem-hv5p, codemem-3v6w)

title: Document hybrid retrieval as optional follow-up after semantic parity
description:
Follow-up to codemem-ll5f. Hybrid retrieval (alternate reranking, shadow logging, judged-query eval workflow) was the part being shadow tested. That is distinct from semantic/vector retrieval, which is part of the current Python default pack/injection experience.

This bead tracks the non-blocking work only:
- hybrid reranking strategy
- shadow logging and judged-query eval tooling
- decision on whether hybrid meaningfully improves over semantic+FTS baseline

Dependency: do not prioritize before semantic/vector parity is shipped in TS.
close_reason: Decision made: hybrid retrieval reranking is not required and will not be ported

title: Port store tag helpers to TS (normalize_tag, file_tags, derive_tags)
description:
Python's store/tags.py (79 LOC) has tag normalization and derivation:
- normalize_tag(): lowercase, strip punctuation, handle stopwords
- file_tags(): extract tags from file paths (language, framework hints)
- derive_tags(): auto-derive tags from memory title/body/metadata

Used by backfill_tags in maintenance (codemem-l3b7) and by the ingest pipeline for auto-tagging new memories.

Small module but don't want to lose track of it — needed when maintenance commands are ported.

Python source: codemem/store/tags.py (79 LOC)

title: Port import-from-claude-mem one-time migration tool to TS
description:
Python has a one-time migration utility (~378 LOC in commands/import_from_claude_mem.py) for importing memories from the old claude.mem format (predecessor to codemem).

This is a low-frequency tool but needed for users migrating from claude.mem. Lower priority than core functionality.

Package: packages/cli/src/commands/import-from-claude-mem.ts (new file)
Python source: codemem/commands/import_from_claude_mem.py

title: Port coordinator admin CLI to TS (invite, enroll, approve, list)
description:
Python has coordinator admin commands (~476+271=747 LOC in commands/sync_coordinator_cmds.py + sync_service_cmds.py) for managing a self-hosted coordinator.

Missing commands:
- codemem coordinator serve — start the coordinator HTTP server
- codemem coordinator invite create — generate an invite payload/link
- codemem coordinator invite list — list pending/active invites
- codemem coordinator devices list — list enrolled devices
- codemem coordinator join-requests list/approve/deny — manage approval workflow

These are lower priority since the coordinator is an advanced feature, but needed before full Python deprecation.

Package: packages/cli/src/commands/coordinator.ts (new file)
Python source: codemem/commands/sync_coordinator_cmds.py, codemem/commands/sync_service_cmds.py

title: Extract address normalization to shared module
description: normalizeAddress is duplicated between coordinator-store.ts and sync-discovery.ts. Both now use URL constructor but are separate implementations. Extract to shared address-utils.ts.
close_reason: Fixed in PRs #269+#271: shared address-utils.ts

title: Optimize chunkOpsBySize to avoid O(n²) JSON serialization
description: chunkOpsBySize re-serializes the growing batch for every op candidate. Track running byte estimate instead. For 200 ops this re-serializes progressively larger JSON ~200 times.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #268: O(n) running estimate

title: Add jitter to sync backoff
description: peerBackoffSeconds uses deterministic exponential backoff with no jitter. Multiple devices syncing with the same offline peer retry at identical intervals (thundering herd). Add jitter: baseBackoff * (0.5 + Math.random() * 0.5).
close_reason: Fixed in PR #270: jitter added

title: Handle orphan sessions from interrupted ingest pipeline
description: If the process is killed between session creation and endSession(), sessions accumulate with ended_at=NULL. Need cleanup in db prune-memories or a startup sweep.
close_reason: Added to PR #265: cleanOrphanSessions() + 2 tests

title: Add startSession/endSession to MemoryStore
description: MCP server memory_remember creates sessions via direct SQL (mcp-server/src/index.ts). This bypasses store abstractions. Add startSession() and endSession() methods to MemoryStore matching Python's API, then update MCP server to use them.

title: Add missing test coverage for store port edge cases
description:
Missing tests identified in review:
1. rowMatchesFilters unit tests (each filter field independently, esp. workspace_id)
2. expandQuery with NEAR/PHRASE operators (added to set but untested)
3. search() with multiple filters combined
4. timeline with depthBefore=0 or depthAfter=0
5. pack with tokenBudget=0
6. remember() with empty title/body
7. dedupeOrderedIds with boolean/float inputs
8. search('AND OR NOT') — empty after operator filtering
close_reason: Merged in PR #255

title: Split search.ts into focused modules (search, timeline, explain, scoring)
description:
search.ts is 635+ lines with 6 concerns crammed together. Split into:
- search.ts: FTS search, expandQuery (~200 lines)
- timeline.ts: timeline, timelineAround (~120 lines)  
- explain.ts: explain, explainItem, dedupeOrderedIds, rowMatchesFilters (~200 lines)
- scoring.ts: recencyScore, kindBonus, rerankResults (~60 lines)
StoreHandle interface should live in its own types file or in scoring.ts.
This is maintainability — the current file will hit 1000+ lines when semantic search, working_set, and search_index are ported.

title: Migrate remaining viewer tabs (Feed, Health, Settings) to Preact
description: After Sync tab migration proves the pattern, convert the remaining tabs. Feed is the largest (memory list rendering, filters, search). Health is medium (stat grids, health cards). Settings is a dialog with form groups. Each tab becomes a Preact component tree. Acceptance: all tabs converted, el() helper fully retired, no vanilla DOM wiring remains in tab modules.
close_reason: Migrated the remaining viewer tabs to Preact in vertical slices: feed shell/interactions, health overview/stats/session summary, and settings form internals/dirty-save-tooltip flows. Preserved existing classes, IDs, orchestration, and data flows, and validated with scoped UI typecheck/build.

title: Preact + Radix scaffolding and Vite config
description: Set up the Preact + Radix foundation before module-by-module migration. Steps: 1) Add preact, preact/compat, @preact/signals to viewer_ui package.json. 2) Configure Vite with @preact/preset-vite for JSX/TSX support. 3) Add preact/compat aliases in vite.config so react imports resolve to preact. 4) Convert one small component (e.g. the notice toast) as a proof-of-concept to validate the build pipeline works. 5) Verify bundle builds, type-checks, and existing tests pass. This is the gating prerequisite for both jhp and 5oj.
close_reason: Completed Preact + Radix scaffolding in packages/ui, enabled Vite/TSX compat, and migrated the peer scope editor collapsible to a Preact + Radix proof component with passing scoped typecheck/build.

title: Add multi-group coordinator discovery membership
description: Coordinator-backed discovery currently supports a single sync_coordinator_group per codemem instance. Add true multi-group membership so a device can register presence in more than one coordinator group and discover peers across multiple explicit cohorts (for example personal, team, lab, or temporary project groups). This should be implemented as a real semantics change, not just a config shape tweak.
acceptance_criteria: codemem supports joining multiple coordinator groups with a clear config model, registers presence per group, merges peer discovery results safely across groups, documents how legacy single-group config migrates forward, and avoids confusing coordinator discovery groups with memory-sharing policy.
notes: Implemented true multi-group coordinator discovery membership with backward-compatible config parsing. Added sync_coordinator_groups config/env support, automatic migration from legacy sync_coordinator_group, per-group presence registration, merged peer lookup across groups, config/discovery tests, and docs explaining the migration story.
close_reason: Completed multi-group coordinator discovery membership with backward-compatible config model

title: Define relay and buffered delivery follow-on after coordinator MVP
description: Capture the concrete follow-on plan for relay/proxy transport and offline buffering once coordinator-backed discovery ships. Keep this separate from the coordinator MVP, but define the extension points and success criteria so the first coordinator implementation does not block later transport work.
acceptance_criteria: There is a concrete follow-on issue or note defining relay/proxy and buffered delivery scope, how it builds on the coordinator MVP, and what metrics or failure cases would justify taking it on next.
notes: Captured the relay/proxy and buffered delivery follow-on as a separate design note after the coordinator MVP, with inheritance constraints, trigger criteria, and prototype boundaries.
close_reason: Completed with relay and buffered delivery follow-on note

title: Document self-hosted coordinator setup and discovery behavior
description: Document how coordinator-backed discovery works, when it is needed, how it coexists with mDNS and cached peer addresses, and how to self-host the reference deployment. Include operator-facing caveats about auth, freshness, and current non-goals like relay/buffer support.
acceptance_criteria: Docs explain the coordinator-backed discovery model, operator setup, config knobs, fallback behavior, and explicit current limitations well enough for self-hosters to evaluate and try the feature.
notes: Documented coordinator-backed discovery in docs/coordinator-discovery.md, updated README and docs/user-guide.md with setup/config/behavior guidance, and corrected viewer wording around the autosaving visibility control.
close_reason: Completed with self-hosted coordinator setup and discovery documentation

title: Implement trust review workflow for lower-confidence shared memories
description: We shipped trust-aware filters and soft ranking, but the vision also calls for a lightweight review workflow so lower-confidence shared memories are visible and can be promoted from legacy or unreviewed states intentionally. Add the first practical trust review surface and state transition flow in the viewer without turning trust_state into an auth or moderation system.
acceptance_criteria: Users can identify lower-confidence shared memories in product surfaces, review a memory and mark it trusted through an explicit action, and the resulting workflow is documented and covered by tests without blocking ordinary retrieval by default.
notes: Clarification 2026-03-12: this issue remains about provenance-review workflow (identifying lower-confidence shared memories and explicitly marking them trusted). Do not overload trust_state with actor-affinity or own-memories-first ranking semantics; those belong in a separate retrieval-weighting track.

title: Prototype optional relay/coordinator transport for shared sync
description: The vision still includes an optional relay/coordinator mode for identity-aware shared sync, but only the design exists today. Build a first prototype that preserves local-first replication semantics while proving the coordinator-assisted transport path, device enrollment model, and coexistence with direct peer-to-peer sync.
acceptance_criteria: There is a concrete relay/coordinator prototype or implementation slice that forwards replication traffic without becoming the source of truth, uses explicit device-based enrollment, coexists with direct sync, and includes tests/docs that define the supported boundaries of the prototype.

title: Define trust-aware review and retrieval behavior for shared memories
description: trust_state is currently descriptive only. Define whether and how review state, provenance confidence, or source weighting should influence retrieval or user review workflows without turning trust_state into an authorization system.
acceptance_criteria: A design-backed issue note or linked plan defines the minimum useful trust-aware behavior, distinguishes review/ranking effects from auth policy, and names the states, transitions, and product surfaces involved in the first follow-on slice.
notes: Wrote follow-on trust-aware review/retrieval design in docs/plans/2026-03-12-trust-aware-review-and-retrieval-follow-on.md covering minimum useful behavior, state transitions, optional filters/ranking, and non-goals that keep trust_state out of auth policy.
close_reason: Completed with trust-aware review and retrieval design doc

title: Define optional relay/coordinator mode for identity-aware shared sync
description: The long-term vision keeps peer-to-peer sync as the default but allows an optional relay or coordinator for more reliable multi-network shared memory. Define the architecture, trust assumptions, pairing/auth story, and coexistence rules with pure peer-to-peer sync before implementation work starts.
acceptance_criteria: A design-backed issue note or linked plan defines relay responsibilities, auth and key story, failure modes, migration from pure P2P, and the boundaries that preserve codemem's local-first behavior when relay mode is not enabled.
notes: Wrote optional relay/coordinator design in docs/plans/2026-03-12-optional-relay-coordinator-mode.md covering relay responsibilities, device-based auth, coexistence with pure P2P sync, and local-first boundaries.
close_reason: Completed with optional relay/coordinator mode design doc

title: Define adaptive widening from personal to shared retrieval
description: Personal-first retrieval is shipped, but the long-term vision also wants a careful fallback path into shared context when personal results are weak. Define the trigger conditions, ranking changes, guardrails, and evaluation plan for widening without flooding results with noisy teammate context.
acceptance_criteria: A design-backed issue note or linked plan defines when widening should trigger, how shared results should be blended or labeled, what telemetry/eval is needed before rollout, and which failure modes must stay out of scope for the first implementation.
notes: Wrote adaptive widening design in docs/plans/2026-03-12-adaptive-widening-personal-to-shared-retrieval.md covering trigger conditions, shared-pass constraints, blending/labeling rules, telemetry, and explicit non-goals.
close_reason: Completed with adaptive widening design doc

title: Add observability for skipped Claude hook payloads
description: Add low-noise metrics/logging for unsupported or malformed hook payloads so silent no-op ingestion paths are diagnosable without disrupting Claude sessions.
close_reason: Implemented: added low-noise skip observability logs in Claude enqueue paths and tests; shipped in PR #156 update.

title: Evaluate transcript fallback for Claude adapter
description: Assess whether transcript fallback is needed after Claude hooks/plugin MVP ships. Only pursue if hook coverage gaps materially impact memory quality or ingestion reliability.
acceptance_criteria: Gap analysis documented against shipped hook-first MVP; recommendation recorded (keep deferred or implement); if implementation is recommended, create scoped follow-up tasks with risk controls and dedupe strategy.
notes:
Created as explicit future enhancement. Implement only after hook-first Claude MVP ships and coverage gaps are proven.
Transcript fallback remains intentionally deferred beyond MVP; keep open as post-MVP evaluation task.
close_reason: Completed: transcript fallback was evaluated and implemented for Claude Stop hooks (assistant text + usage extraction) in PR #156.

title: Publish support matrix and rollout plan
description: Document adapter support tiers, rollout sequencing, and non-goals for unsupported integrations.
acceptance_criteria: Docs include supported/partial/experimental matrix, rollout phases, and explicit non-goals including generic shell-hook wrappers.
notes:
Brainstorm decisions (2026-03-02): publish support tiers (supported/partial/experimental) with rollout gates schema-stable -> OpenCode parity -> Claude MVP -> additional adapters. Keep explicit non-goal: no generic bash/zsh/fish shell-hook wrapper strategy.
Support matrix/rollout draft exists in architecture plan; next step is publishing finalized user-facing support matrix across docs.
Validation findings: Claude UUID 4d297237-0bcd-4f1c-9f4e-c8b934c6fab8 appeared in raw event payloads but was ingested under stream ses_353c83fcaffeTaIKCEoSir0BhX. Queue fully drained, confirming transport works, but source/session identity is still OpenCode-shaped and confusing in mixed-adapter operation. Follow-up tracked in codemem-afm.9/.10/.11.

title: spike(integrations): design first-class bash/zsh/fish integration path
description: Run a discovery spike for non-OpenCode shell integration, defining install model, hooks, safety constraints, and phased implementation plan before shipping code.
acceptance_criteria: Deliver design doc with shell-specific hook approach for bash zsh fish, failure-mode and startup-latency analysis, idempotent install/uninstall contract, and minimum viable rollout plan; include recommendation whether to proceed to implementation and what to defer.
notes:
Expected outputs: docs/rfcs or docs/design note plus concrete follow-up beads if approved. Include test strategy for snippet generation and temp-dotfile smoke tests.
Decision 2026-03-02: deferred/cancelled as out-of-scope for current product direction. codemem integration target remains OpenCode event ingestion; no shell wrapping/hooks planned.

title: Health tab: make sync-stale recommendation actionable with button
description: When the health tab shows 'Sync is stale. Run one immediate sync pass.' with a copy command, replace or augment with an actionable button that triggers sync directly (like the existing Sync Now button on the sync tab).
close_reason: PR #102 (stacked on #99) - actionable Sync Now buttons in health tab recommendations

title: Feed tab: infinite scroll with API pagination
description: Add cursor/offset pagination to /api/observations and /api/summaries endpoints, then implement infinite scroll in the feed tab to load more items as the user scrolls down. Currently the feed shows ~70 fixed items (20 obs + 50 summaries).
close_reason: Added offset pagination to /api/observations and /api/summaries with pagination metadata, updated feed API client and tab logic for infinite scroll auto-loading, added backend pagination tests, and rebuilt viewer bundle.

title: Harden viewer rendering and dialog accessibility
notes: Address final viewer polish for release: unify System health card styling with section cards, drop subtle light-warm duplicate theme, and add a new dark purple/lavender variant inspired by TuneMyMusic while keeping accessibility and professionalism.
close_reason: Completed viewer polish and accessibility hardening: added dark lavender theme variant, preserved single light theme path, and improved settings dialog accessibility (described dialog semantics, keyboard focus trap, and focus restoration).

title: Harden project label normalization for Windows-style paths
description: Improve _normalize_project_label in plugin ingest to handle Windows-style paths deterministically on non-Windows runtimes (e.g., PureWindowsPath when backslashes or drive-letter prefixes are detected). Add focused unit tests for mixed-platform path strings and document expected normalization behavior.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic Windows-style project label normalization in plugin ingest, added focused mixed-platform unit tests, and documented normalization behavior in plugin reference.

title: Add ops metrics panel and align docs with runtime hook behavior
description: Add an operations metrics panel and align public docs with observed runtime hook behavior.
design:
Incremental plan only (no implementation yet).

Scope:
- Build an explicit Ops Metrics presentation in the existing viewer Stats area using current APIs only (no new backend route initially): /api/stats, /api/usage, /api/raw-events, /api/sync/status.
- Keep documentation aligned to actual runtime hook behavior and failure semantics.

PR stack plan:
1) j2j-a (UI/observability):
   - Refine Stats panel into explicit ops-oriented cards.
   - Add clear units on labels/tooltips (tokens, %, counts, time).
   - Add timestamp context (last refresh + sync-derived timestamps where available).
   - Ensure project-filter behavior is explicit for filtered vs global values.
   - Preserve mobile/desktop readability and existing visual language.
2) j2j-b (docs alignment):
   - Finalize README.md, docs/architecture.md, docs/plugin-reference.md, docs/user-guide.md updates.
   - Match docs wording with final UI card labels and API payload semantics exactly.

Acceptance checks:
- Reviewer can map each card to one reproducible field from API/CLI outputs.
- No new server endpoint required in initial pass.
- Existing sync diagnostics/status behavior remains intact.
- Tests/lint pass for touched areas.

Out of scope for this pass:
- New /api/ops aggregation endpoint.
- Large dashboard redesign beyond metrics clarity.
notes: Split work into PRs: #75 (viewer UX/sync health state clarity + tests) and #76 (runtime hook + sync reliability docs wording).
close_reason: Scope completed via merged PRs #75 and #76: ops metrics surfaced in viewer health/stats UX and public docs aligned with runtime hook behavior and sync reliability semantics.

title: Support JSONC config files
description: Allow codemem config parsing from JSONC (comments/trailing commas) while keeping strict JSON compatibility.
notes: Scope as follow-up: parser update, tests for comments/trailing commas, docs update for config filename and behavior.

title: [Audit] Improve per-address sync failure diagnostics
description: Improve error attribution in sync/sync_pass.py so failures are reported per remote address with actionable context. Use sync/http_client.py signal details to aid triage.

title: [Audit] Add tests for autostart failure reporting and pidfile safety
description: Add tests covering subprocess failure truthfulness in commands/sync_service_cmds.py and PID reuse safety in sync_runtime.py. Validate guardrails around autostart and stop behavior.

title: Support multiple teams/coordinators
description: Currently the sync model assumes a single team and coordinator. In the future, support enrolling in multiple teams with separate coordinators for different collaboration contexts.

title: Add recursion depth limit to pushOps
description: pushOps recursively splits on 413 with no depth limit. A pathological peer could cause log2(N) levels of recursion, each creating auth headers (expensive ssh-keygen calls). Add maxDepth parameter.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #270: MAX_PUSH_SPLIT_DEPTH=8

title: Document Windows limitations for sync system
description: Sync depends on ssh-keygen, macOS security CLI, Linux secret-tool — none available on Windows by default. Document as known limitation rather than runtime surprise.

title: Add JSONC block comment support to observer config parser
description: stripJsonComments only handles // line comments. JSONC also supports /* block comments */. Low priority since most configs use line comments.
close_reason: Added to PR #263: block comment support + 3 tests

title: Add console.warn to fromJson on parse failure
description: db.ts fromJson() silently returns {} on JSON parse failure. Corrupt metadata_json values produce empty metadata with zero trail. Add console.warn in the catch block for debugging. Matches Python behavior (also silent) but a warning helps when investigating 'my metadata disappeared' reports.
close_reason: Merged in PR #253

title: Add guided bootstrap tooling for Cloudflare coordinator example
description: The Cloudflare coordinator reference deployment currently requires too much manual setup: create D1, apply schema, insert groups and enrolled_devices rows by hand, then run smoke checks. Add user-friendly bootstrap tooling that walks operators through setup step by step, generates or applies enrollment data for local devices, and makes it easy to get from zero to a working coordinator without hand-writing SQL.
acceptance_criteria: There is a documented script or CLI flow that guides operators through Cloudflare coordinator bootstrap end to end: create group metadata, generate local device enrollment payloads, apply or print D1 enrollment SQL/commands, and verify the deployment with a smoke check. The flow should be easy enough for self-hosters to follow without reading source code or hand-editing SQL.
notes:
Added bootstrap script design note covering script-first placement in examples/, Typer + rich + questionary dependency choice, interactive vs non-interactive modes, first-slice scope, and output shape.
Implemented script-first bootstrap tooling in examples/cloudflare-coordinator/bootstrap.py using Typer + rich only. The helper now detects local device identity, generates enrollment SQL and config snippets, supports interactive and non-interactive JSON output, prints wrangler setup commands, and can optionally run the smoke check. Added README usage docs and a subprocess-backed test covering non-interactive JSON mode.
2026-03-14 strategy shift: Cloudflare bootstrap helper is now secondary/reference-only. Do not prioritize further Worker-specific onboarding polish ahead of the canonical Python coordinator deployment path.
close_reason: Completed with guided Cloudflare bootstrap script and test coverage

title: Ship Cloudflare Worker reference deployment for coordinator
description: Provide a Cloudflare Worker reference deployment that implements the coordinator HTTP contract for presence registration and peer lookup using the agreed device-key auth model. Keep the contract runtime-agnostic, but make the Worker the canonical low-cost example.
acceptance_criteria: The repo includes a documented Cloudflare Worker reference implementation or scaffold for the coordinator contract, with clear setup instructions, supported persistence assumptions, and tests or smoke checks for the public endpoints.
notes:
Design direction update 2026-03-12: Cloudflare Worker remains a useful low-cost alternate/reference deployment, but a built-in codemem coordinator service is now the preferred primary self-hosted implementation path because it avoids multi-artifact management and can reuse Python auth verification directly.
Added a Cloudflare Worker reference coordinator scaffold under examples/cloudflare-coordinator/: Worker endpoint implementation for /v1/presence and /v1/peers, D1 schema, Wrangler example config, README bootstrap/deploy instructions, and a Python smoke-check script that signs requests with codemem device keys. Validated Worker JS syntax with node --check and smoke_check.py syntax with py_compile.
2026-03-14 strategy shift: Cloudflare Worker is no longer the primary deployment target. Keep this as a secondary/reference deployment path only; do not block core coordinator UX or feature rollout on Worker parity in the near term.
close_reason: Completed with Cloudflare Worker reference deployment scaffold and smoke-check path
